races.race_name: Hallgarten & Novum Wines Maghull Novices' Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Be Aware
runners.spotlight: Smart form but has won only twice (runner-up seven times); rallied when beaten 1l by No Questions Asked in Grade 2 novice at Windsor (2m, soft; handles good) in January; raced freely and faded in Grand Annual Handicap at Cheltenham last time; the hood is back on today, a tongue-strap is fitted and he may well bounce back to form; if so, he has each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Too free at Cheltenham Festival but consistent prior to that; each-way possibilities

runners.name: Kala Conti
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler and she made a smooth transition to chasing towards the end of 2025, winning two mares' races at Cork (2m5f/2m1f) and beating subsequent Arkle winner Kargese by 16l in the second of them; could not maintain her progress in Grade 1 novice at Sandown in January but nor was she disgraced; top on RPRs with her mares' allowance and should go well.
runners.comment: Beat subsequent Arkle winner Kargese at Cork; top on RPRs with her mares' allowance

runners.name: No Questions Asked
runners.spotlight: Useful over hurdles and even better over fences; found a bit more improvement when coming from behind to beat Be Aware 1l in three-runner Grade 2 novice at Windsor (2m, soft; handles good) in January; well beaten next time in 2m4f handicap at the Cheltenham Festival, lack of stamina probably a factor; this drop back in trip looks a plus and he wears cheekpieces for the first time (has won when hooded); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Beat Be Aware at Windsor in January and had a possible excuse at Cheltenham; not ruled out

runners.name: Salvator Mundi
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed his finest hour over hurdles when winning the Grade 1 Top Novices on this course (2m, good) 12 months ago; beaten at 1-7 on chasing debut in November and again underwhelmed at Leopardstown over Christmas, but a bloodless win at Thurles (2m2f, soft) in February was much more like it; open to further improvement over fences, comes here fresh after missing Cheltenham and has strong claims.
runners.comment: Grade 1 hurdle winner here last spring; back on track over fences last time; major player

runners.name: King Of Kingsfield
runners.spotlight: Won three of first five races over fences, including a Grade 3 novice at Punchestown (2m2f, good to yielding) in October, but has made mistakes and run poorly at Cheltenham and Newbury since; perhaps he'll be revived by a break and cheekpieces, but he has plenty to find on the figures.
runners.comment: Won three of first five chases but disappointing twice since; lots to find on the figures

runners.name: Mighty Bandit
runners.spotlight: Flat and hurdles winner but already a far superior chaser; jumped well in front (although sometimes to the right) when completing a handicap hat-trick at Doncaster (2m, soft) six weeks ago by 17l; firmly on the up and deserves his chance in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Doncaster win completed his hat-trick in handicap chases; earns his chance in this Grade 1

runners.name: Alnilam
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who made it 2-2 over fences when winning at Cheltenham (2m, good to soft) in October; however, his limitations seem to have been exposed since and he finished well adrift of No Questions Asked and Be Aware at Windsor last time; has had wind surgery but still hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Well adrift of No Questions Asked and Be Aware at Windsor; hard to recommend
runners.owner: Mrs A Williams And A Badri

runners.name: Mirabad
runners.spotlight: Made all in beginners' chase at Exeter (2m1f, good to soft) in December; has run creditably in two 2m Grade 2 events since but he was beaten at 1-4 at Bangor on his latest outing; bottom of the pile on ratings and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Beaten at 1-4 last time; bottom of the pile on ratings and difficult to fancy

races.race_name: William Hill Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Absolutely Doyen
runners.spotlight: Successful on his first five starts for Paul Nicholls; winning run ended when eighth of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps but he did stay on again after getting outpaced; likely to be competitive.
runners.comment: Won first five starts for this yard; fair eighth of 24 to Supremely West at Cheltenham

runners.name: Jeriko Du Reponet
runners.spotlight: Hasn't looked a natural in his five chases (beaten favourite in Kim Muir last time) but he did very well over hurdles in cheekpieces last spring, finishing second to stablemate Doddiethegreat in the Pertemps at Cheltenham before getting the better of him at Punchestown (2m7f, yielding); could stage a revival back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked a natural over fences but could revive back hurdling (did well last spring)

runners.name: Kaka's Cousin
runners.spotlight: Maiden/novice winner who couldn't justify favouritism but still made a positive start to his handicap career when fourth of 17 in the EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, heavy; Get On George second) five weeks ago, faring best of those who raced close to the pace; up in trip; remains open to improvement.
runners.comment: Positive start to handicap career when fourth in EBF Final at Sandown; likely more to come

runners.name: Kikijo
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who has made a big splash on his switch back to hurdling this season, winning soft-ground handicaps over about 3m at Cheltenham and Sandown; the form of the Sandown win is particularly strong but he couldn't reproduce it back on good ground in the Pertemps at Cheltenham and conditions may once again not be as soft as he prefers.
runners.comment: Progressive on soft this winter but well beaten on quicker ground at Cheltenham since

runners.name: Get On George
runners.spotlight: Pair of novice wins over an extended 2m3f (soft/good); flopped in a 3m Grade 2 novice at Doncaster in January but quickly back on the up with an excellent staying-on second of 17 in the EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, soft; Kaka's Cousin fourth) five weeks ago; well worth another attempt at 3m and has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Largely progressive, including second in EBF Final at Sandown latest; enters calculations

runners.name: Minella Rescue
runners.spotlight: Second in the C&D Grade 1 novice at this meeting last year; went on to finish first and second in handicaps over the extended 2m7f at Uttoxeter at the start of the summer but well below par both starts since a break, latterly at the Cheltenham Festival; combination of first-time cheekpieces and a return to this C&D needs to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Has struggled in both outings since a break but was second in C&D Grade 1 a year ago

runners.name: Hold The Serve
runners.spotlight: Flopped on hurdle debut but hasn't looked back since wind surgery, landing a pair of novices before bringing up the hat-trick on 2m5f Kempton handicap debut (good to soft) seven weeks ago; won that going away in a style which suggests he's capable of better again and is likely to be suited by today's longer trip (3m Irish point winner); major player.
runners.comment: Progressive; won going away on 2m5f handicap debut, so 3m should suit; major player
runners.owner: McNeill Family & The Late Mavis Warren

runners.name: Fortune Timmy
runners.spotlight: Useful novice hurdler, including Fontwell wins over 2m3f and an extended 2m1f (both good to soft); also third in a Grade 2 novice at Cheltenham in January before performing with credit in the Grade 1 Turners at the Festival there (2m5f), staying on again when hampered at the last; that suggests this step up in trip could suit now he's handicapping; interesting.
runners.comment: Staying on when hampered in 2m5f Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham; player on handicap debut

runners.name: Ikarak
runners.spotlight: Regular winner, including at Lingfield (2m7f, good to soft) and Ayr (3m, soft) this season; very good third of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month but Sean Bowen still deserts him in favour of Hold The Serve.
runners.comment: In good form, including third in Pertemps latest, but Sean Bowen still jumps off him
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Mrs D J Brown

runners.name: Good To Be Alive
runners.spotlight: Notched a first win for current yard when asserting late on at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) in January and backed that up when second of eight at Kelso (2m5f, good) six weeks ago; Sean Bowen prefers Hold The Serve but this 5yo brings unexposed potential now he's stepping up in trip under Jack Kennedy.
runners.comment: Sean Bowen looks elsewhere but this 5yo has made steady progress; up in trip

runners.name: Quantum Quest
runners.spotlight: Readily made all in Kilbeggan maiden when upped to 3m1f in August and followed up on handicap debut at Roscommon (3m, good to yielding) in September; even better form in defeat later in the autumn, including fifth of 17 in a notably strong 3m Cheltenham handicap on good; returns from a four-month break as a 6yo who could easily have more to offer.
runners.comment: Steady progress since upped to 3m; may do better again back from a break.

runners.name: Ace Of Spades
runners.spotlight: Successful over 2m4f on reappearance here (good to soft) in October and has shown better form at around 3m since, including a win at Huntingdon (good to soft) in January; only midfield behind reopposing stablemate Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham, though.
runners.comment: Two wins this term, including here, but only midfield behind Supremely West at Cheltenham

runners.name: Supremely West
runners.spotlight: Provided yet another example of his yard's prowess in top handicaps when quite comfortably justifying strong market support in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month, with seven of today's rivals in behind; raised 9lb but could be capable of even better.
runners.comment: Landed gamble in the Pertemps at Cheltenham; raised 9lb but probably even better to come

runners.name: Lihyan
runners.spotlight: Ridden last ten starts by William Maggs; seventh hurdle win when seeing off five rivals at Newbury (3m, good to soft) three weeks ago but even a 2lb nudge seems likely to stop him in this much more competitive scenario.
runners.comment: Won in small field at Newbury latest but this looks much harder

runners.name: Wade Out
runners.spotlight: Switched to chasing with two wins in the autumn, the latter in a Cheltenham Listed race (3m1f, soft) despite not jumping that well; his fencing once again lacked fluency when sixth in the National Hunt Chase back at Cheltenham (3m6f, good) in first-time cheekpieces (blinkered now) last month; has a lower mark back hurdling, so no surprise were he to go well despite the fact Sean Bowen looks elsewhere.
runners.comment: Listed novice chase win despite not impressing with his jumping; lower mark back hurdling

runners.name: Push The Button
runners.spotlight: Made a good start over fences for new connections, winning at Carlisle (2m, good) in October and second in a Grade 2 novice at Ascot (2m3f, soft); mistakes crept in when below that level twice since and he now reverts to hurdling; sixth of 17 in this last year off an identical mark.
runners.comment: Good start over fences but mistakes crept in; now back hurdling (sixth in this last year)
runners.owner: The 8 Amigos, Readers & Wiggy, N Skinner

runners.name: Idem
runners.spotlight: Another in this field who took in the Pertemps at Cheltenham, finishing a good fifth of 24 to Supremely West; should be competitive again but nothing in his record suggests he has enough in hand to win a race as hot as this.
runners.comment: Another who ran well in the Pertemps but looks summed up by his mark

runners.name: Eagle Fang
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner; made a bright start to chasing career when winning a 2m6f beginners' chase at Thurles in November but hasn't built on that in his two runs since and is not obviously well treated now back hurdling in a first-time visor; probably stays 3m.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated back hurdling, so first-time visor needs to make a difference

runners.name: Duke Silver
runners.spotlight: Winner of handicaps at around 3m at Listowel (yielding) in September and Leopardstown (18 runners; good to yielding) in December; bounced back from a lesser effort at the Dublin Racing Festival when sixth of 24 to Supremely West at Cheltenham (3m, good); same mark today.
runners.comment: Won two good handicaps in Ireland this term and sixth to Supremely West in Pertemps latest

runners.name: Bold Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Back with Nicky Henderson after last season with Laura Morgan; had marks in the 140s at his peak and has shown he still retains a good chunk of that ability in his two outings after a year off, latterly finishing seventh of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good).
runners.comment: Has shown he still retains a good portion of his ability back with Nicky Henderson

runners.name: Harbour Lake
runners.spotlight: Won a good prize at Haydock (3m, good) last May; pulled up on return to action there in November but better when fourth of seven to Ma Shantou at Cheltenham (3m, good) on New Year's Day; being eased another 2lb can only help this C&D scorer but he requires a big career best as a 10yo.
runners.comment: C&D winner who also scored at Haydock last May; fair fourth at Cheltenham latest

runners.name: Chart Topper
runners.spotlight: Third in a Listed handicap over 2m6f at Galway (good) last summer; didn't enjoy much luck on next two starts (still in the lead when falling two out at Haydock in February) but he was well held in a big-field 2m5f handicap at Cheltenham last month; the cheekpieces worn on that occasion are discarded; still not fully exposed, particularly at 3m.
runners.comment: Well held at Cheltenham but promising before that and still not fully exposed
runners.owner: Aisling Byrne & Paul Raleigh & Matthew Cunningham

runners.name: Bossman Jack
runners.spotlight: Three wins from first four starts and he was keeping on well and disputing third when making a bad mistake at the final flight in the Turners at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month, ultimately finishing sixth; leading claims on the back of that very promising performance.
runners.comment: Keeping on well in share of third when making bad mistake at final flight at the Festival
runners.owner: M W Lake & Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Ballyfad
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and beaten just over 7l when tenth of 21 in the Turners at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month; however, he was kicked at the start that day and may well be capable of better, having finished ahead of the Turners winner King Rasko Grey when short-headed in Leopardstown Grade 1 in February; respected for top Irish trainer who has won the last four runnings.
runners.comment: May well be capable of better than he showed at Cheltenham; stable has won last 4 runnings

runners.name: Soldier Reeves
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 2m5f when storming home for fourth at 150-1 in the Turners at Cheltenham (good to soft) last month; he doesn't have the Cheltenham hill this time and his stablemate Bossman Jack would probably have beaten him at the Festival had he jumped the final flight proficiently, but this 5yo is unexposed at this sort of distance and could be a contender.
runners.comment: Flew home for 4th at the Festival; doesn't have the Cheltenham hill but still a contender

runners.name: Scorpio Rising
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-timer (soft/good to soft) and made it three handicap wins in a row when cruising into the lead in the 2m4f EBF Final at Sandown last month; highly progressive and could play a leading role up in grade.
runners.comment: Cruised into the lead when winning the EBF Final at Sandown; could play a leading role

runners.name: Charisma Cat
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner who built upon previous promise over hurdles when sent up in trip for her handicap debut and winning that 15-runner mares' novice handicap at Newbury in good style; has the 7lb mares' allowance but she still needs to find something extra and can make the odd mistake.
runners.comment: Won big-field mares' handicap at Newbury in good style; needs something extra today

runners.name: Lord Byron
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for 80-1 fifth in the Triumph Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) and this step up in trip could prompt further improvement; however, two starts ago this 4yo was soundly beaten by Montemares and Kosac d'Oudairies at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) and he needs to back up his latest promise.
runners.comment: Kept on well for Triumph fifth and this trip could suit; needs to back up that promise

runners.name: Rooster Crowing
runners.spotlight: 5yo who is trained in France and has won four of his 17 hurdle starts there; better than ever when close third of six in Grade 2 at Auteuil (2m3f, very soft) three weeks ago behind two previous Grade 1 winners and he's not ruled out; has won on good to soft ground on the Flat.
runners.comment: His recent Grade 2 third at Auteuil reads well; this French raider is not ruled out

runners.name: Kosac d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Point, bumper and dual novice hurdle winner who was second in a Grade 2 at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) in February; however, he was just over 6l behind Montemares at Kelso, so has work to do today.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Grade 2 at Kelso in February but work to do with Montemares on that form

runners.name: Montemares
runners.spotlight: Lost his unbeaten record when fourth of six, beaten about 8l, in the Grade 1 Challow at Newbury (2m4f, good) in December; returned to winning ways in fine style in the Grade 2 Premier Novices' Hurdle at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) in February; steps back up in trip and in grade this afternoon but he's one to consider after that impressive latest performance.
runners.comment: Impressive in 2m2f Grade 2 at Kelso in February and he's one to consider

runners.name: Onlyforfrankie
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten sixth of 12 in Grade 2 at Kempton (2m, good to soft) in February and hard to fancy on the back of that; however, he easily won a maiden at Taunton (2m, good) in January and followed up in a warm small-field novice at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) and his pedigree suggests this step up in trip will suit; might not be a forlorn hope each-way.
runners.comment: Beaten 22l in Grade 2 at Kempton but should be better than that; not a forlorn hope e-w

runners.name: Masked Man
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over hurdles, with those novice wins (2m/2m3f, good/good to soft) sandwiching a creditable Grade 2 second at Haydock (1m7f, good to soft) in November; this bright prospect is open to further improvement but others have more substance to their form; first run since December.
runners.comment: Sole defeat over hurdles came when second in Grade 2; others have more substance to form

runners.name: Zanndabad
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner handicap at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) last October and followed up in a warm Newcastle novice (2m6f, good to soft), again with plenty in hand; he's useful on the Flat and could have more to offer in this code now things have started to click, but such improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat and things have clicked over hurdles, but further improvement is needed

races.race_name: William Hill Handicap Chase (Registered As The Freebooter Handicap Chase) (Premier Handicap) (GBB)

runners.name: Mr Hope Street
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner, including in one of only two attempts at 3m; campaigned over 2m4f/2m5f switched to fences this term, best effort when third behind very notable rivals in graduation chase at Haydock; well handicapped on that bare form and could be open to any amount of improvement back up in distance; a typically interesting runner of his type for the Skeltons.
runners.comment: Well treated on best chase effort and looks open to improvement back up in distance

runners.name: Josh The Boss
runners.spotlight: Novice chaser who has several pieces of sound form at up to 2m4f this season, including a second at Aintree; however, below that level in major handicaps in two of his last three starts, seeming a non-stayer at Doncaster (soft) in the sole 3m attempt; stamina to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Seemed non-stayer in a 3m attempt; stamina to prove back up from 2m4f

runners.name: Konfusion
runners.spotlight: Faller in the Cheltenham Festival contest won by Grand National runner Johnnywho; progressive otherwise and his record of 5-11 over fences features wins in notable handicaps at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) and Wetherby (3m, soft) this term; likely to go well barring mishaps.
runners.comment: Faller last time; progressive otherwise and has won two notable handicaps this term

runners.name: Deep Cave
runners.spotlight: 3m hat-trick in 2025 started with a success over hurdles on this card (good) and ended with nabbing Leave Of Absence on the line in notable handicap chase at Ascot (good to soft); couple of backward steps since but a bad early error, and perhaps losing a shoe, contributed to a poor effort last time; may well rebound on a significant day in his calendar.
runners.comment: Possibly best to forgive latest effort; won over hurdles on this card 12 months ago

runners.name: Glengouly
runners.spotlight: Has form at 3m1f; made all in major handicap at Cheltenham (2m4f) in December; unable to dominate in the same manner and eventually pulled up in all three outings since; had wind surgery (his second) since latest; wears new headgear; again far from certain to have his own way.
runners.comment: Made all at Cheltenham in December; unable to dominate in the same way since

runners.name: Bad
runners.spotlight: Useful handicap chaser at up to 2m5f, albeit quirky and having saved his winning in this sphere for Kempton; admittedly right up to best in Ascot contest on penultimate start; last two efforts suggest this new trip is worth exploring but he comes with some risk, especially in such a competitive field.
runners.comment: Looks worth a crack at this new trip but quirky and comes with risk

runners.name: Uncle Bert
runners.spotlight: 1-6 over fences, the success at Aintree (2m4f) on seasonal debut; seemed a non-stayer in the Welsh National before eventually falling, then ran below form at Cheltenham (2m4f); return to this venue may have a positive effect but he ideally needs assistance from the weather, having gained all wins on soft ground.
runners.comment: Ideally needs assistance from the weather, having gained all wins on soft ground

runners.name: Brave Fortune
runners.spotlight: Irish 7yo whose broadly progressive form switched to chasing this season features a comfortable win at Roscommon (sole attempt over 3m1f; good) in the summer and good second in Thurles handicap (2m5f, yielding) last time; still reasonably unexposed and looks interesting back up in distance.
runners.comment: Irish 7yo who is still reasonably unexposed and looks interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Chance Another One
runners.spotlight: Irish 7yo who was successful last August/November in first two chase attempts, including at Kempton (3m, good); something to prove in this scenario, having posted duck eggs in competitive handicaps on last two starts, but in excellent hands and may yet resume improvement; not written off.
runners.comment: Posted duck eggs on last two starts but is in excellent hands and may yet resume progress

runners.name: Leave Of Absence
runners.spotlight: Good third to Lookaway in the bumper on this card four years ago; spoiled his chance with a couple of mistakes in the Cheltenham Festival handicap won by Grand National contender Johnnywho; however, he split that rival and Deep Cave for useful effort at Ascot (3m, good to soft) in December and may resume his progress when jumping errors are avoided.
runners.comment: Made mistakes last time but may resume his progress when jumping errors are avoided

runners.name: Lookaway
runners.spotlight: Landed the bumper on this card in 2022; justified market support in major handicap at Kempton (good to soft; first attempt at 3m under rules) most recently, taking chase record to 3-5 and overall strike-rate to 9-20; remains particularly unexposed over this sort of distance and commands respect returned to Aintree, with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: Won the bumper on this card in 2022 and major handicap chase at Kempton last time

runners.name: In Love
runners.spotlight: French jumper who bagged Grade 3 honours and finished a good fourth in the Grand Steeple-Chase de Paris in 2024; however, has failed to match that form since and, judged on recent RPRs, has something to prove off his mark in this very warm handicap; first British attempt.
runners.comment: French jumper; ran well in top race in 2024; not in the same form since
runners.owner: Andrew Peake, Simon Macauley & Bryant Family

runners.name: Cruz Control
runners.spotlight: Has failed to show his form in both outings this term but a revival looks highly likely back at Aintree on return from four-month break; 2-2 over fences at this venue, namely wins in this race for the last two years (good/good to soft); had a similar prep when winning by 5l off just 1lb lower 12 months ago; commands respect in the hat-trick attempt.
runners.comment: 2-2 over fences at Aintree, namely wins in this race for the last two years; respected

runners.name: Ain't That A Shame
runners.spotlight: Shaped as if in need of the run, en route to taking a late fall, at Kelso six weeks ago on debut for new stable and first start since finishing sixth in the 2024 Grand National; landed a major Irish handicap that year but needs to prove he retains peak ability.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he retains ability in this second run back from long layoff

runners.name: Impose Toi
runners.spotlight: Relished step up in trip when winning C&D handicap (good to soft) in November and won the Grade 2 Long Distance Hurdle at Newbury (3m, soft) and Grade 1 Long Walk at Ascot (3m, good to soft) on his next two starts, with Strong Leader in second on both occasions; below par at Cheltenham on last two runs (good record there previously) and it was disconcerting that he was pulled up in the Stayers', but he's in the picture if back to his best.
runners.comment: Pulled up in Stayers' Hurdle but in the picture on his winning form in the Long Walk

runners.name: Honesty Policy
runners.spotlight: Won the Grade 1 Mersey Novices' Hurdle here 12 months ago (2m4f, good); just two runs in current campaign and they've been positive performances in Grade 1s, finishing third in the Long Walk at Ascot (3m, good to soft) and fifth in the Stayers' Hurdle at Cheltenham (3m, good); this lightly raced 6yo could have more to offer back here in a first-time tongue-tie; chance.
runners.comment: Grade 1 novice win here 12 months ago; remains lightly raced and could have more to offer

runners.name: Strong Leader
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Cheltenham in January but that's not his track and he's had a wind op since; prior to that he ran well when runner-up to Impose Toi at Newbury and Ascot, and his Aintree form figures read 1212 with his last two visits being in this race (good to soft/good); there can be considerable optimism that he'll be a key player back here; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Wind surgery since last time and he has an excellent record at Aintree; key player

runners.name: Air Of Entitlement
runners.spotlight: Won the mares' novice hurdle at last season's Cheltenham Festival (2m1f, good to soft) and returned to form to win the Martin Pipe Handicap there (2m4f, soft) last month; she has something to find now back up in grade, even with the 7lb mares' allowance, but she powered up the hill last time and has an Irish National winner in her pedigree, so the step up in trip might prompt further improvement; not discounted.
runners.comment: Powered up the hill to win the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; this new trip might suit

runners.name: Take No Chances
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of her game in the Mares' Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) last month but this sort of trip may be her optimum; neck second to Strong Leader at Wetherby (3m, good) in November and creditable third behind Impose Toi and Strong Leader in another Grade 2 at Newbury (3m, soft) later that month; ran well here 12 months ago; each-way claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Good form in defeat in 3m Grade 2s in November; each-way claims if back on song

runners.name: Hiddenvalley Lake
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Punchestown last May and at Navan in February on sole start since; however, this race has brought out the best in him in the last two seasons, as he finished third in 2024 (good to soft) and first last year (good); could bounce back and play another leading role.
runners.comment: Return to form is needed but he was third and first in the last two runnings of this race

runners.name: Home By The Lee
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was running in the Stayers' Hurdle for the fifth time when making the breakthrough in that Cheltenham Grade 1 last month (3m, good); pulled up in this race on both visits to Aintree and 33-1 when winning at the Festival, but it was certainly no fluke judged on the pick of his career exploits and he's the one to beat if repeating that latest form.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner of Stayers' Hurdle; unproven here but the one to beat on that Cheltenham form

runners.name: Jingko Blue
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in Grade 2 Relkeel Hurdle at Cheltenham in January (2m4f, good; no match for Kabral Du Mathan) when his jumping lacked fluency, then convincing winner of the BetMGM Cup (Handicap) at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month; he should be fine now back up in trip, having won a 3m Grade 2 novice chase last season, and this unexposed 7yo could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Convincing winner of the BetMGM Cup and this unexposed 7yo could have a part to play

runners.name: Lavida Adiva
runners.spotlight: Course winner who keeps on improving and was a fine second of 24 in the Pertemps Final (Handicap) at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month; receives 7lb mares' allowance but today's assignment requires a clear career best.
runners.comment: Runner-up in the Pertemps Final but today's assignment requires a clear career best

runners.name: Doddiethegreat
runners.spotlight: Won the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham (3m, good to soft) last March and some respectable runs in Grade 2s this term; wore blinkers when pulled up in the Stayers' Hurdle at Cheltenham last month and is back in his usual cheekpieces this afternoon, but he still has something to find.
runners.comment: Some respectable runs in Grade 2s this term but pulled up in the Stayers' Hurdle last time

runners.name: Hewick
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning the King George over fences at Kempton in December 2023; won two conditions hurdles last year; gave it a very good go from the front when seventh in the Stayers' Hurdle at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month following wind surgery but this 11yo is probably vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Gave it a very good go from the front when 7th in Stayers' Hurdle; again looks vulnerable

races.race_name: Randox Grand National Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jagwar
runners.spotlight: Improved his rating in every one of his eight chases, winning four and that includes the 2m4f Plate at last year's Cheltenham Festival; first venture much beyond 2m5f had to wait until his return to Cheltenham last month, in which he came from out the back to get within half a length of the winner Johnnywho; that showed he stays 3m1f and suggested he's still capable of better, but his jumping contained unnerving moments; wears cheekpieces first time and a half-brother probably stays 4m.
runners.comment: Constant improver; went close when upped to 3m1f at Cheltenham after some jumping errors

runners.name: Panic Attack
runners.spotlight: 10yo who is better than ever this term, including with a big handicap double in November in the Paddy Power Gold Cup at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) and Coral Gold Cup at Newbury (3m2f, good to soft); dropping back to 2m4f, however, was probably not ideal when creditable third in mares' race at Cheltenham last month; 8lb higher than in the Newbury race but her dominance that day is hard to forget.
runners.comment: 8lb higher than for her big-race win at Newbury (3m2f) but her dominance is hard to forget

runners.name: Johnnywho
runners.spotlight: Fine over these fences in November; laboured from before three out when a well-beaten fifth to Haiti Couleurs in Irish National last April; that was in the mud, though, and his top efforts have been on good to soft; they include his neck second in the 3m2f Kim Muir at Cheltenham last March and, equipped with first-time cheekpieces after wind surgery, a 3m1f win from Jagwar at this year's Festival; able to race here off the same mark as last time (due to go up 6lb) and he wasn't stopping in either of those big races at Cheltenham; seriously considered.
runners.comment: 2nd and 1st over 3m2f/3m1f at Cheltenham Festivals and wasn't stopping; up 6lb in future

runners.name: Stellar Story
runners.spotlight: Second to Lecky Watson in the 3m Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham last March; while comfortably dismissed in the closing stages of his three starts this winter, the first two of those were Grade 1 events and giving 8lb to Grangeclare West and Gerri Colombe at Fairyhouse (blinkers replaced cheekpieces) on latest outing makes that result look a whole lot better; above all, perhaps, while unraced beyond 3m2f, he has strongly suggested on plenty of occasions that he has serious stamina reserves; each-way appeal.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 3m2f but has strongly suggested that he has serious stamina; e-w appeal

runners.name: Jordans
runners.spotlight: Twice second in Grade 1 novice events last season, most notably when upped substantially in trip to 3m1f at this meeting; possibilities judged on that performance (unraced over further) and although he's been nowhere near that form since, his closest finish this term came when a staying-on fifth of 18 over 2m7f as a hurdler; just a 7yo but not totally dismissed.
runners.comment: Second in the 3m1f Grade 1 novice chase at this meeting last year; below form since

runners.name: Firefox
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied on last two starts; Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown (3m, soft) in February was also the first time he went beyond 2m5f, when 33-1 and spending most of the race out the back but running on into fourth of 12, beaten 14l; some encouragement might be derived from that but he didn't see it out so well when confronted by 3m2f in the Gold Cup at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Ran on late when fourth in Irish Gold Cup (3m); didn't see out 3m2f so well at Cheltenham

runners.name: Favori De Champdou
runners.spotlight: Experienced 11yo who's having a tremendous season; 66-1 winner of a top 3m handicap at Leopardstown (made most) over Christmas and took his form to another new level on the 3m5f Cheltenham cross-country course on last two outings, winning easily on first occasion and well clear of the third when he chased home Final Orders at last month's Festival; well handicapped and with the trip likely to suit, he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: 11yo who's having a tremendous season, over Cheltenham cross-country on last two outings

runners.name: Final Orders
runners.spotlight: 10yo who's had 70 races (only one of today's rivals has managed even half that) and won 11; latest was the 3m5f cross-country at Cheltenham Festival, comfortably holding off Favori De Champdou for career-best form off the same mark as today; having taken so well to that discipline (he also won there in December) and produced his best performances away from soft ground, he has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Has taken so well to Cheltenham cross-country; best performances away from soft ground

runners.name: Perceval Legallois
runners.spotlight: Won major 3m handicaps at Leopardstown over fences (by 7l, going away) and hurdles before starting joint-third favourite for last year's National, but he fell on the first circuit; it's too early to forget his best performance (Christmas 2024) but with nothing to shout about in his two races this winter, this time he could be an outsider.
runners.comment: Joint-third fav for this in 2025 but fell at the ninth; nothing to shout about this winter

runners.name: Three Card Brag
runners.spotlight: Career-best form in the autumn this season, including a win at Cheltenham (3m1f) and second to Panic Attack in the Coral Gold Cup at Newbury (3m2f); flopped when favourite in the Grade 3 won by Grangeclare West at Fairyhouse in February, admittedly after 12 weeks off but there's the additional worry of last year's Grand National in which he was 16-1 and raced very prominently but faded steadily from three out.
runners.comment: Career-best form in the autumn; last year's Grand National and latest start are concerns

runners.name: Oscars Brother
runners.spotlight: A first-season novice won the 2022 Grand National when unraced beyond 3m1f and this Irish challenger (who's one year older) bids to repeat the feat; hat-trick this season included two small-field Grade 2s and when his winning sequence came to an end it was in the Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham Festival, where some slow jumping in the early stages left him with plenty to do but he made late inroads to finish fourth; he still looks capable of better and the longer trip could suit.
runners.comment: Slow early jumping in 3m1f Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham but he made late inroads into 4th

runners.name: Gorgeous Tom
runners.spotlight: Fourth behind two of these in 3m Grade 1 novice event at Cheltenham last March; ran creditably all three starts this winter, notably 11l fourth of 24 off this mark to Panic Attack at Newbury (3m2f) in November when he stuck to his task very well having been left at the shambolic start; improvement is needed but that race gave encouragement for longer trips and his dam is half-sister to Cappa Bleu who was placed in this race in 2012 and 2013.
runners.comment: 11l fourth off this mark to Panic Attack at Newbury (3m2f) and stuck to his task very well

runners.name: Gerri Colombe
runners.spotlight: Five Grade 1 wins and runner-up in the 2024 Cheltenham Gold Cup; reappearance at Christmas came after 421 days off and this season's form is well short of his best, but second to Grangeclare West at Fairyhouse was a big step back in the right direction and he comfortably obliged at odds-on for latest outing; cheekpieces last three starts; well handicapped on his old form and could be on his way back.
runners.comment: 2nd in 2024 Cheltenham Gold Cup and, some positive signs lately, could be on his way back

runners.name: High Class Hero
runners.spotlight: One positive is his staying-on third over an extended 3m4f in the bet365 Gold Cup at Sandown (good; 5lb higher today) last April; another is that he's trained by Willie Mullins, who described him in November as likely to be "an ideal type for the Grand National"; after a solid reappearance, he's been pulled up in good handicaps on his last two outings but both were in the mud and he got loose before the latest, which presumably didn't help.
runners.comment: Staying-on third over an extended 3m4f at Sandown (good; 5lb higher today) last April
runners.owner: Sullivan Group Limited & P Crowley & Neill Hughes

runners.name: Iroko
runners.spotlight: Staying-on fourth in last season's Grand National (good to soft) off 5lb lower as just a 7yo; a second crack at it has been his main target this term but he looked better than ever when winning from Firefox in a hot, three-runner race at Ascot (2m5f; second run after wind surgery) in December and had an excuse (scoped dirty) at the Cheltenham Festival last month; has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Staying-on fourth in 2025 National off 5lb lower; scoped dirty at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Imperial Saint
runners.spotlight: Novice campaign last season went well, notably over the conventional fences here where he registered three handicap wins (2m/2m4f) and was second at this meeting (3m1f); resumed progress with a win at Haydock (3m1f) this January but badly hampered at the Cheltenham Festival; this trip is a big step into the unknown but he needs a second look.
runners.comment: 3 wins over conventional fences here; 3m1f win this January but this trip is a big unknown

runners.name: Haiti Couleurs
runners.spotlight: In the last 13 months he's won Cheltenham's NH Chase (3m6f), the Irish National (3m5f), a hurdle race, Welsh National (near 3m7f; 12lb higher today) and a Newbury Grade 2; no way can he be ruled out, particularly as he finished well on top in those four chases, but there have also been two major blips this season; latest was in the Cheltenham Gold Cup, for which he was just 6-1 and made most but went to nothing turning for home (writing on the wall four out); however, he's bounced back before, the likely tempo of this race can suit him better and the trip looks like doing so as well.
runners.comment: Irish and Welsh National winner; Gold Cup was disappointing but he's bounced back before

runners.name: Mr Vango
runners.spotlight: Usually goes well in the mud; last season's hat-trick culminated in the 4m2f Midlands National and he rallied to go very close against Twig in the 3m2f Becher over these fences on first outing this term; while there was so much to admire to that point, he's cracked with alarming speed in 3m and 4m1f races on his last two outings.
runners.comment: Nothing to prove for stamina or this track, but he cracked alarmingly on last two outings

runners.name: Twig
runners.spotlight: Second at the 2024 Cheltenham Festival and started this season with two wins, notably by a short head from Mr Vango in the 3m2f Becher over these fences (wore cheekpieces second time); warmed up for this over hurdles after three months off; the Becher performance was doughty stuff but his only go at a marathon trip was last year's National in which he finished a remote tenth, always behind.
runners.comment: A doughty 3m2f win over these fences in the Becher but remote 10th in last year's National

runners.name: Amirite
runners.spotlight: A fluent fourth in the 2m5f Topham over these fences was the first of three big runs in good handicaps last April-July; ran creditably in one (Sandown in April 2024) of his three attempts at 3m4f-3m5f and excuses might be made for his latest two outings (first cross-country, then the mud) but other candidates are more persuasive.
runners.comment: A fluent fourth in the Topham over these fences last April; ran creditably once at 3m4f

runners.name: Captain Cody
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown seventh to Favori De Champdou at Christmas was a glimpse of what he's capable of; the performance which really gets the juices flowing, however, came in the Scottish National at Ayr (4m, good to soft) last April when very confidently ridden and unleashed to find a tremendous response on the run-in, cutting down the leader; it's very hard to rule him out after that, despite the 12lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Tremendous response on the run-in to win 4m Scottish National last April; 12lb higher now
runners.owner: Vincent Caldwell & Angela Shamoon & Mrs A Shamoon Ibgi

runners.name: Banbridge
runners.spotlight: So nearly won his second King George at Kempton over Christmas but, although he's a class act, excelling over 3m on good ground at that speed track doesn't mean that this test will suit him; his moderate late headway in the extended 2m4f Grade 1 Ryanair at Cheltenham last month isn't that persuasive either.
runners.comment: So nearly won his second King George at Kempton, which doesn't mean this test will suit

runners.name: Top Of The Bill
runners.spotlight: One of several who are now in the veteran stage but also in the form of their lives; 7l winner at Haydock (extended 3m1f) in November and returned there with an excellent, rallying second in the Grand National Trial (extended 3m4f, again good to soft) in February; ridden last three starts by Toby McCain-Mitchell; back up 2lb and an interesting prospect for 4m2f but this is a much stronger race.
runners.comment: An excellent, rallying second at Haydock (extended 3m4f) but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Monty's Star
runners.spotlight: Just 1-11 as a chaser, largely because he keeps trying in vain at Grade 1 level; fourth in last year's Cheltenham Gold Cup was one of his peak efforts, as was this season's never-nearer fifth in a Leopardstown Grade 1 at Christmas; he didn't fare so well in the Irish Gold Cup last time but was the only one matching strides with the top-class winner on the home turn; going up markedly in trip today, in a handicap, makes him a mouthwatering proposition.
runners.comment: Trying in vain at Grade 1 level; going up markedly in trip in a handicap is mouthwatering

runners.name: I Am Maximus
runners.spotlight: His only three long-distance events resulted in an Irish National win in 2023, a storming success in this race in 2024 (soft) and a valiant second, from further off the pace, in repeat bid here in 2025 (good to soft); what a record that is, and 50-1 second in a 3m Grade 1 at Leopardstown on penultimate start gives every encouragement that all his ability remains; the return of cheekpieces (tried once in 2022) is interesting but, off 1lb higher than last year, he should be set for another prominent finish.
runners.comment: 1st and 2nd in last two Grand Nationals; penultimate race suggests all his ability remains

runners.name: Champ Kiely
runners.spotlight: The furthest he's been is 3m1f in Grade 1 novice at Punchestown Festival (good to yielding) last April which he won in good style, showing improved form; went close over 2m in November and although he slipped up next time and met with heavy defeats twice since, he went well enough to the home turn in Irish Gold Cup on latest; whether he'll stay 4m2f is a big question but he could outrun his odds for a long way.
runners.comment: Whether he'll stay 4m2f is a big question but he could outrun his odds for a long way

runners.name: Spanish Harlem
runners.spotlight: Career with Willie Mullins took a long time to catch fire but it's done so this season and he looked set for victory in the Thyestes at Gowran Park (3m1f, heavy) in January when unseating at the final fence; pulled up behind Grangeclare West last time but back catalogue includes a creditable sixth in the Scottish National and fourth in bet365 Cup at Sandown, so he stays long distances; his revised mark since the Thyestes would be the chief worry.
runners.comment: Stays long distances; his revised mark since bold show in the Thyestes is the chief worry

runners.name: Quai De Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Third in the 3m5f Irish National last April, despite an indifferent round of jumping, and third of 16 with cheekpieces at Leopardstown on latest outing was his best form, despite just the 2m5f; kept jumping out to his right in the latter and although a case can be made for him, it would have to gloss over that he's completed only four of his eight chases.
runners.comment: Third in the 3m5f Irish National last April but completed only four of his eight chases

runners.name: Marble Sands
runners.spotlight: Negotiated these fences safely in the 2m5f Topham one year ago, albeit fading into 12th; a strong-staying display to win at Cheltenham (near 3m4f, soft; 5lb higher today) in November hints at untapped potential over long distances.
runners.comment: A strong-staying winner at Cheltenham (near 3m4f, soft; 5lb higher today) in November
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Beauport
runners.spotlight: Took well to these fences in last year's renewal but subsided rapidly after three out, which might have been surprising for one who'd won a 4m2f Midlands National (March 2024) but he seemed to race a bit too zestfully in the front rank; 3lb lower today, having run respectably on reappearance but moderately in two hurdle races since (wind surgery before latest); now tongue tied first time; a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Stays but he was too zestful in this race last year; now tongue tied first time

runners.name: Answer To Kayf
runners.spotlight: Faded into sixth at Punchestown (3m7f) last May on his only attempt at a marathon trip; worth another go, having rallied so well to win a major 3m handicap at Navan in November, but it's a little hard to ignore that clearly his best five ratings have been registered on soft or heavy going, as have all his four wins.
runners.comment: Faded in a 3m7f race; clearly his best five ratings were registered on soft or heavy going

runners.name: The Real Whacker
runners.spotlight: 11lb below his peak mark and well handicapped judged on best efforts, the most recent of which were over 3m on good ground late in 2024; muted in this three runs this term, only one of which was over fences but he ended up tailed off in that Wetherby race, which he'd won a year earlier; those past glories may now be too distant.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on peak efforts but those past glories may now be too distant

runners.name: Grangeclare West
runners.spotlight: Excellent 3l third in last year's renewal, somehow renewing his effort after diving at the final fence cost him plenty of momentum; creditable fifth at Sandown just three weeks later and surging clear from Gerri Colombe at Fairyhouse this February, after another last-fence dive, showed that he's at least as good as ever; 3lb higher than 12 months ago but lots to like.
runners.comment: 3l third last year, after diving at the final fence (up 3lb today); won his latest outing

runners.name: Lecky Watson
runners.spotlight: Unraced beyond 3m2f; came into his own as a novice last term, notably in the 3m Grade 1 at Cheltenham Festival which he won by 4l from Stellar Story; no impact at the top level in Ireland this season and he clambered over the final fence on his way to a heavy defeat behind several of today's rivals in a Grade 3 last time, so current prospects don't look so bright.
runners.comment: Won 3m Grade 1 at Cheltenham last March from Stellar Story; no impact in Ireland this term

races.race_name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (Grade 2) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bass Hunter
runners.spotlight: Assembling an impressive CV in bumpers, following all-the-way wins at Newbury (good to soft) and Ascot (soft) with an excellent third at the Cheltenham Festival (good) where he stuck to his task gamely after being headed over 1f out; that's the best form on offer and he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Followed wins at Newbury and Ascot with excellent third at Cheltenham; the one to beat

runners.name: Look Me
runners.spotlight: Has a compelling profile, having gone close on debut at the 2025 Irish Grand National meeting (soft to heavy) before making a winning start for Willie Mullins back at Fairyhouse (heavy) in February; has to prove his effectiveness on less testing ground, but he looks a smart prospect and this owner/trainer combination were successful in this event 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Smart prospect; followed close second with a cosy win, both at Fairyhouse; major chance

runners.name: One Knight
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since joining Emma Lavelle; easily saw off eight rivals at Hereford (good ground) last month after a long layoff; well up the pecking order on ratings and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: 2-2 since joining Emma Lavelle; well up the pecking order on ratings; should have a say

runners.name: Risky Obsession
runners.spotlight: Might have been unsuited by heavy ground when tailed off in Listed race at Newbury (100-30) in February, and he'd previously made an excellent impression when winning an Irish point (good to yielding) and a bumper at Warwick (good to soft); surely still has untapped potential and jockey bookings suggest he's the pick of Dan Skelton's three.
runners.comment: Well beaten on heavy last time but previous wins were full of promise; interesting

runners.name: Smile John Boy
runners.spotlight: Made all at Hereford (good) in November and has run well in defeat twice since, albeit finishing behind Risky Obsession at Warwick and Merlin Allen at Kempton; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Two good runs in defeat since his Hereford win but others have stronger claims

runners.name: Nightflyer
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for a different yard at Catterick (soft) in January, but his limitations seem to have been exposed since; only fourth at Warwick 16 days ago and he has much more on his plate here.
runners.comment: Only fourth at Warwick 16 days ago and has much more on his plate here

runners.name: Forthfactor
runners.spotlight: Ridden by Heidi Palin on both outings for current yard, winning at Hexham (good to soft) before finishing a creditable fifth to Bass Hunter at Ascot (soft) when he faded late on; may not have reached his ceiling just yet; one of three contenders for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: Hexham winner before fifth to Bass Hunter at Ascot; may not have reached ceiling just yet

runners.name: Ronnie Russet
runners.spotlight: Significant improvement with each of his three runs, winning at Newcastle in December and January (both good to soft); not hard pressed to pull 11l clear in a big field on latest; solid candidate with each-way claims.
runners.comment: Significant improvement with each run, winning twice at Newcastle; each-way claims

runners.name: Chicker
runners.spotlight: Successful in his first two races (on good ground) but has come unstuck in a higher grade since, well beaten behind Bass Hunter at the Cheltenham Festival last month; difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind Bass Hunter at the Cheltenham Festival; difficult to recommend

runners.name: Rices Pool
runners.spotlight: His second in an Irish point has substance and he justified favouritism in bumper at Taunton (good to soft) with more in hand than the relatively short winning margin suggests; Ben Pauling runs four and stable jockey Ben Jones rides this one; improvement required but certainly possible.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Taunton with something in hand; improvement required but possible

runners.name: The Perfect Poet
runners.spotlight: A fourth runner for the Ben Pauling yard, and like two of the others he's 1-1 under rules; well-backed favourite when comfortably accounting for four rivals at Fontwell (soft) four weeks ago; could be anything but this is a much stiffer task.
runners.comment: Favourite when making winning debut at Fontwell; could be anything but this is much harder

runners.name: Cooltobecareless
runners.spotlight: Favourite in both his bumpers, taking second at Chepstow (good) before going one better at Fakenham (soft); remains open to improvement but has plenty to find on the figures and Harry Skelton rides stablemate Risky Obsession.
runners.comment: Workmanlike winner at Fakenham; remains unexposed but has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Merry Away
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for £72,000 after finishing third in an Irish point in November, and he dug deep when making all in bumper at Warwick (good to soft) five weeks ago on debut for current yard; Ben Jones jumps ship to ride Rices Pool, but this one is unexposed and cannot be discounted.
runners.comment: Dug deep when making all at Warwick on rules debut; unexposed and cannot be discounted

runners.name: Moments Away
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement with each of his three runs, beaten off only late on when second at Chepstow (soft) on debut for this yard last month; may have more in the tank but he's still a maiden and this is his sternest assignment to date; fitted with a tongue-strap for the first time.
runners.comment: Steady improvement but he's 0-3 and this is his sternest assignment to date; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Merlin Allen
runners.spotlight: Beat his 11 opponents handsomely in junior event over C&D in November and ran well under a penalty against older rivals at Kempton (again good to soft) seven weeks ago; in good hands and remains open to progress.
runners.comment: C&D winner of junior event before running well against older rivals at Kempton; respected

runners.name: Governors Rock
runners.spotlight: Progressive in three outings in Ireland, winning under a penalty at Ballinrobe (soft) and subsequently changing hands for £100,000; however, he ran no sort of race in a Listed event at Newbury (heavy; 12-1) on his debut for current yard and this contest is even more competitive.
runners.comment: Two wins in Ireland but ran badly on British debut and this is even more competitive
runners.owner: Gummerson, Martin, W T Walsh & Williams

runners.name: Thelastmanfrompenr
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the stable's smart 1m7f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Notlongtillmay; off to the perfect start at Kelso (heavy; 7-1) in February, leading inside the final 1f and beating eight opponents; open to improvement but he's in much deeper here.
runners.comment: Off to the perfect start at Kelso in February; open to improvement but in much deeper here

runners.name: Barachiel
runners.spotlight: A little more improvement when runner-up at Taunton (good) last month, on first outing after wind surgery; now fitted with a hood and a tongue-strap but he looks out of his depth in this grade.
runners.comment: Some improvement when runner-up at Taunton last month but looks out of his depth

runners.name: Le Roi Remi
runners.spotlight: Overcame greenness to win at Sandown (good to soft) in November with plenty in hand, but that race has not worked out well and he could finish only midfield at Kempton next time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Wide-margin Sandown win has not worked out well; only midfield at Kempton next time

runners.name: Makingplansfor
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Newton Abbot and Lingfield but put firmly in his place in Listed race at Cheltenham in between; needs a great leap forward.
runners.comment: Put firmly in place in Listed race at Cheltenham in November; needs a great leap forward

runners.name: Dashing Donkey
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; won twice at Newmarket (1m, good to soft) last summer; only 2lb higher than for the second success and he is fully effective at this track; considered.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair mark on 2025 best; should make a bold bid from the front

runners.name: South Kensington
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Martin Dunne in the second half of 2025; unplaced in two 7f handicaps on Tapeta for new stable last month; new headgear added today; stamina for 1m still to be proven.
runners.comment: Unplaced in two runs for new stable last month; new headgear; stamina not assured

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Two wins over shorter on AW this winter; solid third at Lingfield (1m) two weeks ago; promise on turf early in his career and another good run should be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Two AW wins over shorter this winter; placed over 1m latest; should be in the shake up

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: Close second at Wolverhampton on Monday's handicap debut (8.6f), finishing off his race well; the front two drew clear on that occasion and rates a major player with Hector Crouch taking over in the saddle; turf debut.
runners.comment: Strong at the finish when 2nd on Monday's handicap debut (8.6f, AW); unexposed; key player

runners.name: Moonlight Bomb
runners.spotlight: 13th time lucky when winning a 9.4f handicap at Wolverhampton in December; not so good twice in January and now returns from an 11-week break; Oisin Murphy booked so a better showing would seem likely.
runners.comment: December's AW win brings him into the picture; two defeats in January need leaving behind

runners.name: Queen Of Good News
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; finished second in her two course runs as a 2yo; well handicapped on last season's best and she has undergone wind surgery during her 176-day absence; should be winning races this year but this perhaps not the day to catch her.
runners.comment: Promise in two course runs; had wind op since last seen; may come on for this reappearance

runners.name: Borderline Madness
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a C&D handicap (good to soft) last July and she added an AW success to her tally in September; not handicapped out of things now returning from 117 days off; stable also run Dashing Donkey.
runners.comment: Two wins last year included one over C&D; absent 117 days; stable also run Dashing Donkey

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Leicester last May off this mark; close fourth at Kempton 17 days ago where she hung markedly right up the home straight; easy lead unlikely today.
runners.comment: On a winning mark and not beaten far on AW latest; looked a hard ride there though

runners.name: Potters Prince
runners.spotlight: Improving his RPR run by run and he was placed for the first time when third of eight at Lingfield (1m, AW) five weeks ago; could have more to offer after just five starts but he doesn't look the easiest of rides.
runners.comment: Improving run by run but another step forward is required now switched to turf

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: Won an 8.3f handicap at Nottingham last May when rated 6lb higher; two low-key runs for his new stable in February, refusing to settle in first-time blinkers at Newcastle in February (slowly-run race); others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Not shone in two runs for new stable; dropped to a dangerous mark but not a solid option

runners.name: Merapi
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Ed Walker and she was well beaten on her stable debut in February; last month's staying-on fifth at Wolverhampton (8.6f) was more like it though and she needs a close look off her lowly mark.
runners.comment: Signs of a revival when fifth at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; on a dangerous mark now

runners.name: Miss Monte Carlo
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in seven runs for Ed Walker but she finished second in three of them; sold for 7,000gns in October 2024; returns from a 540-day absence and dropping back to 1m far from certain to suit; on the plus side, she has been dropped 6lb during her absence.
runners.comment: Absent 540 days; makes stable debut over a trip that may be on the sharp side

runners.name: Le Pelerin
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who ended 2025 with two heavy defeats; subsequent wind operation could help but he returns in a competitive race for the grade.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden; had wind op since last seen; returns in a competitive event

runners.name: Devious Devan
runners.spotlight: Struggled in 2025 and fared little better on last month's return to action (after wind surgery); tumbling down the weights but comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Regressing but drops into a 0-60 and last month's return should have sharpened him up

races.race_name: TigerBet.co.uk National Extra Places Paid Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Del Maro
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in three 1m novice/maiden runs but showed high level of ability; improved for the step up to 1m2f when finishing third of eight in the Group 3 Zetland Stakes at Newmarket when last seen; should have no difficulty winning a novice and he sets a high standard for his rivals to aim at.
runners.comment: Third in 1m2f Group 3 when last seen; unexposed at the trip and sets a high standard

runners.name: Sintra
runners.spotlight: Two promising runs on AW at around 1m; open to improvement but it will be needed if he is to lower the colours of Del Maro.
runners.comment: Two promising AW runs at around 1m; more to come but such progress is essential

runners.name: Point Of Law
runners.spotlight: First foal of a Listed-placed 1m2f winner (RPR 102), herself a sister to 1m-1m4f winner Educator; appealing pedigree and market confidence would look significant.
runners.comment: First foal of a Listed-placed 1m2f winner; market useful on debut
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Knowledge
runners.spotlight: Benefited from the runner-up hanging sharply left when the narrow winner of a 1m novice at Southwell in January; that form hasn't really worked out and, while he is open to improvement and 1m2f should suit, he faces a tough task under a penalty today.
runners.comment: Narrow win in a 1m AW novice in January; tough task conceding weight all round today

runners.name: Florentine Law
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to three winners including Marquisat (7.5f-12.4f including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 118) and Luisa Casati (1m2f AW/1m4f turf Listed; 104); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Listed winning dam of 1m/1m1f Group 1 winner Dreamloper; stable going well and he needs a market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners, notably Group 3 scorer Marquisat (RPR 118); yard going well

races.race_name: TigerBet.co.uk Fast Sign Up Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Harriet Quimby
runners.spotlight: Promising debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last October; beaten even-money favourite at Newcastle later that month but still ran a creditable race; out of an Oaks winner so stepping up in trip looks a good move; improvement is essential all the same.
runners.comment: Two encouraging runs last October; step up to 1m2f should suit; others have achieved more

runners.name: Emerald Reine
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; second foal of a minor 6f winner (RPR 55); she would be a surprise winner on debut.
runners.comment: 3,000gns yearling; dam a minor 6f winner; looks an unlikely winner

runners.name: Maldives
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling; first foal of a Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (RPR 102), herself a half-sister to multiple Group 1 winner Luxembourg; has some fancy entries but she is starting out in a warm novice.
runners.comment: 2.9m gns yearling; fine pedigree and has some fancy entries; starts out in a warm novice

runners.name: Bint Kilfrush
runners.spotlight: Justified short-priced favouritism to win a 1m fillies' novice at Southwell on New Year's Day; new trip will suit judged on her stout pedigree; no easy task under a penalty though.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut (1m, AW) in January; new trip a positive; penalty demands more

runners.name: Harper Violet
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree and she was sent off favourite for her Doncaster debut last October (7f, soft), eventually finishing third of the six runners in a steadily run affair; open to improvement over this longer trip/faster ground.
runners.comment: Fair 3rd in a steadily-run 7f maiden (soft) on debut last October; bred to do much better
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Bintsaleh
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when last of five in a 1m fillies' novice (good) here in October; this 925,000gns yearling is bred for middle distances and she should leave that initial effort well behind her this year.
runners.comment: Green when last of five here (1m) last October; bred to leave that well behind this year

runners.name: Premier Cru
runners.spotlight: She made an encouraging start to her career when fifth of 13 in a 1m fillies' maiden at Kempton in January; today's longer trip will suit but this looks a tough race.
runners.comment: Promising debut in January (1m, AW) but she's in a warmer race and looks vulnerable

races.race_name: Ma And Pa Peak Birthday Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Silver Gunn
runners.spotlight: Course winner who had irregular form in 2025 but he only just failed on his reappearance at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last spring and he's 4lb lower now; on the cards that he'll return with a good effort.
runners.comment: Course winner; irregular form in 2025 but he went close on reappearance off 4lb lower

runners.name: Patagonia Girl
runners.spotlight: Improved to win first two handicaps at about 1m2f, at Chester and Salisbury (good to firm and good), and later defeats came in higher grades than this; off since July; market can guide as to expectations today.
runners.comment: Won first 2 handicaps at 1m2f; beaten in better races since; off since July; check betting

runners.name: Siouxfonic
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in September who won a 1m4f AW maiden at Wolverhampton in December; not so good at Kempton (1m3f) since and there's a further drop in trip today but the return to turf could suit now switched to a handicap; yard also runs Shihoku.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; won 1m4f AW maiden in December; down in trip but now switched to a handicap

runners.name: Desert Heart
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in qualifying runs at up to 1m2f; has yet to distinguish himself in handicaps but the ground was soft in his one attempt at this level on turf and perhaps he'll do better back on good ground.
runners.comment: Some promise in qualifying runs; low-key handicap form but return to turf could suit

runners.name: Mc'ted
runners.spotlight: Won first three handicaps for new yard on AW and turf (1m-1m2f) by emphatic margins but he couldn't defy a penalty in October and was a further 5lb higher when soundly beaten in November; now has to show he can cope with current mark; regular cheekpieces left off.
runners.comment: Won first 3 handicaps for this yard (1m-1m2f) but up weights and needs to show he can cope

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Some promise at 1m2f in four starts for Roger Varian and he made a terrific start for this yard when persevering well to lead close home on Lingfield AW (1m4f) on recent handicap debut; up 3lb and this galloping track should suit now back at 1m2f; good claims.
runners.comment: Some promise for R Varian; kept on to lead close home on handicap/yard debut (1m4f, AW)

runners.name: Beach Point
runners.spotlight: Successful in first two starts after cheekpieces were refitted, at Sandown (1m2f; good and soft) last summer, but his form went to pot in two of three runs after; needs to return in much better form.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f wins at Sandown (good and soft) last summer; well below best in 2 of 3 runs since
runners.owner: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher

runners.name: A Major Payne
runners.spotlight: A maiden after 14 starts but he's been involved in some tight finishes at 1m and didn't seem to be lacking in the stamina department when third of ten over this new 1m2f trip at Redcar (good to soft) in September; that may give him some new options but he probably needs more, even so.
runners.comment: 0-14 but involved in some tight finishes at 1m; seemed to stay 1m2f on final start

runners.name: Shihoku
runners.spotlight: Won AW maiden (about 1m1f) in France last spring; rather up and down over a range of trips for this yard, albeit this is easier than the two turf handicaps contested in Britain; hood refitted; reservations remain.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden in France last spring; up and down over range of trips for this yard

runners.name: Salamanca City
runners.spotlight: Both wins at 1m2f (good to soft and good), the latest at Haydock last April; consistent in defeat in the second half of 2025 until below best on soft ground here (about 1m3f) in September; thereabouts on best form.
runners.comment: Both wins at 1m2f, latest last April; consistent after but below best on soft in September

runners.name: Bintabuha
runners.spotlight: Galloped on strongly when winning one of just two 1m2f handicaps for William Knight, at Wetherby (good to firm) last spring but later forays into Listed company didn't go well and nor did the return to handicaps on final two starts last year; still of interest back on turf for new yard.
runners.comment: Won fair 1m2f Wetherby handicap last spring for W Knight; lesser runs since; new yard

runners.name: Something
runners.spotlight: Three front-running wins at 1m4f, on AW and turf (good to soft; effective on good), but he doesn't have to lead, as shown when held up before having a tight passage behind an improver at Kempton (1m3f) in February; rare to see him over 1m2f but he has a good run in him back on turf.
runners.comment: Three 1m4f wins on AW and turf; good second on AW latest; can go well back on turf

runners.name: Currumbin
runners.spotlight: Two staying wins on AW last winter and has since won over hurdles but he was tailed off in the latter discipline when last seen in October and, reappearing over 1m2f, others are higher up the list.
runners.comment: Two staying AW wins last winter; has won over hurdles since; work to do over 1m2f

races.race_name: In Memory Of Roger Clarke - Vet Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Generic
runners.spotlight: Travelled better than Enemy Agent but was unable to repel his late thrust in a 1m Kempton maiden 17 days ago; has a 7lb turnaround for that head defeat today and may b able to reverse the placings.
runners.comment: Second, beaten a head, by Enemy Agent on debut 17 days ago (1m, AW); 7lb better off today

runners.name: Al Maslool
runners.spotlight: His second run was better than his debut (both 1m on Lingfield's AW track) and he still looked in need of the experience when third last month; can step forward again now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Second run better than his debut (both 1m, AW); has more to offer now switched to turf

runners.name: Enemy Agent
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience but kept on well to collar Generic close home at Kempton 17 days ago (1m); sent off favourite there so a big run was clearly expected; not weighted to confirm those placings but a key player all the same.
runners.comment: Overcame inexperience to beat Generic by a head on last month's debut (1m, AW); contender

runners.name: Master Of Keraunos
runners.spotlight: Two 7f runs in February, improving at the second time of asking; another similar step forward for the extra furlong would see him on the premises.
runners.comment: Placed in two 7f runs in February; steps up in trip with further progress possible

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Wheal Kitty (1m; RPR 67), Kasino (6f AW 2yo; 57) and Frasard (US 1m1f turf); useful standard to aim at and likely best watched.
runners.comment: 1,000gns yearling; half-sister to three winners; likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Sharp Romance
runners.spotlight: 2,500gns foal; third foal; half-sister to winners Charming Whisper (7f-1m2f including 2yo; RPR 87) and Mister Phoenix (German 8.7f); dam 1m4f AW winner (74); she will need to be useful to make a winning debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners out of a 1m4f AW winner; tough enough task on debut

runners.name: Thunderhoof
runners.spotlight: He has shaped with promise in two 7f AW events this year and he is bred to appreciate the step up in trip; each-way shout if improving for 1m.
runners.comment: RPR in the low 70s in two 7f AW runs; bred to appreciate the extra furlong

runners.name: Whole Lotta Rosie
runners.spotlight: 45,000gns yearling; first foal of a maiden sister to a US 1m3f Grade 2 winner; might find this trip on the sharp side on debut.
runners.comment: 45,000gns yearling; bred for further and perhaps a longer-term prospect

runners.name: My Mate Roger
runners.spotlight: Attracted support on his Kempton debut in December (1m) and ran well for a close second of the ten runners; can do better and he is more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd on Kempton debut (1m); open to improvement and more appealing than many

runners.name: Strong Voice
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in his two 7f runs at Southwell last October; the extra furlong and switch to turf will need to have a positive effect if he is to take this though.
runners.comment: Two 7f runs at Southwell last year were encouraging but he'll need much more to take this

runners.name: Timely Salute
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two Chelmsford novice events as a 2yo (7f/1m); gelded since latest; improvement essential now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Modest form in two Chelmsford novice events last year; been gelded; improvement essential

races.race_name: TigerBet.co.uk Best Odds Guaranteed Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Organise
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 11-10f for his Southwell debut (7f) in November and his supporters had few concerns as he travelled well prior to surging clear, despite evident inexperience, in the final furlong; looked a colt of considerable promise on that occasion and he holds strong claims under a penalty.
runners.comment: Quickened smartly to win by daylight at Southwell (7f) on debut; plenty more to come

runners.name: Weheedd
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to US winners Astroturf (1m-8.5f AW/dirt) and Kintra (5f turf 2yo); unraced dam closely related to 1m winner Encompassing; has a useful standard to aim at on debut but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling; half-brother to 2 winners in the US; has a useful standard to aim at

runners.name: Ya Karim
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of his Kempton debut last September (7f) when readily making all over 7f here one month later (Pentonville 5.75l back in fifth); 1m should suit and he commands plenty of respect; hooded first time.
runners.comment: Made all for ready 7f win here last October (2nd run); more to come this year; now hooded

runners.name: Pentonville
runners.spotlight: Behind Ya Karim here (7f, good) on sole 2yo start; similar form when third past the post (promoted to second) at Kempton last month; improvement required.
runners.comment: Promise in his two starts, the latest last month, but this race demands a good deal more
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Outback Heat
runners.spotlight: Dictated a steady pace when winning a 1m maiden at Newcastle in February; showed a willing attitude there but he will need to raise his game if he is to defy a penalty.
runners.comment: Made most when winning over 1m at Newcastle in February; this is tougher under a penalty

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a 1m1f maiden in France (for Andre Fabre); beaten 28l in a Huntingdon maiden hurdle for Jamie Snowden last month; sold for £1,800 since; best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Well held in a French maiden and a maiden hurdle; sold £1,800 since latest; opposable
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: Pixie Diva
runners.spotlight: Both her wins have come on AW, the latest when hugging the stands' rail and leading in the last stride at Newcastle (5f; blanket finish) two weeks ago, but she has run well on turf too; 2lb rise for last time shouldn't prevent another good run.
runners.comment: Comes here on the back of a last-gasp Newcastle success (5f) last month; respected up 2lb

runners.name: Almaty Star
