runners.spotlight: Ultra-reliable operator on AW, running another solid race when second to a class-dropper at Wolverhampton (5f; first time visor) last month; lesser strike-rate on turf but he is fully effective on it and he has been given a real chance by the handicapper, dropped 10lb for his last two turf defeats (in Class 3); should go well.
runners.comment: String of good AW runs this winter; 8lb lower than for latest turf run; should go well

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for her new connections when coming home 2.25l clear of her rivals in a Class 5 at Chelmsford 16 days ago (5f); she looked to be going nowhere through the first half of that race, under the pump before the field had completed a furlong, but the pace folded and she was strong at the finish; a 6lb rise and stronger opposition could find her out.
runners.comment: Outpaced early but ran on well to win at Chelmsford on stable debut; this is a better race

runners.name: Golden Long
runners.spotlight: 6f winner in Hong Kong (good ground); absent for 429 days prior to his British debut at Southwell seven weeks ago, a race where he was heavily backed but missed the break and then took a fierce hold through the early part of the race; he kept on nicely at the finish with that in mind and he looks a strong contender back on turf.
runners.comment: Ex-Hong Kong; gambled on at Southwell in February & shaped with some promise; interesting

runners.name: Thunder Star
runners.spotlight: She was returning from a five-month break when finishing in midfield at Doncaster 13 days ago (5f, good to soft), a race where she tired in the final furlong but still did best of the far-side group; two wins and two seconds from her six C&D runs and she has solid claims off a fair mark.
runners.comment: Good record over C&D; did best of the far-side group at Doncaster last time; contender

runners.name: Brave Nation
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank in 2025 but there were some good runs in defeat and he starts this season off an 8lb lower mark than for last year's reappearance; solid third in one run at Yarmouth; best on good ground or faster; makes his seasonal return with a hood back on and with his stable in good form.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025 but retained his ability; returns off dangerous mark with his yard in form

runners.name: Station Bar
runners.spotlight: Out of half-sister to Accidental Agent whose fine career for the yard included a Group 1 at 1m; came nearest to a win when losing out narrowly on Kempton AW (6f) in November but his first 1m run, there in March, promised more as he had anything but a clear run; can do better as 3yo.
runners.comment: Closest to first win over 6f on AW; hampered in running over 1m latest; bred to do better

runners.name: Zoustellar
runners.spotlight: Three handicap runs on AW, not getting much luck in either of the last two at about this trip; may be a bit better than her current mark if handling turf at the first attempt and taking well to the added cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not had much luck in latest two handicaps at 1m on AW; now switches to turf

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: Failed to find much when returned to 1m on Chelmsford AW in February but the two immediately in front of her won their next two races and she'd earlier come home nicely to finish a never-nearer third at Kempton (7f); should be competitive back on turf now handicapping.
runners.comment: Keeping-on 3rd at Kempton (7f); worth another go at 1m now trying her luck in a handicap

runners.name: Time To Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Saddle slipped two starts ago but she had made an encouraging handicap debut for new yard previously and improved on it when just holding the runner-up at Wolverhampton (also about 1m) in March; up 2lb but still time for her to do better if as effective back on turf.
runners.comment: Up 2lb for narrow Wolverhampton win over 1m; still time for her to do better back on turf

runners.name: Lady Vanguard
runners.spotlight: Little to latch on to in three runs at 7f and 1m (hood added latest, now discarded) but her opening mark is modest and Oisin Murphy, who used to have a fair record for the yard, has his first ride for them since 2023.
runners.comment: Little to latch on to in three runs at 7f/1m but has a modest mark and O Murphy rides

runners.name: Slack Bob
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on turf debut (heavy); has raced on AW since, not having the cleanest of runs when a keeping-on third at Wolverhampton (7f) in January; four siblings won at between 1m and 1m4f and he looks ready for a go at 1m; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Creditable handicap runs at 6f/7f; four siblings won at 1m-1m4f so new trip could suit
runners.owner: The Old Etonian Racing Syndicate II

runners.name: Phantom Shadow
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in 7f and 6f novice events on AW as 2yo (blinkers on last two starts, now replaced with a hood); gelded since; something new may dawn now switched to a more suitable level.
runners.comment: Minor promise at 7f/6f as 2yo, blinkered twice (now hooded); may do better at this level

runners.name: Sports Day
runners.spotlight: Minor form in 7f and 1m events as 2yo but gelded since and his pedigree suggests the return to this trip should suit, as will the switch to handicap company; interesting to see how the betting goes.
runners.comment: Minor form at 7f/1m; gelded since; should find this level more suitable; check market

races.race_name: Tiptop Thunder Throne / EBF Junior 'National Hunt' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Planters Punch
runners.spotlight: Made a good start when beating a clear second at Hereford (2m, good) in November and he built on that with an impressive win in similar conditions at Bangor 11 days ago; has a double penalty but he looks a useful prospect and is a major player again.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 and he was impressive when scoring at Bangor 11 days ago; major player again

runners.name: Guts And Glory
runners.spotlight: Fell in her only point, but she's finished second in both hurdle runs; was no match for an odds-on rival at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft) last month but she gave it a decent shot from the front and was clear of the third; should go well again and she's in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both hurdle runs and latest was at Market Rasen last month; in the mix

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to bumper/hurdle winner Hillfinch; 6-1 for a novice over C&D (good to soft) last month but she got outpaced before finishing a well-held fifth of seven; should know more this time but she needs to leave that initial form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Made a low-key start over C&D last month and he needs to leave that form a long way behind

runners.name: Longmoor
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed in point/2m3f hurdle, half-sister to 3m chase winner Presenting Belle; weak 11-1 shot at Exeter (2m, good) last month but he rallied in the closing stages and did well to snatch third; that was an encouraging debut and he should improve for the experience.
runners.comment: Promising third on Exeter debut (2m, good) last month and he should know more this time

runners.name: Whitletts
runners.spotlight: Built on her debut third at Warwick when runner-up behind a 4-11 shot at Ludlow (2m, good to soft) in December; that was promising but she was pulled up at Hereford in February and left Dan Skelton for £8,500 since; now returns after another break and has something to prove for new yard.
runners.comment: Showed promise in first two runs but was pulled up in her final start for Dan Skelton

runners.name: Look Don't Stare
runners.spotlight: Big prices and he's finished a well-held ninth in two bumpers, with latest at Wincanton last month; best watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Well-held ninth in two bumpers and he's best watched on hurdling debut

runners.name: Mistral St Georges
runners.spotlight: In contention before falling heavily at penultimate flight on debut at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in February, and was unlucky to come to grief four out at Bangor (hampered and unseated rider) 11 days ago; seems to have ability but he was 20-1 last time and that was in the race Planters Punch won by 15l.
runners.comment: Fell on debut and he unseated in the race won by Planters Punch at Bangor last time

races.race_name: Tiptop Duel Flush Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tenter Le Tout
runners.spotlight: Won smoothly on good to soft at Exeter and Chepstow (Grade 2) on first two hurdle starts; has found things tough at Cheltenham in last two runs but the first was behind Maestro Conti and latest was in the Triumph last month; sets the standard back in much calmer waters and she's a key player back at the scene of her last win.
runners.comment: Won Grade 2 over C&D in December; tough task in Triumph latest and she sets standard here

runners.name: Jollie Dame
runners.spotlight: Form figures may look encouraging but she's been beaten between 16l and 23l in her last four races, including two runs over hurdles (2m, good); still has potential but she has quite a lot to find here and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Form figures may look encouraging but she's been beaten 16l or more in her last four runs

runners.name: Molten Sea
runners.spotlight: Quite a useful handicapper at up to 2m for William Haggas on the Flat; well-held fifth of 13 on hurdling debut at Hereford in February but she left that form well behind when justifying favouritism with a 14l win at Kempton (2m, good) last month; has a penalty but that was an impressive win and she could have a lot more to offer in this sphere; strongly respected.
runners.comment: Quite useful on the Flat and she won by 14l over hurdles at Kempton last time; respected

runners.name: Elated
runners.spotlight: From a good family and she showed promise in two bumpers during the winter, more recently when third of ten at Huntingdon (2m, soft) in January; looks interesting now switched to hurdling and she needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Showed promise in both bumpers runs and she looks interesting now switched to hurdling

runners.name: Burning Embers
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare who wasn't beaten far in her sole bumper but she's finished tailed off in two runs over hurdles (2m, good) since; with top yard and still unexposed but needs major improvement; stablemate of Jollie Dame.
runners.comment: Still early days but she's struggled in both hurdle runs and needs a transformation
runners.owner: S T Racing Burnt Ends Partnership

runners.name: Nowherenearmilan
runners.spotlight: Well-held seventh in both bumpers and remote third at a big price on hurdling debut at Stratford (2m, good) last month; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well held in both bumpers and was a remote third on hurdling debut at Stratford

runners.name: Lakeview Lass
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful jumper Midnight It Is but she struggled in both her bumpers and needs a transformation on this switch to hurdling.
runners.comment: Struggled in both her bumpers and needs a transformation on this switch to hurdling

runners.name: Footage Detectives
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in her sole bumper and was tailed off on hurdling debut at Stratford (2m, good) last month; one of two runners for the yard.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in sole bumper and was tailed off on hurdling debut last month

runners.name: Ciao Bellisima
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on Wetherby debut (2m3f, soft) in December and same story when dropped to 2m at Hereford (300-1) the following month.
runners.comment: Tailed off in both hurdle runs during the winter and can only be watched

races.race_name: Tiptop Riders Restroom Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ballybreeze
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 over C&D (good) includes a neck success off 1lb higher last April; failed to get home over 2m3f on soft ground here last month on return from a break; respected back down in trip.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 over C&D includes a win off 1lb higher last April; respected

runners.name: Ostend
runners.spotlight: Needs to improve on his third-place finishes (modest/remote) this term but would be interesting if the ground is good; won off current mark at Market Rasen (2m3f) the last time he raced on such going.
runners.comment: Back on his winning chase mark and would be interesting if the ground is good
runners.owner: Jamie Rock, A Lewis, M Howell & Partner

runners.name: Pep Talking
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-12 for current yard; campaigned mostly at longer trips but the way he shaped over a sharp 2m2f last time (ran well on return from break) suggests this galloping 2m may not be a problem; in the mix.
runners.comment: Record of 5-12 for current yard; ran well on return from break last time

runners.name: Whatyouwaitingfor
runners.spotlight: Good-ground hurdles scorer for previous yard; no match for the winner at Doncaster (2m3f) on chase/stable debut and return from absence but shaped as if this drop back in trip will suit; may progress.
runners.comment: This drop back in trip is likely to suit on second chase start; may progress

runners.name: Koapey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who had a solid record until pulled up in last two starts, effectively failing to back up the sound effort he posted at Uttoxeter (2m, good) on chase/seasonal debut; turnaround is required.
runners.comment: Had a solid record until pulled up in last two starts; turnaround is required

runners.name: Dj Pete
runners.spotlight: Close second in this race last year; prevailed at Bangor (2m1f, good to soft) most recently, bouncing back from a poor effort and taking record since wearing cheekpieces to 2-4; likely player in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Second in this race last year; has a record of 2-4 since wearing cheekpieces

runners.name: Doctors Hill
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D novice (soft) in November and was a good second in a Newbury handicap (2m, good to soft) in January; looks interesting on that form but he's taken backward steps on his last two starts and is now 1-8 over hurdles.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he's finished down the field in last two starts; others preferred

runners.name: Celtic Art
runners.spotlight: Six-time hurdle winner who hit a personal best when scoring at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) in January but his form has slumped in two subsequent runs; has bit to prove again and this is competitive on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Hit a personal best when winning at Taunton in January but he's struggled since

runners.name: Authodidacte
runners.spotlight: 1m2f/1m4f wins in France and then bought for 33,000euros; won on hurdling/stable debut at Leicester (1m7f, heavy) in December 2024 and she returned from a year off with a dominant display in a handicap at Uttoxeter (2m, soft); 11lb higher after another break but that was a big step forward last time and she's open to more progress; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 over hurdles with a win at Uttoxeter in December; up 11lb but she's respected

runners.name: Cosmic Connection
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who scored on his hurdling/stable debut at Warwick last month and was a clear second under a penalty over C&D (good to soft) last week; looks on a workable mark now switched to a handicap and this unexposed 5yo is respected.
runners.comment: Won on hurdling debut before a clear second under penalty; respected on handicap debut

runners.name: The Kemble Brewery
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete over hurdles (pulled up) and fences (fell) in his last two starts but this lightly raced 7yo is still unexposed in handicaps and he won twice during his novice hurdle campaign; returns after 157 days off and he needs checking in market back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in last two starts but he needs checking in market back from a break

runners.name: Cornish Storm
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back handicaps at Taunton (2m3f, good) and Warwick (2m, heavy) during the winter but he flopped at Newbury in final run for Stuart Kittow; new headgear is now tried and he has a bit to prove on first run for new trainer.
runners.comment: Flopped in hat-trick bid at Newbury and has bit to prove for new trainer; new blinkers

runners.name: In The Air
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who did well over fences in the second half of last year, with three wins and two seconds; returns after six months off but he's only 2lb higher than for his last success and he has won when fresh in the past; needs close look back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Three chase wins in second half of last year; needs watching in market back over hurdles

runners.name: Lacrima
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins in France including three in a row at around 2m2f (soft/heavy) last autumn; unexposed over hurdles but he's hard to weigh up from a handicapping point of view and this looks a tough assignment on his British debut; blinkers are removed and market should guide after 164 days off.
runners.comment: Four chase wins in France but this looks a tough assignment on his British debut

runners.name: Zambezi Fix
runners.spotlight: All three hurdle wins have come over C&D but he failed to beat a rival here in December and was tailed off again at Ffos Las in January; overall record is now 4-48 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner but he's struggled in last two starts and overall record is 4-48

races.race_name: Tiptop Lavish Latrine Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Boola Boss
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-16 over fences and he defied today's mark with a 12l win in this race (good) last year; has not seen much action since but he returned after four months off with a creditable fourth at Newbury (2m6f, good to soft) last month; should benefit from that run and he's an interesting contender back at this track.
runners.comment: Won this race by 12l last year and was a good fourth at Newbury last month; respected

runners.name: Gris Majeur
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner who made the breakthrough over fences in a Ludlow handicap (2m4f, good to firm) in October; that was a four-runner race but he's been placed in three runs since including over 3m at Taunton; should go well again and it would be a positive if the ground dries out.
runners.comment: In-form 9yo who should go well again and it would be a positive if the ground dries out
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Camulus
runners.spotlight: Won his final two starts last season and he made it 3-8 over fences when completing a hat-trick over 3m1f at Wincanton (the vet reported he was treated for post-race ataxia) on his comeback in February; up another 4lb but he seems versatile ground-wise and is open to more progress as a staying chaser; key player.
runners.comment: Made it 3-8 over fences when completing a hat-trick on comeback at Wincanton; key player

runners.name: Buckna
runners.spotlight: Acts on good and soft ground; won two chase handicaps at Hereford (3m1f) late in 2025 but he's struggled in his last two starts; still has potential in this sphere but he needs to get back on track with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Two wins at Hereford late last year but he's struggled in last two runs; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Choice Runner
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in novice hurdles but he's been well held in three handicaps in that sphere; still has low-mileage but this is his first attempt beyond 2m4f and he needs a transformation on chase debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo but he needs a transformation at this new trip on chase debut

runners.name: The Newest One
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was runner-up at Warwick (3m, good to soft) on New Year's Eve but he's had a heavy defeat and a fall in two subsequent runs; new headgear is now tried but he's hard to predict and has not won since October 2024; down the list.
runners.comment: 11yo who is hard to predict and he's lost his way again in last two starts; down the list

runners.name: Happy And Fine
runners.spotlight: Scored at Warwick (3m, good to soft) in December but he's failed to complete in three subsequent runs and didn't get very far at Stratford (unseated at first) two weeks ago; only 2lb lower than for his last success but he comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Won at Warwick in December but he's failed to complete in three runs since; risks attached
runners.owner: L Cross, S Cross & J Fitzpatrick

runners.name: Sizable Sam
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is hard to predict but he may have had stamina issues when pulled up in the Devon National (3m7f) and he ran well over an inadequate 2m3f (good to soft) on his penultimate run; this trip is a much better fit and he's well handicapped on old form; not ruled out in new headgear.
runners.comment: Had excuse over 3m7f last time and has claims if he can bounce back; new headgear

races.race_name: Tiptop Sensational Shower Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Presenting Doy
runners.spotlight: Suffered her first reverse under rules when pulled up on reappearance (2m7f; reportedly made a respiratory noise); off three months (wind surgery) before Market Rasen (extended 2m4f, soft) in February when by no means disgraced in form terms, although that 10l ninth of 12 shows that improvement is needed if others give their running; dropped 2lb, 7lb claimer is recruited and the opposition have their own questions to answer.
runners.comment: By no means disgraced last time and the opposition have their own questions to answer

runners.name: Love Me
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m handicap in 2024 for Henry de Bromhead; back to form in November on first run for this yard and not discredited next time but, with tongue-tie fitted after wind surgery, she pulled up lame on chase debut in January and was well beaten when upped to an extended 2m3f three weeks ago; this trip is a big step into unknown territory.
runners.comment: Well beaten when upped to an extended 2m3f three weeks ago; goes even further today

runners.name: Katie's Melody
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner; four runs over hurdles, best when third at Wincanton (extended 2m5f, heavy) in January; handicap debut under 7lb claimer at Newbury (3m1f, soft; favourite) last time was disappointing but these are early days and she could bounce back.
runners.comment: Handicap debut under 7lb claimer at Newbury (3m1f, soft; favourite) was disappointing

runners.name: I Am The Moon
runners.spotlight: Chasing didn't go to plan before she won readily when back over hurdles at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) in February; ran badly, however, at Ludlow (3m but not because of the trip) three weeks later; ran respectably at 3m on final start last term.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster (2m3f, soft); ran badly at Ludlow (3m but not because of the trip) latest

runners.name: Harmony Heights
runners.spotlight: Staying-on second on handicap debut at Lingfield (2m7f, soft) in December; flopped in five-runner race at Newbury (3m1f, soft) two months later but had first-time cheekpieces which are now absent; seriously considered on the Lingfield effort but return to better ground asks a question.
runners.comment: Staying-on 2nd on handicap debut at Lingfield (2m7f, soft); flop at Newbury (3m1f, soft)
runners.owner: M Songer, C Bailey & M Dodd

runners.name: Benignitas
runners.spotlight: Sent chasing on last four starts, with two minor honours over the extended 2m3f at Ffos Las; however, moving up to this trip here with cheekpieces restored (she tried them twice in 2020) on latest outing did not work out and she is now 0-16 under rules.
runners.comment: Back from chasing but with stamina unproven and she is now 0-16 under rules

runners.name: Shan't Wait
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but he went close in a Southwell handicap (2m, good to soft) in December; was never really travelling when a well-held third at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) last month but that was after a short break and cheekpieces are now added; dangerous if he can get back near best.
runners.comment: Didn't fire at Sedgefield but has claims if he can get back near best; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Rapid Mission
runners.spotlight: Flat winner but he's 0-9 over hurdles and has been beaten 17l or more in four handicaps (2m1f-3m) since November; mark continues to fall but he needs the cheekpieces to make a difference on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles and well held in handicaps in his last four runs; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Thorgath
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was a good second on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) in January and he backed that up with a close fourth over C&D the following month; up another 2lb but he travelled strongly for a long way last time and is open to more progress; strongly respected with tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Two good efforts in handicaps including a close fourth over C&D; respected

runners.name: Hillwalk
runners.spotlight: Placed in both bumpers and he showed some promise in the first two of his three hurdle runs (2m4f/2m) during the winter; returns after a short break and he needs a close look in the market on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo and he needs a close look in the market on handicap debut

runners.name: Inion Tiogair
runners.spotlight: 9yo who is exposed over hurdles but she scored over C&D (soft/good to soft) in two of her last three starts; 3lb higher than for her last win two weeks ago but she remains feasibly treated and should get a decent tow into this race; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Has won over C&D in two of her last three runs and she remains feasibly treated; respected

runners.name: Sophie Power
runners.spotlight: Won by a nose at Leicester in November (1m7f, soft) but she's struggled in three subsequent runs and was pulled up at Ffos Las last month; had another wind operation since but she needs a major revival back up in trip; hood and tongue-tie are removed and cheekpieces are now tried.
runners.comment: Has lost his way since his win at Leicester and he needs a major revival in new headgear

runners.name: Bushstreet Bowman
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in his two competed starts in novice events and was pulled up on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, soft; 12-1) in February; still early days but he needs a transformation back up in trip.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Huntingdon and he needs a transformation back up in trip
runners.owner: Ben Pauling Racing & Dr Suzannah Turk

runners.name: Peace In The Park
runners.spotlight: Promising second in a Huntingdon handicap (2m, heavy) in February but he failed to back that up at Stratford (2m, good) last month; still has low mileage but he's untried at this trip and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 6yo but he's untried at this trip and others are more convincing

runners.name: Raindance Queen
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden; big prices and well beaten in four starts since November including in a hurdle handicap at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) last month; out of the weights again and can only be watched.
runners.comment: 19-race maiden and he's struggled in his last five starts; 4lb out of the weights

runners.name: Saint Vic
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent ten-race maiden who was well held in a Huntingdon handicap (2m4f, good) last April and has been on the sidelines since; best watched back from a year off.
runners.comment: Inconsistent ten-race maiden and he's best watched back from a year off

runners.name: Ifan Full Pelt
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo but he struggled in his qualifying runs and was pulled up at another big price on handicap debut at Warwick (2m3f, good to soft) in January.
runners.comment: Has struggled in all five runs including when pulled up on handicap debut at Warwick

runners.name: D'Gesteevo
runners.spotlight: No form claims after eight hurdle races, five in handicaps; tongue tied on latest and tried cheekpieces before that; 1lb out of the weights
runners.comment: No form claims after eight hurdle races and it's not hard to look elsewhere

runners.name: Triple Haych
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point but has achieved very little over hurdles (2m-2m3f) and he fell on handicap debut at Ludlow (2m5f, good; 100-1) last month; can only be watched until he shows some promising signs.
runners.comment: Has achieved very little under rules and he fell on handicap debut last month; opposable

races.race_name: QuinnBet Conditional Jockeys' Training Series Mares' Handicap Hurdle (Part Of The Racing Excellence)

runners.name: It's Maisy
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence but she is holding her form well, third of seven in 3m3f Sedgefield handicap hurdle 23 days ago; a bold showing is on the cards off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good third at Sedgefield 23 days ago; bold showing is on the cards off the same mark here

runners.name: Seasmoke
runners.spotlight: Scored over fences at Market Rasen (3m) in November but she's failed to go on and underwent breathing surgery (also fitted with cheekpieces) before coming in a well-held third of five at Exeter (3m) 20 days ago; reverts to hurdles with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Scored over fences at Market Rasen in November but has failed to go on; reverts to hurdles

runners.name: Strathcatcha
runners.spotlight: Seems of very limited ability and she was pulled up in 2m4f Hexham novice hurdle 17 days ago; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Seems of very limited ability and she was pulled up in Hexham novice hurdle 17 days ago
runners.owner: M Waterton, D Amos, G Rutherford

runners.name: Royal Maggs
runners.spotlight: A two-time points scorer who posted easily her best effort in this sphere when second of eight in 2m4f novice here 11 days ago; this longer trip promises to suit now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Best effort when second here 11 days ago; this longer trip promises to suit now h'capping
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing, S Knowles & Partners

races.race_name: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Chase

runners.name: Empty Nest
runners.spotlight: Usually goes well over C&D - form figures 212 - that win gained from a 2lb higher mark (favourite) over a year ago; recent Wetherby efforts were not so good but there was plenty to admire about the manner in which he rallied behind Always A Reason over C&D (good to soft) in January.
runners.comment: Recent Wetherby efforts were modest but C&D form figures 212; competitively handicapped.
runners.owner: M Blades, S Morrell, K Hathaway

runners.name: Bravethewaves
runners.spotlight: Seized the initiative, already having the race in safe keeping when left clear at the second last, at Doncaster (2m3f, good; from 9lb lower) in February; well beaten at Southwell subsequently and likely to face competition for the lead here; close to a career best required.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster (2m3f; from 9lb lower) in February; likely to face competition for lead.

runners.name: Hawkseye View
runners.spotlight: Made it 12th-time lucky over fences when scoring over C&D (good to soft; from 2lb lower) three starts back; excuses since - fell, and then out of depth in a valuable Class 2 Series Final at Carlisle; could bounce back with Danny McMenamin returning to the saddle.
runners.comment: C&D winner (from 2lb lower) three starts back; Danny McMenanmin returns to the saddle.

runners.name: The Clonmel Kid
runners.spotlight: Failed to win over hurdles (0-7), going closest when runner-up in a Hexham maiden (2m4f) 13 months ago; respectable on recent chase debut at Wetherby (3m; fourth of nine); 2lb lower, and back in trip with cheekpieces retained.
runners.comment: Respectable on recent rules chase debut at Wetherby (3m; fourth); 2lb lower; back in trip.

runners.name: Kingston Rock
runners.spotlight: 0-19; form figures PPP4 since November are hardly a ringing endorsement; problematic at the start, but did shape a bit better when a never-nearer fourth to Ceowulf over C&D (good to soft; 28-1) 11 days ago; 3lb out of weights; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-19; bit better, a never-nearer fourth over C&D (good to soft; 28-1) latest; 3lb wrong.
runners.owner: J L And Mrs H R Gledson

runners.name: Quickasican
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability over hurdles for Rebecca Menzies in 2022; lightly raced since, failing to complete in two attempts over fences (PF) for this trainer; monitor only for time being.
runners.comment: Signs of ability over hurdles in 2022; has failed to complete in two attempts over fences.

races.race_name: John Hanley Forever Lucky Number 7 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Camarrate
runners.spotlight: Winless since May 2024 and he's not proving easy to catch right this season, a below-par sixth in 2m3f Carlisle handicap hurdle 20 days ago.
runners.comment: Winless since May 2024 and he's not proving easy to catch right this season

runners.name: Valtteri Its James
runners.spotlight: A winning Irish pointer; has been brought along steadily in this sphere, coming in a never-dangerous seventh in 2m1f novice here four weeks ago; goes up in trip now handicapping and is capable of better.
runners.comment: Winning pointer; brought along steadily and capable of better up in trip for h'cap debut

runners.name: Cher Tara
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence but in good form for her current yard until coming in a never-dangerous sixth in 2m4f Musselburgh handicap 22 days ago; she can bounce back off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: In good form until sixth at Musselburgh 22 days ago; can bounce back off a slipping mark

runners.name: Abbey Scope
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful debut bumper winner; matched that form in this sphere when runner-up on his handicap debut at Catterick (2m3f) in January but he was pulled up at Musselburgh (2m4f) last time; has something to prove.
runners.comment: Runner-up on at Catterick but pulled up at Musselburgh last time; has something to prove

runners.name: Toby Two Scoops
runners.spotlight: Still to register a victory but he comes here after a good second of eight in 2m Wetherby handicap hurdle 44 days ago; he can make his presence felt again reverted to a longer trip.
runners.comment: Still to win but good Wetherby second latest; can make presence felt back up in trip

runners.name: National Question
runners.spotlight: Got back on the up with a third success of the season in 2m1f handicap at Carlisle 20 days ago; up another 4lb but this course winner is a likely player once more for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Back on the up with success at Carlisle latest; this course winner is a likely player

runners.name: Largy Meadow
runners.spotlight: Debut bumper second (for Stuart Crawford) in Ireland but he's failed to kick on for current yard in four runs over hurdles, undergoing wind surgery before falling two out (well held) in 2m4f Ayr handicap last month.
runners.comment: Debut bumper second for Stuart Crawford but he's failed to kick on for his current yard

runners.name: Long Time Listener
runners.spotlight: A point bumper winner in 2024; only poor form shown over hurdles but he posted his best run of the season (in first-time cheekpieces) when fourth of 11 in 2m4f Sedgefield handicap 23 days ago; needs to build on it in retained headgear.
runners.comment: Best run this term (in cheekpieces) when Sedgefield fourth latest; needs to build on it

runners.name: Lone Star
runners.spotlight: Scored at Market Rasen (on good ground) last June and she returned from a seven-month absence with a very good third of nine over C&D 11 days ago; this C&D winner can go very well off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back from seven months off with very good C&D third latest; can go very well

runners.name: Noble George
runners.spotlight: Twice a runner-up in bumpers; failed to build on earlier promise in this sphere when pulled up on his handicap debut at Bangor (2m4f) three weeks ago; cheekpieces are retained and he could still do better for good yard.
runners.comment: Pulled up on at Bangor three weeks ago; he could still do better for good yard

runners.name: Achnamara
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over hurdles (at about 2m4f) in 2024-25 but has failed to fire this term and beat just two in 2m4f Sedgefield handicap hurdle ten days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Failed to fire this term and beat just two at Sedgefield ten days ago; others appeal more

runners.name: Diamond Trilogy
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in a handful of runs for Dianne Sayer this term, pulled up over C&D in January; tongue tied for his new yard with lots to prove after a break.
runners.comment: Offered little for Dianne Sayer this term; tongue tied for new yard with lots to prove

runners.name: The Final Brig
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in his four runs over hurdles, pulled up in C&D novice in January; cheekpieces go on for his handicap debut after a break.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in his four runs over hurdles; cheekpieces go on for his handicap debut
runners.owner: 8 Amigos, J Grindal, W Hamilton, J Burns

races.race_name: Acorn Catering Equipment Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Special Rate
runners.spotlight: With Phil Kirby when left alone in a match at Kelso (3m, soft) in February 2024, that being his fourth (and most recent) win over fences; runner-up in this last year (beaten 11l) for Lizzie Quinlan; in the frame three times (Musselburgh) for Hugh Burns; competes from an easing mark, needing more.
runners.comment: In frame three times (Musselburgh) for H. Burns; competes from easing mark, needing more.

runners.name: Geryville
runners.spotlight: 7-52; enjoyed the run of the race, making all, at Wetherby (2m4f, soft; 18-1; from 6lb lower) on penultimate start; ran to a similar level (third) behind the newly blinkered Rath Gaul Hill back there subsequently; no issue with Newcastle; player with Alice Stevens claiming 3lb.
runners.comment: Wetherby winner (2m4f) penultimate start; respectable third since; no issue with Newcastle

runners.name: Parisencore
runners.spotlight: 21 months since his last win, an authoritative one in the Perth Silver Cup (2m4f, good to firm; from 5lb higher); beaten 26l, 16l and 14l respectively in three runs since April 2025; good ground is ideal, so probably worth considering with his trainer in such fine form.
runners.comment: Authoritative winner of 2024 Perth Silver Cup; lacklustre since but stable in hot form
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

runners.name: Hello Judge
runners.spotlight: Carlisle specialist; sits close to a career-high mark following a gritty win there (2m, good to soft) in February; "never travelling and jumped poorly" in the Monet's Garden Series Final there three weeks ago; bit to prove for the time being, even acknowledging a 2024 C&D win.
runners.comment: Carlisle specialist (close to a career-high); "never travelling and jumped poorly" latest.

runners.name: Glory And Honour
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Hexham (2m, good; in added blinkers) last June; that was in a lower-grade contest (Class 4) and his productivity ebbed away thereafter (0-5; hurdles and fences); bit to prove off a 190-day absence; likely to draw benefit from this comeback run.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Hexham (2m, good; in blinkers) last June; fortunes have dipped since.

runners.name: Filibustering
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on since making winning hurdles/stable debut at Kelso (2m, juvenile) in March 2025, failing to see out races at Doncaster, Kelso, and Ludlow; hopes are pinned on a revival with an added tongue-tie; trainer is 5-11 (45%) over jumps here in the last five years.
runners.comment: Struggled since winning hurdles/stable debut at Kelso (2m) in March 2025; tongue-tie on.

runners.name: As Fast As Wind
runners.spotlight: Ready Kempton winner (2m novices' handicap, good) for Daisy Hitchins last October; consistent for Tristan Davidson (366233), giving her best when a close third in the Sea Pigeon Series Final at Musselburgh (2m1f, good to soft) recently; 2lb higher but bang there with repeat.
runners.comment: Consistent for T. Davidson (366233); close third at Musselburgh (2m1f) latest; 2lb higher.

runners.name: Double Digits
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Sedgefield (2m; second chase start) in February but no excuses when runner-up there (beaten 19l) a few weeks later; returns to hurdles (2-7) from a slightly lower mark, possessing C&D experience (beaten a nose in a maiden here in March 2025); possible.
runners.comment: Runner-up over fences at Sedgefield (2m) in February; slightly lower mark over hurdles.

runners.name: Thank You Blue
runners.spotlight: Weakened when third of five (13-8 favourite) in this last year; back to form, completing a February hat-trick (Sedgefield twice and Ludlow, rising steadily in the weights); reportedly "ran flat" when pulling up at Ayr (2m5f, soft; from 5lb lower) latest; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Third in this last year; the winning run came to an abrupt end at Ayr (2m5f, soft) latest.

runners.name: Inappropriate
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m juvenile contest here in November 2024, and added to his tally in a Series Final at Musselburgh (Class 2) the following spring; twice runner-up on the Flat here (1m2f) this winter, but well beaten over hurdles at Fakenham (2m) one month ago; others more obvious.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up on the Flat here this winter; well beaten over hurdles at Fakenham latest.

runners.name: Bust A Move
runners.spotlight: Justified market support to make winning handicap debut over C&D (good to soft; from 4lb lower) in February 2025; failed to excel in a hood at Wetherby or Huntingdon (November/December); returns from a four-month break on better ground, which holds few fears; merits respect.
runners.comment: C&D winner on handicap debut (4lb lower) in Feb 2025; no luck since (0-2); merits respect.

races.race_name: QuinnBet Extra Places Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lost Frequencies
runners.spotlight: Hexham bumper winner for Stuart Coltherd; sets the benchmark, runner-up at Wetherby and Kelso (both 2m) in late 2024; marginally out of depth in The French Furze here (Class 2; 2m6f) in November; returns to a more appropriate level and is tongue-tied on first start for Sam England.
runners.comment: Sets the benchmark; out of his depth in The French Furze here (Class 2; 2m6f) in November.

runners.name: Diamond Koda
runners.spotlight: Accounted for Peckforton Hills when getting off the mark over hurdles at the 12th attempt at Doncaster (2m4f, Class 5 conditionals'; good to soft) in February; down the field (from 8lb higher) in a stronger contest at Newbury subsequently; now drops back into a more suitable looking novice.
runners.comment: Made breakthrough at Doncaster (2m4f, Class 5) in February; beaten since; back in a novice

runners.name: Dunkerque
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden winner (2m1f, good) for Gavin Cromwell last July; low-key in three starts for this trainer, beaten 7l when runner-up to a long odds-on favourite at Sedgefield (2m4f) in February; others more convincing.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe maiden winner (2m1f) for G. Cromwell; low-key in three starts for this trainer.

runners.name: Hashtag Queen
runners.spotlight: Regressive form in bumpers; fitted with a hood and tailed off on hurdles debut at Wetherby (2m maiden, good; 250-1) a fortnight ago; early days but she's still the first to discount.
runners.comment: Tailed off on hurdles debut at Wetherby (2m maiden; 250-1) a fortnight ago; discounted.

runners.name: Port Station
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper form and has shaped okay since introduced to hurdles (323); lacked the pace to stake a claim when third to Lewisham Grove in a C&D maiden (beaten approximately 10l) in January; is one to consider in this winnable novice.
runners.comment: Lacked for pace when third in C&D maiden (beaten approximately 10l) in January; possible.

runners.name: Dotties Promise
runners.spotlight: Justified market support, making all in a Kelso bumper (2m, good; 13-8 favourite) last September; jumped poorly on hurdles debut at Catterick (2m) on New Year's Day, and well beaten back there (2m4f) a few weeks later; difficult to retain the faith.
runners.comment: Kelso bumper winner (2m; 13-8 fav) last September; well beaten both attempts over hurdles.
runners.owner: Castle View Racing & W F Corbett

runners.name: Executive Producer
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 3m point (October 2024); ran freely, well beaten, in both bumpers (Musselburgh and Haydock); needs to settle better - no obvious signs of the hood and tongue-tie having the desired effect - to be competitive on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Well held in two bumpers (Musselburgh and Haydock); needs to settle better; hurdles debut.

races.race_name: High Gosforth Park Golf Club Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Midday Rendezvous
runners.spotlight: 9-1, made a winning debut in 12-runner bumper over C&D four weeks ago, overcoming greenness to get up on the post; saddled with a 7lb penalty but has more to offer for yard with fine bumper record this year; player.
runners.comment: Winning debut over C&D; saddles 7lb penalty but looked green there and he's a player

runners.name: Doubleosue
runners.spotlight: Jet Away mare who was purchased for £50,000 after landing Irish point on her debut in January; a noteworthy newcomer for good yard.
runners.comment: Jet Away mare who landed Irish point in January; a noteworthy newcomer for good yard

runners.name: Little Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Sans Frontieres mare; dam, 9.4f Flat winner (RPR 64), is a half-sister to useful 1m/1m1f winner Treasurer; others appeal more on this occasion.
runners.comment: Sans Frontieres mare; others appeal more on this occasion

runners.name: Attin
runners.spotlight: 56,000euros 3yo; No Risk At All gelding; half-brother to winners Akiria (Polish 5f 2yo/6f) and Alchimie (French 2m2f hurdle); dam, German 7f 2yo/French 7.5f winner, is a half-sister to winners Alianthus (German 1m Group 2) and Apokalypse (Listed-placed 7f/7.5f); a considered newcomer, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: No Risk At All gelding; considered newcomer, especially if the market vibes are positive
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

runners.name: Marquis
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros 3yo; Son of Sea The Moon who is a brother to 1m6f-2m Flat (RPR 97) and 2m4f/2m7f hurdle winner Moon King and also a half-brother to French 1m4f-2m winner Marelie; unraced dam is closely related to Group-placed 1m winner Maid to Remember; a rare bumper runner for this yard and not out of things, particularly if the betting speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Rare runner for yard and not out of things, particularly if betting speaks in his favour

runners.name: Regal Thunder
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding who fetched 45,000 gns as a 3yo and was placed in Irish points either side of Christmas; possibilities on his Rules debut.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding; placed in Irish points either side of Christmas; possibilities

runners.name: Yuka Lele
runners.spotlight: Lauro gelding; dam, unraced sister to 2m4f-2m7f hurdle/chase winner Shesupincourt, is a half-sister to useful winners First Drift (bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Destroyer Deployed (bumper/2m4f hurdle/3m chase); the market can guide.
runners.comment: The market can guide for this Lauro debutant

runners.name: Carlungie
runners.spotlight: Arrigo gelding; dam, unplaced on the Flat and over jumps, is a half-sister to winners Malystic (bumper and smart 1m7f-2m1f hurdle/chase) and Misty Mani (2m-2m3f hurdle).
runners.comment: Arrigo gelding; the betting can guide for this newcomer
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Strong Man Tom
runners.spotlight: 25-1, last of nine in bumper at Hereford 17 days ago; this son of Massaat needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Last of nine in bumper at Hereford; son of Massaat needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Teddie
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and hooded/tongue tied, went too freely when a remote sixth of nine in bumper at Catterick on his debut in January; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Too free when remote sixth in bumper at Catterick; significantly more is required

runners.name: Credit Union
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a pair of C&D bumpers either side of Christmas for Rebecca Menzies; lots more required for his new yard after a break.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in a pair of C&D bumpers either side of Christmas for Rebecca Menzies

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Karrygrant
runners.spotlight: Excellent effort on debut at Leopardstown (7f, yielding to soft) in October when beaten 0.75l into second; raced keenly and in front over 1f out when run out of things late on at Navan (1m2f, heavy) 12 days ago, finishing third; speedily-bred so big player down in trip.
runners.comment: Good debut second; keen when third over 1m2f at Navan; big player down in trip

runners.name: Templenoe
runners.spotlight: From the family of Classic winner Tarascon; started slowly in 7f Galway maiden on only start at two; didn't see out the mile in testing conditions when sixth of 23 at the Curragh on return; solid fifth of 13 there over 7f on latest; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Some okay form in big fields to date but might be one for handicaps in due course

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Third foal; 1,500euros yearling; half-sister to 5f-7f winner Silver Service (inc 2yo; RPR 94); dam 6f winner (73), out of French Listed-placed 1m winner; hooded; best watched.
runners.comment: 1,500euros yearling; half-sister to 5f-7f winner Silver Service (inc 2yo; RPR 94)

runners.name: New Radical
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree and encouraging debut third behind a potentially smart type at Dundalk (1m) in February; raced freely on that occasion and will have to take a step forward to take this.
runners.comment: Third behind a potentially smart type on debut at Dundalk; will need to improve but could

runners.name: Gold Inn
runners.spotlight: Ran out of steam in closing stages both times Dundalk over 7f; bred for sprint trips but stable in fine fettle at present; market can guide on turf debut.
runners.comment: Okay AW runs; bred for sprint trips but stable in fine fettle at present; market can guide

runners.name: Prince Andorra
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase but outran 150-1 odds when seventh of 14 on 1m Dundalk debut last month, beaten around 7l after a slow break; stable among the winners and he could run a big race if he gets away on terms.
runners.comment: Outran huge odds at Dundalk on debut after a slow break; could run a big race

runners.name: Queen Earthlight
runners.spotlight: Minor promise on Gowran debut (8.5f, heavy) in October but failed to build on that over 7f at Dundalk in January; still early days for top stable but would need to see market support to be of interest.
runners.comment: Still early days for top stable but would need to see market support to be of interest

runners.name: Emily's Boy
runners.spotlight: Lost ground at the start when finishing last and only 6-1 for Dundalk debut; well beaten at that track and the Curragh since; hooded now.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing at Dundalk and the Curragh in three starts; hood goes on now

runners.name: Halon Bay
runners.spotlight: Sent off 150-1 and made no impression on Curragh debut a fortnight ago; will need more time; gelded.
runners.comment: Sent off 150-1 and no impression on Curragh debut a fortnight ago; needs more time; gelded

runners.name: Perggosa
runners.spotlight: Big prices and never involved in two AW maidens; safe to rule out on this occasion.
runners.comment: Big prices and never involved in two AW maidens; safe to rule out on this occasion

runners.name: Dixie Doodle
runners.spotlight: Tailed off last on Cork debut over 5f last summer; speedily-bred filly so this trip is a big doubt.
runners.comment: Tailed off last on Cork debut over 5f last summer; speedily-bred so trip is a big doubt

runners.name: Elora Prince
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten all four nursery runs; best of two Dundalk runs was on latest when fourth over 1m, one place behind Personified; blinkers now; each-way candidate if he can take a step forward.
runners.comment: One place behind Personified latest; each-way candidate if he can take a step forward

runners.name: Elly Bay
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all four maidens, including when runner-up over C&D last July; disappointing in two handicaps when at the head of the market; hooded on return; first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Second in C&D maiden last July; disappointing in two handicaps; new yard; hooded

runners.name: Smart Sioux
runners.spotlight: Best run when staying-on sixth in Naas fillies' maiden over 5f last July but beaten out of sight in four handicaps since; upped in trip now.
runners.comment: 6th in Naas fillies' maiden over 5f last July but beaten out of sight in 4 handicaps since

runners.name: The Love Machine
runners.spotlight: Navan winner off 61 in October for Mick Mulvany (6f, yielding); pair of no-shows since joining this yard; looked a non-stayer in two 7f attempts; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Pair of no-shows since joining this yard; looked a non-stayer in two 7f attempts

runners.name: Spirit Honour
runners.spotlight: Three AW runs at Dundalk; ran green on debut; distant runner-up behind warm favourite on second start; weak in the betting when well beaten last time; market will reveal expectations on turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability first two AW starts but below that last time; turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Personified
runners.spotlight: Some okay efforts in three Dundalk maidens before creditable third there on handicap debut last month; half-sister to Dewhurst winner Beethoven could prove a different proposition now on turf; respected.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Dewhurst winner Beethoven could prove a different proposition now on turf
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; ran well on first two nursery outings last year; never a factor on 1m2f return at Navan; beaten 1.75l into fourth in Gowran claimer (1m, soft) on Wednesday; market can separate yard's runners.
runners.comment: Fourth in Gowran claimer (1m, soft) on Wednesday; market can separate yard's runners

runners.name: Gold Find
runners.spotlight: Ran three times at Dundalk at two; dwelt at start when down the field on turf/handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, heavy) after being gelded 12 days ago; blinkers are reached for now.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown a great deal in maidens or a handicap; blinkers go on now

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a whole lot in three maidens but well-backed for handicap debut at Gowran on Wednesday when respectable 2.5l fourth of 10 (7f, soft); turned out quickly off same mark; in the mix.
runners.comment: Well-backed for handicap debut at Gowran on Wednesday when respectable 2.5l fourth

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in defeat in seven turf runs before running no race on AW debut at Dundalk in October; handles an ease in the ground and was second off current mark at Sligo last summer; first try at 1m but should stay.
runners.comment: Handles an ease in the ground and was second off current mark at Sligo last summer

runners.name: Dream Legend
runners.spotlight: Down the field in turf/AW maidens last year; best handicap effort in November when not beaten far into sixth at Dundalk (6f); fly-leapt the stalls on Dundalk return ten days ago so that effort easily forgiven, but the jury is out up in trip; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Fly-leapt the stalls on Dundalk return so that effort easily forgiven but the jury is out
runners.owner: E D Delany & Mrs Lisa Delany

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; best run when third on nursery debut over 5f at Naas in September but way below that since; needs to get back on track up in trip; seasonal debut; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Third on nursery debut over 5f at Naas in September but way below that since; 4lb wrong

runners.name: Dervo Annie
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; long odds and soundly beaten in trio of maidens at Navan/Dundalk over 5f-7f; market will provide clues on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in trio of maidens at Navan/Dundalk over 5f-7f; market will provide clues

runners.name: Mehman
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner is 1-19 on turf; is 2lb below that last turf win which came at Naas (5f, good) in May 2024; handles an ease but this trip may just stretch his stamina.
runners.comment: 2lb below sole turf win, handles an ease, this trip may just stretch his stamina

runners.name: Summer Island
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner in February off 6lb lower on second start for this stable; has run well on good to soft so should handle conditions, but came up short off revised mark at Dundalk latest so a bit to prove off this rating.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower penultimate, beaten off revised mark latest, bit to prove back on turf
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson

runners.name: Bay Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year, including once on turf at Nottingham (8.5f, good) off 9lb lower; picked up at the sales in October for 10,000gns and worth a market check on first start for Ciaran Murphy.
runners.comment: Two-time winner in Britain, bought for 10,000gns in Oct', check market

runners.name: Lahore Da King
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner is 0-5 on turf; fairly well beaten in both turf handicap runs on easy ground and is 10lb above his last AW win; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Two-time AW winner, below best on previous turf runs on easy ground, others preferred

runners.name: Giselles Defence
runners.spotlight: Won four times for Ian Williams on a variety of ground, all at 1m2f; well treated on that form but he's failed to beat a rival home in four runs for this stable; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Well treated on best British form, last in four runs for this yard, hard to fancy

runners.name: Selective Power
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner who appreciates an ease in the ground; 5lb above his last success in September at Roscommon (7.5f, soft) but has run well here before and had a recent run at Dundalk so he'll be fit; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner, likes an ease, has had a spin at Dundalk, each-way chance

runners.name: Uncle Albert
runners.spotlight: No success since winning well at Cork (7f, heavy) in March 2024; shaped well at Down Royal last May and 6f was too sharp on first run back at Navan last month; on a dangerous mark if rediscovers best with blinkers back on.
runners.comment: No joy since 2024 Cork win, 6f too sharp latest, on dangerous mark if rediscovers best

runners.name: Kevin The Great
runners.spotlight: 0-8 and yet to trouble the judge but has shown glimmers of ability; hinted more than once last season that he needed further than 1m2f but weakened over that trip on return at Navan last month; blinkers now go on, but this trip is a query.
runners.comment: 0-8, hinted he needed further than 1m2f last season, drop in trip a query here; blinkered

runners.name: Kakori
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with a promising run behind this year's Irish Lincoln winner Ribee at Gowran (7f, heavy); had an excuse when bombing out at the Curragh on seasonal return when the 9-2 favourite (grazed the inside and outside left hock); capable of better.
runners.comment: Good effort on h'cap debut last Oct', excuses for poor run on return, capable of better

runners.name: Goldrush Kid
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb higher in first-time cheekpieces at the Curragh last March (1m, soft); no joy since and beaten around 9l in both starts at that venue this season; blinkers back on instead of cheekpieces; not discounted.
runners.comment: 6lb below last win, beaten over 9l in two runs this term, not dismissed

runners.name: Romantic War
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-15 on turf; has placed twice with one of those efforts a 5.25l third over C&D (soft) last October; 9lb out of the handicap and might need this run after 155 days off.
runners.comment: Maiden, placed over C&D last October, might need this run after break

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut off 6lb lower at Thurles last October; backed that up with a decent third at Leopardstown  (7f, heavy) later that month and another solid run at Dundalk (10.5f) in December; returns with yard in red-hot form and ground fine; chance.
runners.comment: Consistent in handicaps, ran well when last seen at Dundalk in Dec', yard in hot form

runners.name: Lohengrin
runners.spotlight: Gained breakthrough win on the beach at Laytown (7f) last September; below best in two spins over hurdles and over 10.5f at Dundalk in three starts since; off since December and best form is on good ground.
runners.comment: Won at Laytown in Sept', poor form since, off since Dec', best turf form on good ground

runners.name: Suerte
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden is 0-5 on the grass and yet to finish in the first three; best run came at Dundalk and hopes are pinned on the new headgear combination sparking improvement.
runners.comment: Maiden is 0-5 on turf, best run came on AW, new headgear needs to spark something

runners.name: Alnofoor
runners.spotlight: Return to turf saw him shed the maiden tag in decisive fashion at Navan (6f, heavy) last month; 9lb rise partially negated by rider's 3lb claim; should be fine dropped to 5f but draw could have been kinder.
runners.comment: 6f win at Navan latest (heavy), up 9lb but rider takes off 3lb, draw could've been kinder

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: First reserve; three-time winner in Britain, including twice on turf, but last success came in August 2023; is 8lb below that last win and should strip fitter for his stable debut at Navan; could go well from good draw for local yard if gets a run.
runners.comment: Three-time winner in Britain, well treated, sharper for latest, could go well; reserve

runners.name: Steel Magnolia
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; C&D winner off 1lb higher in October (Only Spoofing second); below best last few runs at Dundalk and has been off since December but has gone well fresh before; can't discount in first-time visor.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 1lb higher in Oct', off since Dec', has gone well fresh, visor goes on

runners.name: Art Of Unity
runners.spotlight: Veteran is 5-60 on turf; best at 5f and had a few of these behind when winning over C&D (soft) after a break in October (subsequently disqualified); well beaten at Navan when last seen and probably best watched on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner on soft, well beaten when last seen at Navan, watch on debut for new yard

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner but hasn't got his head in front since September 2022; no signs of the drought ending in recent starts and this trip on the sharp side; others preferred.
runners.comment: On losing run, no sign of drought ending in recent starts, trip a query, others preferred

runners.name: Jordan Breeze
runners.spotlight: AW winner ran a fine race from a wide draw on seasonal return at Dundalk (5f) 10 days ago (Jazzit 0.75l behind); handles an ease and should go well again off the same mark.
runners.comment: AW winner, good run on return at Dundalk from poor draw, handles an ease, go well

runners.name: Girl Is On Fire
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in three AW maidens at 6f-7f for Natalia Lupini; off since December and probably best watched down in trip on stable, turf and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Mild promise in AW maidens, best watched down in trip on stable, turf and h'cap debut

runners.name: Only Spoofing
runners.spotlight: Likeable veteran won for the fourteenth time at Musselburgh (5f, good) last April and is 13lb lower now; showed some spark when a 2l fourth at Dundalk on penultimate and was third over this C&D (soft) off 5lb higher in October; can't discount.
runners.comment: Veteran, 13lb below last win, has C&D form, ground fine, can't discount

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; maiden is 0-7 on turf but he has run well in defeat (including over C&D); pleasing return at Navan (6f, heavy) 12 days ago behind Alnofoor and will be suited by the drop back to 5f here; player off just 2lb higher.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf, has run well over C&D, pleasing return latest, drop to 5f a plus, player

runners.name: Never Shout Never
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-42 (1-28 on turf); didn't look like a winner in waiting during a winter AW campaign and below-par again on return to the turf at Navan (6f, heavy); 5f more suitable but needs a revival.
runners.comment: 2-42, out of form during AW winter campaign, below-par turf return latest, revival needed

runners.name: Iryna's Star
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on second handicap start at Dundalk (7f) in January but hasn't replicated that since; weakened over 5f on turf debut at Naas latest and 4lb drop is not enough to tempt (1lb wrong).
runners.comment: Best run on AW (7f), weakened over 5f on turf bow latest, 1lb wrong, others preferred

runners.name: Platino Bianco
runners.spotlight: AW winner off 13lb lower; hiked up 12lb for close second in Dundalk claimer in February; struggled off revised rating on return to turf at Navan latest; has run well here before but may be on tough mark for now.
runners.comment: AW winner off 13lb lower, struggled on return to turf at Navan, may be on tough mark

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: 0-8 with all starts coming on AW; best run since joining this yard on latest when third at Dundalk; closely matched with Jordan Breeze on that run but drawn widest of all here for turf debut.
runners.comment: 0-8 on AW, not far behind Jordan Breeze latest, drawn widest for turf debut

runners.name: Glen Breeze
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown much in a couple of 7f Curragh maidens and in a 7f claimer at Dundalk; can only be watched dropped back to 5f in first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens and a claimer, cheekpieces on for h'cap debut, watch

runners.name: She's A Gift
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; maiden is 0-7 on turf; best efforts have come on the AW and has struggled on four previous starts on easy ground; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf, best form on AW, has struggled previous runs on easy ground; reserve

runners.name: Simmering Seas
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form but has shaped promisingly enough in first two handicaps; hampered and clipped heels when a 4.75l sixth on seasonal return and stable debut at Naas (5f, soft); same mark here and nicely drawn; interesting.
runners.comment: Modest maiden, promise in both handicaps, trouble in running latest, interesting
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; on losing run since a C&D win on heavy in April 2023; poor form last season but signs of life on reappearance at Navan last month (6f, heavy); on dangerous mark if building on that.
runners.comment: C&D winner, signs of life on reappearance at Navan, on dangerous mark; reserve

runners.name: Bay Of Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Made all for Navan nursery win (5f, soft) last September; good third there next time from 6lb higher; led most of the way when 0.75l third at Naas (5f, soft) on return last month; good chance in receipt of 3yo allowance with the help of rider's 5lb claim.
runners.comment: Front-runner should be hard to beat receiving 3yo allowance and in top order of late
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Sporting Hero
runners.spotlight: Excellent 9-27 return on AW; 0-17 on turf; seven wins at Dundalk, most recently on yard debut over 6f last month when making all off 75; failed to back that up there ten days ago; races from 8lb lower turf mark plus assistance of 10lb claimer but percentage call is to oppose.
runners.comment: 9-27 return on AW; 0-17 on turf; 8lb lower turf mark but percentage call is to oppose

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: Won Navan maiden (5f, yielding) last August and wasn't beaten far next three starts at 5f/6f, the latter two at Dundalk; looked in need of the run on return at Cork on soft last week; ground will suit and one to consider in receipt of 3yo allowance.
runners.comment: Navan maiden winner likes an ease in the ground; should come on for return run last week

runners.name: Mint Man
runners.spotlight: One win apiece on turf/AW; well beaten behind Nezeeh on sole C&D try in October when trying to make all; should be all the better for return run at Dundalk last month but others look more likely.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind Nezeeh on sole C&D try in October when trying to make all

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Two wins on AW, four on turf; won at the Curragh around this time last year and gained second win of the year at that venue in July off 74 when seeing off Nezeeh by 0.75l; running well in defeat on AW this year and considered back on turf.
runners.comment: Four-time turf winner won at Curragh around this time last year; player back on grass now

runners.name: Kilmood Susan
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden (5f, good) winner who was tailed off last over C&D on final start for Danny Murphy in October; never a factor over further trips since joining this yard; can't fancy unless market speaks; blinkers go back on.
runners.comment: Never a factor over further trips since joining yard; can't fancy unless market speaks

runners.name: Asdana
runners.spotlight: Gained first win at Cork last August (5f, good); ended last season with fine second at the Curragh (5f, heavy), overcoming slow start and headed late on; typically needs reappearance run and much better expected now after Curragh effort eight days ago.
runners.comment: Good form in sprint handicaps last season on varying ground; should improve for return

runners.name: Venetian
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; four AW wins; 1-16 on turf; kept very busy on AW in recent months, on latest collared close home when runner-up over 7f off current mark; 7lb claimer takes over from Colin Keane; one to consider.
runners.comment: Better on the AW but has won on turf; collared close home at Dundalk last time; chance

runners.name: Nezeeh
runners.spotlight: Gained fifth win of career off 1lb lower over C&D (soft) in October; out of form on AW in November; trailed in last of 14 on Curragh return a fortnight ago; contender if he's near his best with capable 7lb claimer aboard.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 84 in October; in poor form since; contender if he's near his best

runners.name: Aurora Nova
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) on this card last year and gained third win of the year over C&D (good) in July off 69; tailed off at the Curragh on return; ground may be against her on this occasion.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner on good but the ground might be against her on this occasion

runners.name: Lagoon Nebula
runners.spotlight: Landed competitive juvenile handicap hurdle at Cheltenham last April on good; good efforts on the Flat at up to 2m after; refused to race on chase debut at Clonmel last month; could figure.
runners.comment: Cheltenham handicap hurdle winner; has run good races on the Flat at up to 2m

runners.name: Baila Conmigo
runners.spotlight: Progressing well on AW for current yard, recording second win at Dundalk last month off 61 before solid third there 10 days ago; not certain to run to that level back on turf but isn't dismissed.
runners.comment: In brilliant form on the AW but not certain to back that up on turf; isn't dismissed

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Was sporting a tongue-tie for the first time on the AW when staying on best over 2m two weeks ago; he's 4lb higher and back in trip, which asks a bit of him; engaged 7.50 Dundalk on Friday.
runners.comment: 2m AW winner last time out; he's 4lb higher and back in trip, which asks a bit of him

runners.name: Radar Ahead
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner on good; 0-9 on Flat but finished runner-up in two 1m4f maidens at this course; makes Flat handicap debut on return from break for local yard so market should prove to be informative.
runners.comment: Second in two maidens here; makes Flat handicap debut for local yard; watch market

runners.name: Superficial
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Group 1 winner Al Riffa; never landed a blow in two hurdle races this season but ran well on his last two starts in Flat handicaps, fifth of 18 over 1m4f at Galway and close third at Listowel (1m3f, heavy) in September from 1lb lower; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Group 1 winner Al Riffa; ran well in Flat handicaps last season; likes mud

runners.name: Tara Power
runners.spotlight: Has run well here several times but 0-25 record is off-putting and was tailed off behind Baila Conmigo at Dundalk when last seen; 7lb wrong.
runners.comment: 0-25 turf record is off-putting and was tailed off behind Baila Conmigo at Dundalk lto

runners.name: Almenfee
runners.spotlight: German recruit has made no impact in three starts for this stable; best form on better ground than this; best watched on return.
runners.comment: German recruit has made no impact in 3 starts for this stable; best form on better ground

runners.name: Thru The Gap
runners.spotlight: Struggled in 1m course claimer on final 2025 start; tailed off on handicap debut at Navan 12 days ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Struggled in 1m course claimer on final 2025 start; tailed off on handicap debut at Navan

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; didn't show a lot in maidens; soundly beaten on handicap debut at Galway last August; better effort on return at Navan (1m4f) when staying on from rear to take sixth of 12; place chance if she gets away on terms.
runners.comment: Better effort on return at Navan (1m4f) when staying on from rear to take sixth of 12

runners.name: Putapoundinthejar
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins last term over 2m; midfield in 1m2f Curragh handicap off 72 in October; quiet enough hurdles campaign this season, down the field in Listed DRF handicap latest; market will guide back on Flat.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown a pile on the Flat and quiet campaign over hurdles this season; watch market

runners.name: Obscenity
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens; looked raw when fifth at Killarney on 1m handicap debut but much-improved at the Curragh (1m2f, heavy) in October off 64 when beaten 2l into fourth of 25; further improvement is feasible on return up in distance; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Best effort yet at the Curragh in October on heavy ground; further improvement likely

runners.name: Bridgehead
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle at this course in July 2024 before sixth on the Flat at Leopardstown same month; stable in good form and market will reveal expectations returning from 632 days off.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles but off the track 632 days; market will reveal expectations

runners.name: Lady Mairen
runners.spotlight: Smart form in 2024 included a 4.75l fourth of 8 in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m, good); ran well on debut on yielding at Galway (7f); dam side of pedigree suggests she'll stay 1m4f, trainer can ready one and she's preferred by Billy Lee; top-rated at 87; obvious chance if she's fit.
runners.comment: Smart form in 2024, top-rated at 87, should stay 1m4f on breeding, preferred by Lee

runners.name: Jannah Star
runners.spotlight: Rated 76; generally consistent filly has placed in four of five starts in maidens at 1m3f-1m4f (including one over C&D); below best on most recent start at Listowel in first-time cheekpieces which are quickly dispensed with; easy ground fine and she has a chance.
runners.comment: Rated 76, placed in four of five starts (inc' over C&D), ground fine, chance

runners.name: Forthill
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; rated 74; promise all three starts last season and was beaten just 1.75l on final start of 2025 at the Curragh (1m4f, heavy) in November; could go well but jockey arrangements suggest she's the second string.
runners.comment: Rated 74, promise all three starts last season, looks the second string on rider bookings

runners.name: Luker's Tipple
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; best of four runs on the Flat a neck second in a C&D maiden (good) last April when trained by Andrew Kinirons; handles an ease and has gone well fresh; rated 77 and in the mix.
runners.comment: Best of four Flat runs came over C&D, handles an ease, rated 77, in the mix

runners.name: Tim Toe
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner shaped with promise on both starts in 2m maiden hurdles at Gowran (heavy) in November and Leopardstown (yielding) in December; interesting Flat debutant for top jumps yard.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, good efforts both maiden hurdles, interesting Flat debutant

runners.name: Joe Cool
runners.spotlight: Yard in fine form and left previous efforts behind when a 1.75l second of 23 at 66-1 on seasonal reappearance at the Curragh (1m, heavy) and the third has won since; worth a go at this trip on the evidence of that run; he's a player.
runners.comment: Form of comeback 1m Curragh second has been boosted, longer trip should suit, player

runners.name: Beechingstoke
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and pulled up in a Fairyhouse bumper in December; yard in form but can only be watched after that.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 28-1 in Fairyhouse bumper in December, can only be watched after that

runners.name: Grennanstown Lady
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three starts over hurdles; off since October and best watched on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Modest form over hurdles, off since October, best watched on Flat debut

runners.name: Nothingbutadream
runners.spotlight: Vadamos gelding; dam ran twice over hurdles, closely related to 2m3f chase winner Toohighforme, half-sister to 3m hurdle winner Let The Show Begin; not much appeal on paper; watch.
runners.comment: Vadamos gelding, dam ran twice over hurdles, not much appeal on paper, watch

runners.name: Good Deal
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in January 2025; rated 109 over hurdles and race-fit but probably best watched on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, rated 109 over hurdles, race-fit but likely best watched on Flat debut

runners.name: Must Meet Cecil
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over hurdles including at this venue is rated 101 in that sphere; has had a recent pipe-opener over timber but likely best watched on belated Flat debut.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles, rated 101 in that sphere, watch on belated Flat debut

runners.name: Jet Renegade
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; similar RPRs in mid 60s in two 1m4f Flat runs at Dundalk; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, similar RPRs (mid 60s) in 1m4f maidens at Dundalk, h'caps beckon

runners.name: Battling Siki
