runners.spotlight: Modest effort on debut at Dundalk (1m4f) but much better there second time up over 10.5f when a 3l fourth of eight; needs to improve again to take this on turf debut.
runners.comment: Modest debut effort at Dundalk, better back there latest, more needed on turf debut

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten both runs over hurdles and similar story on Flat debut at Dundalk when 33-1; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten twice over hurdles and on Flat debut at Dundalk, hard to fancy

runners.name: Captain Conby
runners.spotlight: Talented jumper wasn't disgraced on sole previous start on the Flat at Roscommon in July 2023 (RPR 75); however, he's been absent a long time and is likely best watched on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Talented jumper, promise sole previous start on Flat, watch after mammoth layoff

runners.name: Faithful Follower
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler has been soundly beaten at big prices in two Flat starts at Dundalk; others preferred.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, no show at huge odds in two Flat starts on AW, others preferred

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Magnetude
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily before taking a big step forward on his handicap debut when scoring at Wolverhampton (1m4f) on his return last month; up 4lb this progressive colt remains on a workable mark and rates a big player.
runners.comment: Big step forward on handicap/seasonal debut when scoring at Wolverhampton; big player

runners.name: Galilean Quality
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs in 2025, signing off with success in 1m2f Pontefract novice in October; since gelded and he appeals as the sort to do better still this season, especially now up in trip.
runners.comment: Landed 1m2f Pontefract novice in October; since gelded and sort to do better still in 2026

runners.name: Bystander
runners.spotlight: Dual Newcastle winner who's generally struggled since returning from more than a year off; his only below-par effort on Tapeta came when not seeing out 1m6f, however, and the handicapper's given him every chance.
runners.comment: His only below-par effort on Tapeta came over 1m6f; handicapper has given him every chance

runners.name: Tortured Soul
runners.spotlight: C&D record is currently three wins and two seconds from five starts; most of his career wins have come in the winter but he continues in good form and has strong claims.
runners.comment: Three wins from five C&D starts; continues in good form and his claims are strong

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with and on the downgrade but remains consistent; nudged up a couple of pounds following two near-misses last month but will be happier back up in trip and should go well once more.
runners.comment: Hard to win with and on the downgrade but remains consistent; should go well again

runners.name: Everyoneknowsadave
runners.spotlight: C&D winner for George Wood (only time he's ridden him) last July off a 3lb lower mark; entitled to have come on for last month's comeback here and is one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner for George Wood (only time he's ridden him) last July off 3lb lower; chance

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Charlie Fellowes; three runs for current yard, best of them on second occasion when wearing second-time cheekpieces (1m5f, Polytrack) in February; may not have seen out 2m last time but he's had plenty of chances over various trips now.
runners.comment: May not have seen out 2m last time but he's had plenty of chances over various trips now

runners.name: Bint Al Daar
runners.spotlight: C&D winner against her own sex under Joanna Mason early last month off a 2lb lower mark; continued in form since and this is less competitive, so she needs a look.
runners.comment: Continued in form since C&D win off 2lb lower; this is less competitive; she needs a look

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Went close in a couple of ordinary small-field bumpers late last summer but has struggled on the Flat and over hurdles since; this is more realistic now handicapping in headgear for the first time but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: This is more realistic now handicapping in headgear but others have more pressing claims
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Robbo
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent gelding who's with his third yard; better effort at Lingfield in February but gave himself no chance of seeing out 1m4f on his first crack at beyond 1m2f latest thanks to pulling hard; needs to settle early if he's to have a chance.
runners.comment: Inconsistent gelding who's with his third yard; needs to settle if he's to have a chance

runners.name: Optician
runners.spotlight: Five-time Wolverhampton winner for Shaun Lycett, all off lower marks; pulled up here last September, having clipped heels on his first run in five months and for this yard, and has been absent since; this trip a bare minimum now.
runners.comment: Pulled up here last September for his new yard; off since; this trip a bare minimum now

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Azd
runners.spotlight: Has looked quirky but he ran well in first-time blinkers at Lingfield (1m2f; handicap debut) most recently, taking record since being gelded to 222; helps to set the form standard, with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Record of 222 since being gelded; ran well with blinkers fitted latest; solid chance

runners.name: Sailor Song
runners.spotlight: Seemed to need the outing/experience in C&D event on debut; fared better at Doncaster (1m2f) since, finishing second of nine; already a big player on form and could well take another step forward; respected.
runners.comment: Already a big player on his Doncaster effort and could well take another step forward

runners.name: King Of Thebes
runners.spotlight: 1,500,000gns yearling; Dubawi half-brother to three winners, notably Morgan Le Faye (1m2f-1m4f including Group 2; RPR 111); dam French 1m3f-2m4f scorer (including Group 1; 112); perhaps not straightforward as he's already gelded and wears headgear on debut but his purchase price and pedigree are notable; powerful yard also runs Sailor Song; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: 1,500,000gns yearling; by Dubawi; wears headgear on debut; heed the market signals

runners.name: Stepanov
runners.spotlight: Ran below market expectations at Newcastle (7f) in sole 2yo start; rather belied his bigger odds with second of four at Wolverhampton (9.5f) on reappearance but needs further progress for today's additional distance.
runners.comment: Fared better last time but needs further progress for today's additional distance

runners.name: Masterpiece
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for previous yard; has achieved nothing in two AW Flat contests for new stable, latest over C&D.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; has achieved nothing in two Flat runs

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns 2yo; Camelot half-brother to five winners, notably Bolshoi Ballet (1m-1m4f including US Grade 1; RPR 119); dam unraced; one of two newcomers in the field; check the betting.
runners.comment: 20,000gns 2yo; by Camelot; one of two newcomers in the field; check the betting

runners.name: Guthrum
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in C&D event last month on belated debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in C&D event last month on belated debut

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Ready to go early as a 2yo, winning on second start and close up in a 5f Listed race last May; her handicapping goose appeared to be cooked as a result but she was a bit better than the bare result at Wolverhampton (5f) recently and she shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Good early 5f form as 2yo; has share of weight but creditable 4th at Wolverhampton latest
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Ambishio
runners.spotlight: Front-running 2yo promise although Beverley's stiff 5f proved too much on two occasions; gelded in the close season and was seen to advantage on C&D comeback, showing resolution to hold off his rivals, the first two well clear; back up 3lb; set to go well.
runners.comment: Front-running 2yo promise; showed resolution when C&D winner on return; set to go well

runners.name: Hayynah
runners.spotlight: Early ability at 6f and 7f but she benefited from the drop to 5f when winning over C&D in February; that wasn't particularly competitive but as an unexposed improver she needs considering on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Benefited from the drop to 5f when C&D winner latest; may improve on handicap debut

runners.name: Ice Cold Alex
runners.spotlight: Weights chance on defeat of Saucy Jane on Pontefract form (5f) last spring, when they finished first and second, but some water under the bridge since then and he ran one of his lesser races on sole AW start, at Newcastle in June; needs to have improved during his break to win today.
runners.comment: Beat Saucy Jane on 5f debut; lesser effort on sole AW start; off since September

runners.name: Proof
runners.spotlight: Busy 2yo who came into his own in the autumn, wide-margin 5f winner at Ffos Las (heavy) and creditable shows over 6f (soft and heavy) on final two starts; unraced on AW and prolific winning sibling Spoof was 0-11 on sand for this yard so has some questions to answer.
runners.comment: Busy 2yo; in good form over 5f and 6f on heavy/soft turf in the autumn; unraced on AW

runners.name: Comic Strip
runners.spotlight: Progressive 2yo who was involved in tight finishes for all three 5f Tapeta handicaps, winning over C&D and at Wolverhampton before having what may have been a difficult task at the weights at Newcastle in December; can be dangerous off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won first two handicaps, over C&D and at Wolverhampton; may have had stiff task since

runners.name: Riffa Spirit
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied in three AW starts for the Crisfords, winning a 6f Chelmsford novice in August but only eighth of 11 on handicap debut at Lingfield in September; sold 15,000gns since; no tongue-tie on first 5f run; too many questions this time.
runners.comment: Won 6f Chelmsford novice for the Crisfords; 8th of 11 on handicap debut; new yard

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Timely Affair
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who got off the mark here in January and posted a good second of nine to Aim For The Bull in 1m Kempton handicap 17 days ago, not enjoying the smoothest of passages under 2f out; enjoys a 1lb weights pull here and can go well.
runners.comment: Good second at Kempton latest, not enjoying smooth run; can go well despite a 2lb rise

runners.name: Brother Dave
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with C&D victory (third one) and has continued in good form, fading late on when third of 11 in 1m classified event here last time; ought to be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: In good form, third in 1m classified event here last time; ought to be thereabouts again

runners.name: Shades Of May
runners.spotlight: Made it two wins in his last three starts in 7f handicap at Chelmsford 16 days ago; up 4lb but he's not taken lightly in his current mood.
runners.comment: Made it two wins in last three starts at Chelmsford 16 days ago; not taken lightly

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Back on song when landing 1m Kempton handicap 17 days ago; up 3lb but this C&D winner must enter calculations from a low draw.
runners.comment: Landed 1m Kempton handicap latest; up 3lb but this C&D winner must enter calculations

runners.name: Ardaddy
runners.spotlight: C&D winner but below par of late, beating one in 1m handicap here two weeks ago; it's now 13 starts since that victory too.
runners.comment: C&D winner but below par of late; it's now 13 starts since that victory

runners.name: Hannah's Return
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2024 but she posted her best effort of the season when third of nine in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 19 days ago; she can make her presence felt off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Best effort of season when third at Wolverhampton latest; can make her presence felt

runners.name: Gundogan
runners.spotlight: Winless since August 2024 but he comes here in decent nick, fifth of nine in 7f Lingfield handicap 17 days ago; place prospects off an easing mark.
runners.comment: Winless since August 2024 but comes here in decent nick; place prospects off easing mark

runners.name: Just King High
runners.spotlight: Course scorer who returned to form in a headgear change when second of eight in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 29 days ago on his final run for Thomas Faulkner; not out of things for his new handler.
runners.comment: Course scorer; good Wolverhampton second final run for Thomas Faulkner; not out of things

runners.name: Pink Socks
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner early in 2026, including over C&D, for Ian Williams; not discredited on just her second run for current yard when fifth in 1m handicap here two weeks ago; in the mix off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fifth in handicap here two weeks ago; in the mix off a slipping mark

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: I Can Boogy
runners.spotlight: Started 2026 with 1m classified success at Newcastle and posted a solid third of 11 in similar event there (7f) last time; this course winner needs considering.
runners.comment: Solid third at Newcastle last time; this course winner needs considering

runners.name: Heretic
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over 1m here in 2025 and back to form of late, posting a good third of 11 in 1m handicap here three weeks ago; he's well in the mix eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Back to form of late, third here latest; this dual course winner is well in the mix

runners.name: Drafted
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f winner in Ireland for Charles Byrnes in 2025 but yet to hit top form for his current yard, only eighth in 6f Wolverhampton handicap 29 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form for current yard, only eighth at Wolverhampton 29 days ago

runners.name: Captain Pickles
runners.spotlight: Thriving in 2026 and he bagged a third success in 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap 12 days ago when overcoming a wide passage; up 3lb but this C&D scorer merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Gained third win at Wolverhampton latest; this C&D scorer merits serious consideration

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: On a lengthy losing sequence but she fared better than of late when third of 11 in 6f classified event at Wolverhampton 18 days ago; this C&D winner needs considering.
runners.comment: On a lengthy losing sequence but solid third at Wolverhampton latest; not dismissed

runners.name: Fort Augustus
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and he came in a below-par sixth in 7f Lingfield handicap 17 days ago; cheekpieces are tried now (hood also refitted) but others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Remains winless; cheekpieces are tried (hood also refitted) but others more persuasive

runners.name: Mavetheforcebewivu
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but she was largely in good form in 2025; returns from eight months off in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Largely in good form in 2025; back from eight months off in a change of headgear

runners.name: Mickey Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run in classified event over C&D in February and not disgraced back in handicap company when fifth over C&D following month; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: C&D winner; not disgraced when fifth over C&D last time; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Turbo Command
runners.spotlight: Veteran three-time C&D winner; off 12 months before a well-held fifth of seven in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; off 12 months off before well-held fifth at Newcastle last month

runners.name: Thomas Equinas
runners.spotlight: It's now 18 starts since his last victory and he came in only ninth of 12 in 6f Kempton handicap last month; others are preferred.
runners.comment: It's 18 starts since last victory and only ninth of 12 in 6f Kempton handicap last month

runners.name: Barney's Bay
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2023 and he has yet to make the frame in seven starts in 2026; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Without a victory since 2023 and he has yet to make the frame in seven starts in 2026

runners.name: Farandaway
runners.spotlight: Plenty of short-priced defeats before finally off the mark on his seventeenth start in a Newcastle maiden a few weeks ago; hard to know the merits of that but he returns to handicap company on a workable mark and confidence will have been boosted.
runners.comment: Returns to handicap company on a workable mark and confidence will have been boosted

runners.name: Sherlock
runners.spotlight: Hampered and eased off when a beaten-favourite last time, having previously got off the mark on his first run in a sprint (5f) off 6lb lower; unexposed as a sprinter.
runners.comment: Hampered and eased off when a beaten-favourite last time; unexposed as a sprinter

runners.name: Thankuappreciate
runners.spotlight: Stable yet to get rolling for the year but he was a bright spot when springing a surprise over C&D off 3lb lower in February; not entirely convinced over 7f a couple of times since and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Surprise winner over C&D off 3lb lower in February; not convinced over 7f twice since

runners.name: Kirkdale
runners.spotlight: String of consistent efforts on Tapeta since sporting headgear, culminating in last weekend's Wolverhampton win off 5lb lower; not a lot of luck round Newcastle prior to that and it would come as no surprise to see him follow up.
runners.comment: String of consistent efforts on Tapeta in headgear; no surprise to see him defy a 5lb rise

runners.name: Jesse Luc
runners.spotlight: Three wins, two seconds and a third to his name from 11 starts over C&D; beaten as favourite (behind Farandaway) here a month ago but is on a workable mark now and one to consider back under Rossa Ryan.
runners.comment: Three wins, two seconds and a third to his name from 11 starts over C&D; shortlisted

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who closed out last season with a narrow beating of Kirkdale off 3lb lower at Newcastle (first-time blinkers; goes in first-time cheekpieces here); interesting to see how he goes in the market back from five months off.
runners.comment: Interesting to see how he goes in the market back from five months off upped 3lb

runners.name: Northcliff
runners.spotlight: This his time of year, with two May wins in 2024 and a hat-trick last spring; arrives off the back of a string of modest efforts, though, the returning visor not making much difference last time.
runners.comment: This his time of year but arrives off the back of a string of modest efforts

runners.name: Early Release
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning at Kempton early last month (said to have appreciated being able to dominate); unable to get to the front down to 5f here next time and is another 3lb higher tonight; a lot will hinge on how things play out early.
runners.comment: Another 3lb higher tonight; happiest in front; lot will hinge on how things play out early

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Handicap

runners.name: Piperstown
runners.spotlight: Landed a sixth victory on Tapeta when going in at Wolverhampton off 3lb lower three weeks ago; never won off a mark quite this high but gives his trainer, who also run He's An Angel, a decent hand.
runners.comment: Landed a sixth victory on Tapeta three weeks ago; never won off a mark quite this high

runners.name: Kamekist
runners.spotlight: Lost his way last year after a spring win for Eve Johnson Houghton off 13lb higher; well supported ahead of his C&D comeback in February, when faring much better off this mark, and is one to consider in a first-time tongue-tie for a yard going well of late.
runners.comment: Well supported ahead of his C&D comeback in February; one to consider for a yard in form

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with and made it 0-12 on the AW when well held at Wolverhampton last time; essentially regressive and is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Hard to win with; essentially regressive and is some way down the pecking order

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Often fluffs the start and is always reliant on luck given his run style; not had the breaks since a Wolverhampton win in January but is back into 0-55 company for the first time since and holds claims, especially if he settles better in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Back in 0-55 company and holds claims especially if he settles better in a first-time hood

runners.name: Beautiful Things
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's finished well held in two runs back in 0-50 classified company; would be a surprise winner switching from cheekpieces to a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who's finished well held in two runs back in 0-50 classified company

runners.name: Griselda
runners.spotlight: Won three nurseries for George Scott two summers ago but has regressed sharply since, coming 26lb down the weights for her current yard; a better effort in a first-time tongue-tie, without seeing out 7f, last time gave her something to build on.
runners.comment: Better run in the tongue-tie, without seeing out 7f, latest gave her something to build on

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner on Tapeta but never round here; arrives in good form after two Wolverhampton victories last month, latterly off 3lb lower, but has never won on the AW off a mark quite this high; trainer also runs East Tyrone.
runners.comment: Prolific winner on Tapeta but never here; arrives in good form but high in the weights

runners.name: Buraback
runners.spotlight: Does most racing now over 5f and hasn't won over this trip in three years; typically consistent of late, off a sliding mark, but he's only finished placed once from ten starts round here.
runners.comment: Does most racing now over 5f; typically consistent of late off a sliding mark

runners.name: East Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Odds-on to miss the break and an even shorter price to pull hard; will always come with risks attached given his run style and has been expensive to follow this year; goes back to blinkers from a visor tonight for a yard also responsible for Fircombe Hall.
runners.comment: Will always come with risks attached given his run style; expensive to follow this year
runners.owner: Alan And David Fairhurst And P Kirby

runners.name: Man On A Mission
runners.spotlight: Most wins come on Polytrack but goes well here, has slid to a fair mark and been offering more of late; won twice before for headgear coming off (cheekpieces removed tonight) and is a player in an open handicap.
runners.comment: Goes well here, has slid to a fair mark and been offering more of late; player

runners.name: Reachin' Out
runners.spotlight: From heavyweight stable and made an absolute mess of them on debut in rain affected going at Randwick-Kensington. Drawn to effect. Rates highly.
runners.owner: GW Holdings, F W Cook Et Al

runners.name: Rachini
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Broke maiden at Wyong 18 Mar. Threw the anchor out late after leading here. More suitable draw.
runners.owner: Bjorn Baker Racing, B E Risetto Et Al

runners.name: Satono Glow
runners.spotlight: Ex John O'Shea & Tom Charlton. Trained here now. One out of the box. Made short work of the small field in the Thoroughbred Breeders Stakes at Flemington in only her second race appearance. Drawn to receive every chance.
runners.owner: R J Miller, Werrett Bloodstock Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: She's Extra
runners.spotlight: Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: Mrs A T Throsby, Mrs D Statham Et Al

runners.name: Medicinal
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Won the 2yo Mdn Plate at Ballarat two back 22 Feb. Pushed forward and found enough to score as a $1.80 chance at Flemington last start. Since impressed with a Randwick trial win 27 Mar.

runners.name: Lumbini
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Won the 2yo Plate Gold Coast 19 Dec. Off pace, solid through line Tresemm Magic Night at Rosehill at this distance. Since impressed with a Randwick trial win 27 Mar.

runners.name: Screen Icon
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Form from three runs has been respectable. Out the back, closed with raking strides in the Sweet Embrace Stakes here second-up. Since trialled at Randwick 27 Mar.
runners.owner: Miss C J Stone, Ms M J Musgrave Et Al

runners.name: Ciaron's Star
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Sound but non-threatening effort at Rosehill over this distance. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 30 Mar. Can make her presence felt.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, M S Hovey Et Al

runners.name: Cherry Bomshell
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Finished well to take the 2yo F Qlty over 1000m last outing. Since won a Warwick Farm trial 20 Mar. Awkwardly drawn.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, L C Henderson Et Al

runners.name: After Summer
runners.spotlight: Trailed pace and kicked strongly on a soft deck at Rosehill in second run from spell. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Added journey should assist. Can challenge.
runners.owner: Sutton Racing Pty Ltd, S Chappell Et Al

runners.name: Ohope Wins
runners.spotlight: Steps out for second Aust start Ex-NZ. Won New Zealand Oaks Ellerslie 21 Feb. First Australian start knuckled down with purpose in soft going at Rosehill when in the market. Will take stopping in this company.

runners.name: Stand My Ground
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Newcastle 12 Mar. Run down in the shadows after giving a kick from on pace in soft going at Rosehill at second run back. Stripping fitter and not out of this.
runners.owner: Dr U Crosson, Mrs S Crosson Et Al

runners.name: Soverato
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Knuckled down in choppy conditions for second this track when in the market. Distance increase suits. Key player.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, A Berry Et Al

runners.name: Profoundly
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Close placing in the Kembla Grange Classic at Kembla Grange. Delivered an explosive win in the Adrian Knox Stakes in choppy conditions this track. Should appreciate stepping up in distance.
runners.owner: D L Freedman, M D Sullivan Et Al

runners.name: Long Legs
runners.spotlight: Knuckled down with purpose in soft going at Rosehill when pushed out in the market. First try at 2400m. Has some claims.
runners.owner: B K Racing & Breeding Pty Ltd & B J Vassallo

runners.name: Dance With Destiny
runners.spotlight: Put in some good work late at Hawkesbury in second run from spell. Quickly up in trip. Keener on others.
runners.owner: Champion Thoroughbreds, G M Fraser Et Al

runners.name: Queen Of Clubs
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Well back and never sighted this track. Usually gets back. Unexposed at trip.

runners.name: Classic Gem
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Third outing for new trainer/ this region. Went close at Caulfield two back 21 Mar. Settled back and passed a few late in the Adrian Knox Stakes this track at second run back.
runners.owner: Sutton Racing Pty Ltd, S Chappell & N Sutton

runners.name: Satono Invader
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Ridden cold, produced solid finish late this track. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Not fancied.

runners.name: La Morra
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Well back and made limited headway this track. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: Dr K P Rewell, Mrs J M Stone Et Al

runners.name: Mountain Queen
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Failed to threaten in rain affected going here at cricket score odds. Rise to 2400m a new challenge. Looks tested.
runners.owner: MCS Racing, S Reid Et Al

runners.name: Tempted
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Knuckled down strongly from back half to salute in the Surround Stakes Open 1400m here. Since impressed with a Rosehill trial win 24 Mar. At optimum distance.

runners.name: Beiwacht
runners.spotlight: Went close at Randwick two back 7 Mar. Faded off the lead at Rosehill over a longer test. Jump-and-run colt. Drops to shorter trip.

runners.name: Beadman
runners.spotlight: Randwick winner two back. Didn't quite finish it off the last bit in Open company at Rosehill in second run from spell. Excels on rain affected tracks. Won only other time at Randwick.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock, China Horse Club Racing Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Marhoona
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of fight winning Kia Ora Galaxy at Rosehill. Has early speed. Both efforts here stack up. Worth a look.

runners.name: Skyhook
runners.spotlight: Close up in sound performance Darby Munro Stakes at Rosehill at resumption when in the market. Fitter second-up. Good gate. Been thereabouts here.
runners.owner: Ryan & Alexiou Racing, Stallion Match Et Al

runners.name: Devil Night
runners.spotlight: Started $81, fighting out the finish at Caulfield second-up. Untried in the wet. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Caffe Florian
runners.spotlight: Gosford Guineas winner Gosford 28 Dec. Got back, closed the race off solidly at Rosehill at this trip. Could give some cheek at odds.

runners.name: Grafterburners
runners.spotlight: First-up (13-weeks). In superb form last campaign. Proven fresh and looks fit for return to racing. A distance specialist.
runners.owner: G A Betts, K S Swindail Et Al

runners.name: Pallaton
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold and never in the picture at Rosehill at this trip. Others with stronger claims.

runners.name: Nashville Jack
runners.spotlight: Went close at Flemington two back 28 Feb. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge in stakes company at Rosehill. Others have more zip.

runners.name: Machine Gun Gracie
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Showed fighting qualities to win the Epona Stakes at Rosehill. Gets in light. Has early speed.
runners.owner: G R Browne, Miss J Erkelens Et Al

runners.name: Litzdeel
runners.spotlight: Won the (Bm96) Flemington 4 Nov. Issued decent challenge in choppy conditions The Roy Higgins at Flemington. Has a light weight. Distance increase suits.
runners.owner: P J Lanskey, Mrs H Hueston Et Al

runners.name: Juja Kibo
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Close placing in the N E Manion Cup at Rosehill. No threat to winner but ran on for the minors in rain affected going here when in the market. Absorbs pressure well and under less weight.
runners.owner: M Johnston, Mrs A J Johnston Et Al

runners.name: Piggyback
runners.spotlight: Ridden back and never a factor at Rosehill. Adept heavy/proven track. Has her first look at 3200m here. Can put last run behind her.
runners.owner: Dynamic Syndications Racing, S D Murray Et Al

runners.name: Soul Of Spain
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Sky High Stakes at Rosehill. Did little on a soft deck at Rosehill. Respect on previous sound form.
runners.owner: A J A Bosma, N J C Smith Et Al

runners.name: Whisky On The Hill
runners.spotlight: Stuck tough, holding off challengers The Roy Higgins at Flemington at $6. First try at 3200m. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: G Devonport, R & C Legh Racing Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Athabascan
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Long time since last saluted. Worked home solidly in choppy conditions this venue. Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: OTI Racing, F W Cook Et Al

runners.name: Mr Monaco
runners.spotlight: Striving for hat-trick. Took out the 3yo+ (Bm88) at Randwick 28 Feb. Just got there in a gripping finish at at Rosehill. Won only other time at Randwick.
runners.owner: L G C Le Metayer, Blueblood Thoroughbreds Et Al

runners.name: Highland Bling
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Terrific effort when just shaded at Morphettville 9 Mar. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 30 Mar. Query wet.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, Mrs A Stokes Et Al

runners.name: Newlook
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Let down with a fine turn of speed from the back half to win. Nicely weighted. Winner at only other start at Randwick.
runners.owner: Busuttin Racing, Surf Coast Racing Et Al

runners.name: Changingoftheguard
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Led, soon under pressure and dropped off this track. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: N C Williams, L J Williams Et Al

runners.name: Valiant King
runners.spotlight: Never sighted at Rosehill. Unplaced in wet. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: A Kheir, Qatar Bloodstock Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Campaldino
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Solid performance in defeat in the Chairmans Qlty this venue. Gets in light. More ground a plus.
runners.owner: Darby Racing, Azzurri Tiger Racing Et Al

runners.name: Hutchence
runners.spotlight: Sent out $51 when unable to threaten at Warwick Farm. Void of early speed and needs the breaks to fall his way. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: E G C Ritchie & A J Ramsden

runners.name: River Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Last win was 23 Jun 2024. Went home tamely on a soft deck at Rosehill at $31 but suited out to 3200m. Keep safe.

races.race_name: Moet & Chandon Queen Of The Turf Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Treasurethe Moment
runners.spotlight: Multiple G1 winner. Close placing in the Futurity Stakes at Caulfield. Dominant on-pace performance in the Sunline Stakes at Caulfield at second run back. Drawn to get an economic trail.

runners.name: Pride Of Jenni
runners.spotlight: High class galloper. Close placing in the All-Star Mile at Flemington. Strode forward off wide gate, fought on bravely to just miss in rain affected going at Flemington at second run back. Ready to peak third-up.
runners.owner: A Ottobre, Mrs L Ottobre & M Ottobre

runners.name: Lady Shenandoah
runners.spotlight: Multiple G1 winner. Just wacked away without threatening over 1500m George Ryder Stakes at Rosehill. Breaks from a low barrier. Winner at track and distance.

runners.name: Lazzura
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Close placing in the Millie Fox Stakes at Rosehill. Wore them down late at Rosehill in second run from spell. Since trialled at Rosehill 2 Apr.

runners.name: She's A Hustler
runners.spotlight: Resuming (19-weeks). A the bottom of her win range here. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: M & J Racing, T J Auld Et Al

runners.name: Leica Lucy
runners.spotlight: Started $4.60 when battled home in Open company in choppy conditions at Flemington. Has won multiple times in wet. A distance specialist. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: A Kheir, Mrs H J Crofskey Et Al

runners.name: Arctic Glamour
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Couldn't quite peg back winner at Rosehill. Since trialled at Rosehill 2 Apr. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: M H Wood, Mrs M E Wood Et Al

runners.name: Idle Flyer
runners.spotlight: Off midfield when flew home to score at Rosehill at second run back. Strong wet stats. Drawn to effect and a multiple winner this track under Zac Lloyd. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: MCS Racing, A B Tall Et Al

runners.name: Melody Again
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Finished within striking distance Coolmore Classic at Rosehill. Since impressed with a Scone trial win 1 Apr. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Dr M A Kowalczuk & Mrs E W Kowalczuk

runners.name: Pinito
runners.spotlight: Won the Aspiration Qlty at Randwick two back 7 Mar. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge in black-type company at Rosehill. Awkwardly drawn. Drops to shorter trip.

runners.name: Perfumist
runners.spotlight: Set tempo, offered little resistance when challenged at Rosehill at second run back. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, S Cheetham Et Al

runners.name: Dark Glitter
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Sent forward but flat-footed when things got serious at Rosehill. Has an adverse draw. Keener on others.
runners.owner: M F Van Gestel, Mrs L M Van Gestel et al

runners.name: Modella
runners.spotlight: Took out the 3yo+ (Bm88) at Rosehill 14 Mar. Went home tamely on a soft deck at Rosehill at $6.50. Has an adverse draw. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Mystery Downs, Mrs C M Cook Et Al

runners.name: Dubai Honour
runners.spotlight: Elite performer. Ears scrubbed off early to cross and lead but kept on bravely on a soft deck for the minors first up. Fitter, proven second-up. A distance specialist.
runners.owner: Exors of the late Mohamed Obaida

runners.name: Autumn Glow
runners.spotlight: Undefeated star. Too strong George Ryder Stakes at Rosehill. Previous winner in wet here. Suited by the longer assignment now.
runners.owner: Arrowfield Pastoral Pty Ltd Syndicate & Hermitage Syndicate

runners.name: Caviar Heights
runners.spotlight: Left in winner's wake but ran on soundly for 2.7 len second in the small field at Rosehill. First time around Randwick. This looks stronger.

runners.name: Sir Delius
runners.spotlight: Attacked line late to claim third in the small field at Rosehill second-up. All the better and can make his presence felt.
runners.owner: Go Bloodstock Australia, A J N Brooks Et Al

runners.name: Aeliana
runners.spotlight: Elite performer. Won the Ranvet Stakes at Rosehill two back 21 Mar. On pace and stuck on grittily for narrow Kia Tancred Stakes win at Rosehill. Coming back in distance should suit.
runners.owner: Star Thoroughbreds Pty Ltd, Boeara Holdings Et Al

runners.name: Lindermann
runners.spotlight: Went down fighting after making all Ranvet Stakes at Rosehill at this trip. Proven in wet. Leads, handy. Can play a role.

runners.name: Wootton Verni
runners.spotlight: Made short work of the small field in the Neville Sellwood Stakes at Rosehill at this distance. Harder now.
runners.owner: C J Waller, Go Bloodstock Australia Et Al

runners.name: Light Infantry Man
runners.spotlight: Responded well from just off leaders to snare a final bound win at Flemington. Hard fit and one for multiples.
runners.owner: Halo Racing Services Pty Ltd, A Kheir Et Al

runners.name: Gangsta Granny
runners.spotlight: 100% win-place strike rate. Prominent and showed plenty of ticker in soft going to just go under in thrilling finish in the Star Kingdom Stakes at Rosehill in second run from spell. Can lead or take a sit. Winner at track and distance.
runners.owner: A E Koundouris & Mrs G Koundouris

runners.name: Flying For Fun
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Reeled in leaders to salute at Rosehill at resumption. Since impressed with a Warwick Farm trial win 30 Mar. Will be fitter second-up.
runners.owner: M W O'Keeffe & Ms V J King

runners.name: Monte Supreme
runners.spotlight: Last assignment this trip when worked home solidly in soft going at Rosehill. Gets through rain affected ground. Usually gets back. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: V W Bates & Mrs Y D Bates

runners.name: Coco Jamboo
runners.spotlight: Last win was 4 May 2024. Comfortably beaten on a soft deck at Rosehill at resumption. Keener on others.
runners.owner: D A Dickie, Mrs L Dickie Et Al

runners.name: Splash Back
runners.spotlight: Resuming (23-weeks). Query wet. Usually gets back. New to Randwick.
runners.owner: Ronball, C McGregor & Miss Z J McGregor

runners.name: Infancy
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Rosehill at first run back. Fitter second-up. Has a tricky draw and rising in weight. Form sound at this course and journey.
runners.owner: Miss S M Parkinson, Mrs K Lovett Et Al

runners.name: Tiger Shark
runners.spotlight: Just wacked away without threatening over 1000m ATA/Bob Hoysted Hcp at Flemington at first run back. Handles the wet. Will be fitter second-up. One for exotics.
runners.owner: Epona Park, R Marro et al

runners.name: In Flight
runners.spotlight: Quietly handled in back half, shaken up and finished full of running at Rosehill at second run back. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Wet track specialist. Better draw here.
runners.owner: Proven Thoroughbreds, Ms K A Tibbey Et Al

runners.name: More Territories
runners.spotlight: Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge in black-type company at Rosehill. Stripping fitter and goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Finemore Thoroughbreds & Planette Thoroughbred Trading P/L

runners.name: Catch The Glory
runners.spotlight: Got up in final strides at Rosehill first up. Excels on rain affected tracks. Fitter second-up. Can deliver again.
runners.owner: J C Coyle, B D Kirkby Et Al

runners.name: She's Got Pizzazz
runners.spotlight: Valiant but vanquished in the 4yo+ M Open Hcp down the Flemington straight 28 Mar. Stiffer opposition here.

runners.name: Spring Lee
runners.spotlight: Led, dug deep into her reserves but shaded late over 1200m in the Birthday Card Stakes at Rosehill at this distance. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: Cressfield, Ms L Baumann Et Al

runners.name: Monte Kate
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Rode the speed and went down in head-bobber at Kembla Grange over this distance. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: V W Bates & Mrs Y D Bates

runners.name: Husk
runners.spotlight: First-up (23-weeks). Placed this track first-up last campaign while indicating was forward winning a Rosehill trial. Up against it in this company.

runners.name: Nitrogen
runners.spotlight: High-class filly, winner of six Graded stakes, including Grade 1 Alabama at Saratoga last August; won Grade 3 on reappearance (wandered) in fast time, but only third to Majestic Oops in Grade 2 Azeri on sloppy track, again over this C&D; strong chance to bounce back.
runners.comment: Six-time Graded winner, including Grade 1 level; C&D winner on reappearance; must go well

runners.name: Claret Beret
runners.spotlight: Typically well placed by trainer Saffie Joseph to win six times, though has lost four of five attempts in Graded stakes; up in trip, first Graded victory last time in 8.5f Grade 3 Royal Delta at Gulfstream where she swooped from off pace to win by 5l; claims, if she did indeed improve for that extra distance.
runners.comment: Improved to win 8.5f Grade 3 at Gulfstream by 5l, behind early; chance if confirming form

runners.name: Blue Fire
runners.spotlight: Winner of half of her 12 starts, though nothing remotely this good; again cleverly placed by trainer Steve Asmussen when scrambling home in black type event at Fair Grounds last time; genuine, but nothing to recommend her in this grade as yet.
runners.comment: Campaigned at lower level, wining half of 12 starts; needs to improve sharply to contend

runners.name: Om N Joy
runners.spotlight: Completed five-timer in 2025 in Grade 3 Torrey Pines Stakes at Del Mar (1m); in frame since in Grade 2 at Keeneland (7f) and Grade 1 Beholder Mile at Santa Anita, the latter on reappearance when a distant third, beaten nearly 13l by Splendora; something to find.
runners.comment: Completed 2025 five-timer in Grade 3; decent efforts in frame since but something to find
runners.owner: Connie S Baker, Jerry Baker Et Al

runners.name: Regaled
runners.spotlight: Improved late as 4yo, winning Grade 3 at Delaware before third to Scylla in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Distaff at Del Mar (close behind runner-up Nitrogen and now 6lb better off); beaten under 1l by Majestic Oops (and now 2lb better off) in Grade 2 over C&D (sloppy; Nitrogen third) on return; must go close.
runners.comment: Much improved; split Majestic Oops and Nitrogen in C&D Grade 2 on return; must go well
runners.owner: Ribble Farms & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Dazzling Move
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive, though no wins above Grade 3 level; near 50-1 when only fifth in Grade 2 Inside Information Stakes at Gulfstream on return; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Has flashed ability, though no wins above Grade 3 level; near 50-1 when fifth on comeback

runners.name: Majestic Oops
runners.spotlight: Super tough filly who has made relentless improvement over 35 starts; in form of her life at present, defeating Regaled and Nitrogen determinedly in Grade 2 Azeri over C&D (sloppy) last time; sure to give another good account, though may find Nitrogen a tougher foe this time; admirable sort.
runners.comment: Tough filly who beat Regaled and Nitrogen in C&D Grade 2 Azeri last time; sure to go well

runners.name: Nerazurri
runners.spotlight: Very useful form and in good heart in 2026, winning black type events in January (easily, after left alone up front) and February (taken on up front, still prevailed by more than 2l); readily beaten nearly 3l by Nitrogen in Grade 3, again over C&D, in between; game and consistent but has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Front-runner, winner of two C&D black types and second to Nitrogen in Grade 3; bit to find

runners.name: Five G
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and upwardly mobile early in 2025, winning 8.5f Grade 2 Gulfstream Park Oaks in March; off nine months afterwards and disappointing in 7f Grade 1 at Santa Anita in December, but she looked improved when fluent winner of an allowance at Gulfstream on 2026 reappearance; open to more improvement.
runners.comment: Improved front-runner in 2025, winning Grade 2; impressive in allowance on return; claims

runners.name: Dynamic Pricing
runners.spotlight: Winner of three Graded stakes, notably Grade 1 Just A Game at Saratoga last June (1m, yielding); never able to challenge in Grade 1 First Lady Stakes here on firmer surface on final start; has won two of her three seasonal debuts and could go close, if surface suits; trainer has great record.
runners.comment: Winner of three Graded stakes, including at top level (yielding surface); player

runners.name: Lush Lips
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish filly who's thrived in US; winner of four of last five, notably Grade 1 Queen Elizabeth II Cup here (1m1f) last October; won slow-paced Grade 3 Honey Fox at Gulfstream Park (1m) on reappearance by under 1l, cutting down leaders and much the best, having allowed first run; strong claims.
runners.comment: Very smart filly; has won four of last five, including Grade 1 here in 2025; strong claims

runners.name: Segesta
runners.spotlight: Very smart form in Grade 1 company last autumn; second in First Lady Stakes here (1m1f; beaten a neck after racing freely) in October then won Matriarch at Del Mar by just over 2l, settling better and accelerating most impressively; strong claims on reappearance (trainer has great record in this).
runners.comment: Improved last two starts of 2025 in Grade 1s, winning readily at Del Mar; strong claims

runners.name: Destino d'Oro
runners.spotlight: In form of her life at present, winning at Grade 2 level in 8.5f Pegasus World Cup Filly & Mare Turf in January (came from well back) and 1m1f Hillsborough Stakes at Tampa Bay in March (stalked slow pace, did well to win in close finish); somewhat beholden to run of race but really progressive.
runners.comment: Progressive filly with pair of Grade 2 wins in 2026, coming off slow pace latest; claims

runners.name: Pin Up Betty
runners.spotlight: This is a tough spot to reappear for last year's Grade 3 Ladies Turf winner at Kentucky Downs; fourth and fifth in two Grade 1 attempts and needs to improve a fair bit on balance of form; seems ideally suited by Churchill Downs and this may put her right for next month's meeting there.
runners.comment: Scored in Grade 3 in 2025 but has found it tougher in two Grade 1s; may need the outing

runners.name: Expensive Queen
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in Britain where she earned a peak mark of 83; very useful form in the US, winning an allowance at Gulfstream in January and 8.5f Listed at Fair Grounds (by more than 3l) following month; beaten 6l when only fourth in sole Grade 1 race, however, so needs to take game to new level.
runners.comment: Ex-British mare; improved in US and won last two but well held in her sole Grade 1 bid

runners.name: Medoro
runners.spotlight: Thoroughly consistent mare, in frame 13 of 14 starts; placed in two of three Grade 1 attempts, but beaten at a much lower level when second to Expensive Queen in 8.5f Listed race at Fair Grounds last time (now 6lb better off for little over 2l); smart but hard to be confident she can defeat these.
runners.comment: Consistent mare, in frame 13 of 14 starts but beaten in all three Grade 1s; outside chance

runners.name: Aussie Girl
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in Ireland; has taken well to US, where winner three times to date, notably Grade 3 Endeavour Stakes at Tampa Bay in January; made all on latest start, scoring by more than 2l, but does not have to lead; looks likely to get run of race; reportedly going to stud after this race.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish mare with three US wins, notably 2026 Grade 3; tactical speed aids place claims

runners.name: Fast Market
runners.spotlight: Took nine races to get off mark before winning a maiden at Aqueduct in September; markedly improved last two starts, second in Grade 2 then justifying favouritism in Grade 3 Pebbles Stakes (8.5f) at Aqueduct by more than 1l, forced wide and running on well; others appear to have more potential.
runners.comment: Much improved form last two outings, winning Grade 3 at Aqueduct latest; still needs more

runners.name: Deep Satin
runners.spotlight: Three career wins include two Listed races, latterly at Saratoga last July when game in blanket finish; form figures 5245 in Graded stakes tells its own story, though second in Grade 2 Ballston Spa at Saratoga last July (1m) was a strong performance; has tactical speed and needs run of race.
runners.comment: Dual Listed winner beaten in all four Graded stakes; needs run of the race
runners.owner: John D Gunther & Eurowest Bloodstock Services

runners.name: Stung
runners.spotlight: Lining up for three wins on the trot. Started $6 when handily placed and showed fine turn of foot to register a 1.8 len win here. Winner at only other start at Morphettville. Can back it up.
runners.owner: R & C Legh Racing P/L, B & C Tbreds P/L & Pulitano Tbreds

runners.name: Steel Trap
runners.spotlight: Sent out $9 when unable to threaten at Sandown-Lakeside when off an extended spell. Strips fitter second run back. Rise to 1800m a new challenge. Capable of making an impact.

runners.name: Mating Call
runners.spotlight: Worked home solidly here. More ground a plus. Sharper now and will take stopping in this company.

runners.name: Oak Park Maddison
runners.spotlight: Worked hard both ends and narrowly outpointed. On pacer. First try at 1800m. Deep into her campaign and one for exotics.
runners.owner: Campolina Pty Ltd & Mrs E Doudle

runners.name: Kazaru
runners.spotlight: Let-up (50-days). Annexed the Mdn Plate at Sale 1 Feb. Let down with a fine turn of speed from the back half to win over a longer assignment. Since trialled at Caulfield Heath 26 Mar.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, A M Kerr Et Al

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: Off the speed, picked up soundly and wasn't beaten far this venue. Winner this trip. Can challenge.
runners.owner: B H Perks & Ms A M Clough

runners.name: Morgana
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Failed to run on in the small field at Caulfield. Leads, handy. Super fit from racing and can play a role.
runners.owner: Mrs C S Ryan & J Pace

runners.name: Synthesise
runners.spotlight: Excellent effort chiming in late here second-up. Rise to 1800m a new challenge. One for multiples.
runners.owner: P R Gunner, D R Wagner & S R Wagner

runners.name: Naraghi
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Broke maiden at Wangaratta 28 Feb. Raced in back half and never likely at Flemington. Since trialled at Caulfield Heath 26 Mar.
runners.owner: L Howley, C W Kwok Et Al

runners.name: Oui Oui Ma Cherie
runners.spotlight: Settled back and didn't figure in the Clare Lindop Stakes this venue. Unplaced from 4 starts here. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: S P Gower, P J Treloar Et Al

runners.name: Boomeroo
runners.spotlight: Rounded them up easily at Sale when $3.20 second-up. One for exotics.

runners.name: Lufitaah
runners.spotlight: On pace and toughed it out well at Ballarat second-up. Just out of her maiden and facing tougher challenge here.
runners.owner: M F R Infitaah, S Najda Et Al

runners.name: Thundara
runners.spotlight: On pace and stuck on grittily for narrow 3yo+ Mdn Plate win at Balaklava. Stiffer challenge now after breaking through.
runners.owner: A Bain, Mapperley Stud Et Al

runners.name: Big Rita June
runners.spotlight: Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge in black-type company this venue. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: L B Grantham, Ms E G Grantham Et Al

runners.name: Angling Angel
runners.spotlight: Ran a cracking race when just edged out in rain affected going at Sale 29 Mar. Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: B Sinclair, G D Sutton & I C Jones

runners.name: Prancing Lily
runners.spotlight: Threw the anchor out late after leading at Balaklava. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: P M Nolan, G Taormina Et Al

runners.name: Ashwaan
runners.owner: Sheikh Abdul Aziz bin Humaid Al Nuaimi

runners.name: Welcome Dream
runners.owner: Sheikh Abdul Aziz bin Humaid Al Nuaimi

runners.name: Mostabshir
runners.jockey: Al Moatasem bin Said Al Balushi

runners.name: Wanees
runners.owner: A L Butler & The Meydan Ten

runners.name: Miqdaar
runners.jockey: Al Moatasem bin Said Al Balushi

runners.name: Dolayli
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: New Comedy
runners.owner: Ahmed Ebrahim Bin Aamer Al Shemeili

runners.name: Gold Sterling
runners.owner: HH Al Sayyid Tariq Mohammed Abbas Al Said

runners.name: Moonshot
runners.owner: Hussain Ali Mohd Yousef Al Marzooqi

races.race_name: William Hill Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Absolutely Doyen
runners.spotlight: Successful on his first five starts for Paul Nicholls; winning run ended when eighth of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps but he did stay on again after getting outpaced; likely to be competitive.
runners.comment: Won first five starts for this yard; fair eighth of 24 to Supremely West at Cheltenham

runners.name: Jeriko Du Reponet
runners.spotlight: Hasn't looked a natural in his five chases (beaten favourite in Kim Muir last time) but he did very well over hurdles in cheekpieces last spring, finishing second to stablemate Doddiethegreat in the Pertemps at Cheltenham before getting the better of him at Punchestown (2m7f, yielding); could stage a revival back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked a natural over fences but could revive back hurdling (did well last spring)

runners.name: Kaka's Cousin
runners.spotlight: Maiden/novice winner who couldn't justify favouritism but still made a positive start to his handicap career when fourth of 17 in the EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, heavy; Get On George second) five weeks ago, faring best of those who raced close to the pace; up in trip; remains open to improvement.
runners.comment: Positive start to handicap career when fourth in EBF Final at Sandown; likely more to come

runners.name: Kikijo
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who has made a big splash on his switch back to hurdling this season, winning soft-ground handicaps over about 3m at Cheltenham and Sandown; the form of the Sandown win is particularly strong but he couldn't reproduce it back on good ground in the Pertemps at Cheltenham and conditions may once again not be as soft as he prefers.
runners.comment: Progressive on soft this winter but well beaten on quicker ground at Cheltenham since

runners.name: Get On George
runners.spotlight: Pair of novice wins over an extended 2m3f (soft/good); flopped in a 3m Grade 2 novice at Doncaster in January but quickly back on the up with an excellent staying-on second of 17 in the EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, soft; Kaka's Cousin fourth) five weeks ago; well worth another attempt at 3m and has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Largely progressive, including second in EBF Final at Sandown latest; enters calculations

runners.name: Minella Rescue
runners.spotlight: Second in the C&D Grade 1 novice at this meeting last year; went on to finish first and second in handicaps over the extended 2m7f at Uttoxeter at the start of the summer but well below par both starts since a break, latterly at the Cheltenham Festival; combination of first-time cheekpieces and a return to this C&D needs to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Has struggled in both outings since a break but was second in C&D Grade 1 a year ago

runners.name: Hold The Serve
runners.spotlight: Flopped on hurdle debut but hasn't looked back since wind surgery, landing a pair of novices before bringing up the hat-trick on 2m5f Kempton handicap debut (good to soft) seven weeks ago; won that going away in a style which suggests he's capable of better again and is likely to be suited by today's longer trip (3m Irish point winner); major player.
runners.comment: Progressive; won going away on 2m5f handicap debut, so 3m should suit; major player
runners.owner: McNeill Family & The Late Mavis Warren

runners.name: Fortune Timmy
runners.spotlight: Useful novice hurdler, including Fontwell wins over 2m3f and an extended 2m1f (both good to soft); also third in a Grade 2 novice at Cheltenham in January before performing with credit in the Grade 1 Turners at the Festival there (2m5f), staying on again when hampered at the last; that suggests this step up in trip could suit now he's handicapping; interesting.
runners.comment: Staying on when hampered in 2m5f Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham; player on handicap debut

runners.name: Ikarak
runners.spotlight: Regular winner, including at Lingfield (2m7f, good to soft) and Ayr (3m, soft) this season; very good third of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month but Sean Bowen still deserts him in favour of Hold The Serve.
runners.comment: In good form, including third in Pertemps latest, but Sean Bowen still jumps off him
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Mrs D J Brown

runners.name: Good To Be Alive
runners.spotlight: Notched a first win for current yard when asserting late on at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) in January and backed that up when second of eight at Kelso (2m5f, good) six weeks ago; Sean Bowen prefers Hold The Serve but this 5yo brings unexposed potential now he's stepping up in trip under Jack Kennedy.
runners.comment: Sean Bowen looks elsewhere but this 5yo has made steady progress; up in trip

runners.name: Quantum Quest
runners.spotlight: Readily made all in Kilbeggan maiden when upped to 3m1f in August and followed up on handicap debut at Roscommon (3m, good to yielding) in September; even better form in defeat later in the autumn, including fifth of 17 in a notably strong 3m Cheltenham handicap on good; returns from a four-month break as a 6yo who could easily have more to offer.
runners.comment: Steady progress since upped to 3m; may do better again back from a break.

runners.name: Ace Of Spades
runners.spotlight: Successful over 2m4f on reappearance here (good to soft) in October and has shown better form at around 3m since, including a win at Huntingdon (good to soft) in January; only midfield behind reopposing stablemate Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham, though.
runners.comment: Two wins this term, including here, but only midfield behind Supremely West at Cheltenham

runners.name: Supremely West
runners.spotlight: Provided yet another example of his yard's prowess in top handicaps when quite comfortably justifying strong market support in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good) last month, with seven of today's rivals in behind; raised 9lb but could be capable of even better.
runners.comment: Landed gamble in the Pertemps at Cheltenham; raised 9lb but probably even better to come

runners.name: Lihyan
runners.spotlight: Ridden last ten starts by William Maggs; seventh hurdle win when seeing off five rivals at Newbury (3m, good to soft) three weeks ago but even a 2lb nudge seems likely to stop him in this much more competitive scenario.
runners.comment: Won in small field at Newbury latest but this looks much harder

runners.name: Wade Out
runners.spotlight: Switched to chasing with two wins in the autumn, the latter in a Cheltenham Listed race (3m1f, soft) despite not jumping that well; his fencing once again lacked fluency when sixth in the National Hunt Chase back at Cheltenham (3m6f, good) in first-time cheekpieces (blinkered now) last month; has a lower mark back hurdling, so no surprise were he to go well despite the fact Sean Bowen looks elsewhere.
runners.comment: Listed novice chase win despite not impressing with his jumping; lower mark back hurdling

runners.name: Push The Button
runners.spotlight: Made a good start over fences for new connections, winning at Carlisle (2m, good) in October and second in a Grade 2 novice at Ascot (2m3f, soft); mistakes crept in when below that level twice since and he now reverts to hurdling; sixth of 17 in this last year off an identical mark.
runners.comment: Good start over fences but mistakes crept in; now back hurdling (sixth in this last year)
runners.owner: The 8 Amigos, Readers & Wiggy, N Skinner

runners.name: Idem
runners.spotlight: Another in this field who took in the Pertemps at Cheltenham, finishing a good fifth of 24 to Supremely West; should be competitive again but nothing in his record suggests he has enough in hand to win a race as hot as this.
runners.comment: Another who ran well in the Pertemps but looks summed up by his mark

runners.name: Eagle Fang
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner; made a bright start to chasing career when winning a 2m6f beginners' chase at Thurles in November but hasn't built on that in his two runs since and is not obviously well treated now back hurdling in a first-time visor; probably stays 3m.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated back hurdling, so first-time visor needs to make a difference

runners.name: Duke Silver
runners.spotlight: Winner of handicaps at around 3m at Listowel (yielding) in September and Leopardstown (18 runners; good to yielding) in December; bounced back from a lesser effort at the Dublin Racing Festival when sixth of 24 to Supremely West at Cheltenham (3m, good); same mark today.
runners.comment: Won two good handicaps in Ireland this term and sixth to Supremely West in Pertemps latest

runners.name: Bold Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Back with Nicky Henderson after last season with Laura Morgan; had marks in the 140s at his peak and has shown he still retains a good chunk of that ability in his two outings after a year off, latterly finishing seventh of 24 to Supremely West in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good).
runners.comment: Has shown he still retains a good portion of his ability back with Nicky Henderson

runners.name: Harbour Lake
runners.spotlight: Won a good prize at Haydock (3m, good) last May; pulled up on return to action there in November but better when fourth of seven to Ma Shantou at Cheltenham (3m, good) on New Year's Day; being eased another 2lb can only help this C&D scorer but he requires a big career best as a 10yo.
runners.comment: C&D winner who also scored at Haydock last May; fair fourth at Cheltenham latest

runners.name: Chart Topper
runners.spotlight: Third in a Listed handicap over 2m6f at Galway (good) last summer; didn't enjoy much luck on next two starts (still in the lead when falling two out at Haydock in February) but he was well held in a big-field 2m5f handicap at Cheltenham last month; the cheekpieces worn on that occasion are discarded; still not fully exposed, particularly at 3m.
runners.comment: Well held at Cheltenham but promising before that and still not fully exposed
runners.owner: Aisling Byrne & Paul Raleigh & Matthew Cunningham

runners.name: Bossman Jack
runners.spotlight: Three wins from first four starts and he was keeping on well and disputing third when making a bad mistake at the final flight in the Turners at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month, ultimately finishing sixth; leading claims on the back of that very promising performance.
runners.comment: Keeping on well in share of third when making bad mistake at final flight at the Festival
runners.owner: M W Lake & Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Ballyfad
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and beaten just over 7l when tenth of 21 in the Turners at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month; however, he was kicked at the start that day and may well be capable of better, having finished ahead of the Turners winner King Rasko Grey when short-headed in Leopardstown Grade 1 in February; respected for top Irish trainer who has won the last four runnings.
runners.comment: May well be capable of better than he showed at Cheltenham; stable has won last 4 runnings

runners.name: Soldier Reeves
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 2m5f when storming home for fourth at 150-1 in the Turners at Cheltenham (good to soft) last month; he doesn't have the Cheltenham hill this time and his stablemate Bossman Jack would probably have beaten him at the Festival had he jumped the final flight proficiently, but this 5yo is unexposed at this sort of distance and could be a contender.
runners.comment: Flew home for 4th at the Festival; doesn't have the Cheltenham hill but still a contender

runners.name: Scorpio Rising
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-timer (soft/good to soft) and made it three handicap wins in a row when cruising into the lead in the 2m4f EBF Final at Sandown last month; highly progressive and could play a leading role up in grade.
runners.comment: Cruised into the lead when winning the EBF Final at Sandown; could play a leading role

runners.name: Charisma Cat
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner who built upon previous promise over hurdles when sent up in trip for her handicap debut and winning that 15-runner mares' novice handicap at Newbury in good style; has the 7lb mares' allowance but she still needs to find something extra and can make the odd mistake.
runners.comment: Won big-field mares' handicap at Newbury in good style; needs something extra today

runners.name: Lord Byron
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for 80-1 fifth in the Triumph Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, soft) and this step up in trip could prompt further improvement; however, two starts ago this 4yo was soundly beaten by Montemares and Kosac d'Oudairies at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) and he needs to back up his latest promise.
runners.comment: Kept on well for Triumph fifth and this trip could suit; needs to back up that promise

runners.name: Rooster Crowing
runners.spotlight: 5yo who is trained in France and has won four of his 17 hurdle starts there; better than ever when close third of six in Grade 2 at Auteuil (2m3f, very soft) three weeks ago behind two previous Grade 1 winners and he's not ruled out; has won on good to soft ground on the Flat.
runners.comment: His recent Grade 2 third at Auteuil reads well; this French raider is not ruled out

runners.name: Kosac d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Point, bumper and dual novice hurdle winner who was second in a Grade 2 at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) in February; however, he was just over 6l behind Montemares at Kelso, so has work to do today.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Grade 2 at Kelso in February but work to do with Montemares on that form

runners.name: Montemares
runners.spotlight: Lost his unbeaten record when fourth of six, beaten about 8l, in the Grade 1 Challow at Newbury (2m4f, good) in December; returned to winning ways in fine style in the Grade 2 Premier Novices' Hurdle at Kelso (2m2f, good to soft) in February; steps back up in trip and in grade this afternoon but he's one to consider after that impressive latest performance.
runners.comment: Impressive in 2m2f Grade 2 at Kelso in February and he's one to consider

runners.name: Onlyforfrankie
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten sixth of 12 in Grade 2 at Kempton (2m, good to soft) in February and hard to fancy on the back of that; however, he easily won a maiden at Taunton (2m, good) in January and followed up in a warm small-field novice at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) and his pedigree suggests this step up in trip will suit; might not be a forlorn hope each-way.
runners.comment: Beaten 22l in Grade 2 at Kempton but should be better than that; not a forlorn hope e-w

runners.name: Masked Man
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over hurdles, with those novice wins (2m/2m3f, good/good to soft) sandwiching a creditable Grade 2 second at Haydock (1m7f, good to soft) in November; this bright prospect is open to further improvement but others have more substance to their form; first run since December.
runners.comment: Sole defeat over hurdles came when second in Grade 2; others have more substance to form

runners.name: Zanndabad
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner handicap at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) last October and followed up in a warm Newcastle novice (2m6f, good to soft), again with plenty in hand; he's useful on the Flat and could have more to offer in this code now things have started to click, but such improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat and things have clicked over hurdles, but further improvement is needed

races.race_name: William Hill Handicap Chase (Registered As The Freebooter Handicap Chase) (Premier Handicap) (GBB)

runners.name: Mr Hope Street
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner, including in one of only two attempts at 3m; campaigned over 2m4f/2m5f switched to fences this term, best effort when third behind very notable rivals in graduation chase at Haydock; well handicapped on that bare form and could be open to any amount of improvement back up in distance; a typically interesting runner of his type for the Skeltons.
runners.comment: Well treated on best chase effort and looks open to improvement back up in distance

runners.name: Josh The Boss
runners.spotlight: Novice chaser who has several pieces of sound form at up to 2m4f this season, including a second at Aintree; however, below that level in major handicaps in two of his last three starts, seeming a non-stayer at Doncaster (soft) in the sole 3m attempt; stamina to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Seemed non-stayer in a 3m attempt; stamina to prove back up from 2m4f

runners.name: Konfusion
runners.spotlight: Faller in the Cheltenham Festival contest won by Grand National runner Johnnywho; progressive otherwise and his record of 5-11 over fences features wins in notable handicaps at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) and Wetherby (3m, soft) this term; likely to go well barring mishaps.
runners.comment: Faller last time; progressive otherwise and has won two notable handicaps this term

runners.name: Deep Cave
runners.spotlight: 3m hat-trick in 2025 started with a success over hurdles on this card (good) and ended with nabbing Leave Of Absence on the line in notable handicap chase at Ascot (good to soft); couple of backward steps since but a bad early error, and perhaps losing a shoe, contributed to a poor effort last time; may well rebound on a significant day in his calendar.
runners.comment: Possibly best to forgive latest effort; won over hurdles on this card 12 months ago

runners.name: Glengouly
runners.spotlight: Has form at 3m1f; made all in major handicap at Cheltenham (2m4f) in December; unable to dominate in the same manner and eventually pulled up in all three outings since; had wind surgery (his second) since latest; wears new headgear; again far from certain to have his own way.
runners.comment: Made all at Cheltenham in December; unable to dominate in the same way since

runners.name: Bad
runners.spotlight: Useful handicap chaser at up to 2m5f, albeit quirky and having saved his winning in this sphere for Kempton; admittedly right up to best in Ascot contest on penultimate start; last two efforts suggest this new trip is worth exploring but he comes with some risk, especially in such a competitive field.
runners.comment: Looks worth a crack at this new trip but quirky and comes with risk

runners.name: Uncle Bert
runners.spotlight: 1-6 over fences, the success at Aintree (2m4f) on seasonal debut; seemed a non-stayer in the Welsh National before eventually falling, then ran below form at Cheltenham (2m4f); return to this venue may have a positive effect but he ideally needs assistance from the weather, having gained all wins on soft ground.
runners.comment: Ideally needs assistance from the weather, having gained all wins on soft ground

runners.name: Brave Fortune
runners.spotlight: Irish 7yo whose broadly progressive form switched to chasing this season features a comfortable win at Roscommon (sole attempt over 3m1f; good) in the summer and good second in Thurles handicap (2m5f, yielding) last time; still reasonably unexposed and looks interesting back up in distance.
runners.comment: Irish 7yo who is still reasonably unexposed and looks interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Chance Another One
runners.spotlight: Irish 7yo who was successful last August/November in first two chase attempts, including at Kempton (3m, good); something to prove in this scenario, having posted duck eggs in competitive handicaps on last two starts, but in excellent hands and may yet resume improvement; not written off.
runners.comment: Posted duck eggs on last two starts but is in excellent hands and may yet resume progress

runners.name: Leave Of Absence
runners.spotlight: Good third to Lookaway in the bumper on this card four years ago; spoiled his chance with a couple of mistakes in the Cheltenham Festival handicap won by Grand National contender Johnnywho; however, he split that rival and Deep Cave for useful effort at Ascot (3m, good to soft) in December and may resume his progress when jumping errors are avoided.
runners.comment: Made mistakes last time but may resume his progress when jumping errors are avoided

runners.name: Lookaway
runners.spotlight: Landed the bumper on this card in 2022; justified market support in major handicap at Kempton (good to soft; first attempt at 3m under rules) most recently, taking chase record to 3-5 and overall strike-rate to 9-20; remains particularly unexposed over this sort of distance and commands respect returned to Aintree, with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: Won the bumper on this card in 2022 and major handicap chase at Kempton last time

runners.name: In Love
runners.spotlight: French jumper who bagged Grade 3 honours and finished a good fourth in the Grand Steeple-Chase de Paris in 2024; however, has failed to match that form since and, judged on recent RPRs, has something to prove off his mark in this very warm handicap; first British attempt.
runners.comment: French jumper; ran well in top race in 2024; not in the same form since
runners.owner: Andrew Peake, Simon Macauley & Bryant Family

runners.name: Cruz Control
runners.spotlight: Has failed to show his form in both outings this term but a revival looks highly likely back at Aintree on return from four-month break; 2-2 over fences at this venue, namely wins in this race for the last two years (good/good to soft); had a similar prep when winning by 5l off just 1lb lower 12 months ago; commands respect in the hat-trick attempt.
runners.comment: 2-2 over fences at Aintree, namely wins in this race for the last two years; respected

runners.name: Ain't That A Shame
runners.spotlight: Shaped as if in need of the run, en route to taking a late fall, at Kelso six weeks ago on debut for new stable and first start since finishing sixth in the 2024 Grand National; landed a major Irish handicap that year but needs to prove he retains peak ability.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he retains ability in this second run back from long layoff

runners.name: Impose Toi
runners.spotlight: Relished step up in trip when winning C&D handicap (good to soft) in November and won the Grade 2 Long Distance Hurdle at Newbury (3m, soft) and Grade 1 Long Walk at Ascot (3m, good to soft) on his next two starts, with Strong Leader in second on both occasions; below par at Cheltenham on last two runs (good record there previously) and it was disconcerting that he was pulled up in the Stayers', but he's in the picture if back to his best.
runners.comment: Pulled up in Stayers' Hurdle but in the picture on his winning form in the Long Walk

runners.name: Honesty Policy
runners.spotlight: Won the Grade 1 Mersey Novices' Hurdle here 12 months ago (2m4f, good); just two runs in current campaign and they've been positive performances in Grade 1s, finishing third in the Long Walk at Ascot (3m, good to soft) and fifth in the Stayers' Hurdle at Cheltenham (3m, good); this lightly raced 6yo could have more to offer back here in a first-time tongue-tie; chance.
runners.comment: Grade 1 novice win here 12 months ago; remains lightly raced and could have more to offer

runners.name: Strong Leader
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Cheltenham in January but that's not his track and he's had a wind op since; prior to that he ran well when runner-up to Impose Toi at Newbury and Ascot, and his Aintree form figures read 1212 with his last two visits being in this race (good to soft/good); there can be considerable optimism that he'll be a key player back here; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Wind surgery since last time and he has an excellent record at Aintree; key player

runners.name: Air Of Entitlement
runners.spotlight: Won the mares' novice hurdle at last season's Cheltenham Festival (2m1f, good to soft) and returned to form to win the Martin Pipe Handicap there (2m4f, soft) last month; she has something to find now back up in grade, even with the 7lb mares' allowance, but she powered up the hill last time and has an Irish National winner in her pedigree, so the step up in trip might prompt further improvement; not discounted.
runners.comment: Powered up the hill to win the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; this new trip might suit

runners.name: Take No Chances
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of her game in the Mares' Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) last month but this sort of trip may be her optimum; neck second to Strong Leader at Wetherby (3m, good) in November and creditable third behind Impose Toi and Strong Leader in another Grade 2 at Newbury (3m, soft) later that month; ran well here 12 months ago; each-way claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Good form in defeat in 3m Grade 2s in November; each-way claims if back on song

runners.name: Hiddenvalley Lake
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Punchestown last May and at Navan in February on sole start since; however, this race has brought out the best in him in the last two seasons, as he finished third in 2024 (good to soft) and first last year (good); could bounce back and play another leading role.
runners.comment: Return to form is needed but he was third and first in the last two runnings of this race

runners.name: Home By The Lee
