runners.spotlight: Improved his rating in every one of his eight chases, winning four and that includes the 2m4f Plate at last year's Cheltenham Festival; first venture much beyond 2m5f had to wait until his return to Cheltenham last month, in which he came from out the back to get within half a length of the winner Johnnywho; that showed he stays 3m1f and suggested he's still capable of better, but his jumping contained unnerving moments; wears cheekpieces first time and a half-brother probably stays 4m.
runners.comment: Constant improver; went close when upped to 3m1f at Cheltenham after some jumping errors

runners.name: Panic Attack
runners.spotlight: 10yo who is better than ever this term, including with a big handicap double in November in the Paddy Power Gold Cup at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) and Coral Gold Cup at Newbury (3m2f, good to soft); dropping back to 2m4f, however, was probably not ideal when creditable third in mares' race at Cheltenham last month; 8lb higher than in the Newbury race but her dominance that day is hard to forget.
runners.comment: 8lb higher than for her big-race win at Newbury (3m2f) but her dominance is hard to forget

runners.name: Johnnywho
runners.spotlight: Fine over these fences in November; laboured from before three out when a well-beaten fifth to Haiti Couleurs in Irish National last April; that was in the mud, though, and his top efforts have been on good to soft; they include his neck second in the 3m2f Kim Muir at Cheltenham last March and, equipped with first-time cheekpieces after wind surgery, a 3m1f win from Jagwar at this year's Festival; able to race here off the same mark as last time (due to go up 6lb) and he wasn't stopping in either of those big races at Cheltenham; seriously considered.
runners.comment: 2nd and 1st over 3m2f/3m1f at Cheltenham Festivals and wasn't stopping; up 6lb in future

runners.name: Stellar Story
runners.spotlight: Second to Lecky Watson in the 3m Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham last March; while comfortably dismissed in the closing stages of his three starts this winter, the first two of those were Grade 1 events and giving 8lb to Grangeclare West and Gerri Colombe at Fairyhouse (blinkers replaced cheekpieces) on latest outing makes that result look a whole lot better; above all, perhaps, while unraced beyond 3m2f, he has strongly suggested on plenty of occasions that he has serious stamina reserves; each-way appeal.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 3m2f but has strongly suggested that he has serious stamina; e-w appeal

runners.name: Jordans
runners.spotlight: Twice second in Grade 1 novice events last season, most notably when upped substantially in trip to 3m1f at this meeting; possibilities judged on that performance (unraced over further) and although he's been nowhere near that form since, his closest finish this term came when a staying-on fifth of 18 over 2m7f as a hurdler; just a 7yo but not totally dismissed.
runners.comment: Second in the 3m1f Grade 1 novice chase at this meeting last year; below form since

runners.name: Firefox
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied on last two starts; Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown (3m, soft) in February was also the first time he went beyond 2m5f, when 33-1 and spending most of the race out the back but running on into fourth of 12, beaten 14l; some encouragement might be derived from that but he didn't see it out so well when confronted by 3m2f in the Gold Cup at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Ran on late when fourth in Irish Gold Cup (3m); didn't see out 3m2f so well at Cheltenham

runners.name: Favori De Champdou
runners.spotlight: Experienced 11yo who's having a tremendous season; 66-1 winner of a top 3m handicap at Leopardstown (made most) over Christmas and took his form to another new level on the 3m5f Cheltenham cross-country course on last two outings, winning easily on first occasion and well clear of the third when he chased home Final Orders at last month's Festival; well handicapped and with the trip likely to suit, he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: 11yo who's having a tremendous season, over Cheltenham cross-country on last two outings

runners.name: Final Orders
runners.spotlight: 10yo who's had 70 races (only one of today's rivals has managed even half that) and won 11; latest was the 3m5f cross-country at Cheltenham Festival, comfortably holding off Favori De Champdou for career-best form off the same mark as today; having taken so well to that discipline (he also won there in December) and produced his best performances away from soft ground, he has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Has taken so well to Cheltenham cross-country; best performances away from soft ground

runners.name: Perceval Legallois
runners.spotlight: Won major 3m handicaps at Leopardstown over fences (by 7l, going away) and hurdles before starting joint-third favourite for last year's National, but he fell on the first circuit; it's too early to forget his best performance (Christmas 2024) but with nothing to shout about in his two races this winter, this time he could be an outsider.
runners.comment: Joint-third fav for this in 2025 but fell at the ninth; nothing to shout about this winter

runners.name: Three Card Brag
runners.spotlight: Career-best form in the autumn this season, including a win at Cheltenham (3m1f) and second to Panic Attack in the Coral Gold Cup at Newbury (3m2f); flopped when favourite in the Grade 3 won by Grangeclare West at Fairyhouse in February, admittedly after 12 weeks off but there's the additional worry of last year's Grand National in which he was 16-1 and raced very prominently but faded steadily from three out.
runners.comment: Career-best form in the autumn; last year's Grand National and latest start are concerns

runners.name: Oscars Brother
runners.spotlight: A first-season novice won the 2022 Grand National when unraced beyond 3m1f and this Irish challenger (who's one year older) bids to repeat the feat; hat-trick this season included two small-field Grade 2s and when his winning sequence came to an end it was in the Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham Festival, where some slow jumping in the early stages left him with plenty to do but he made late inroads to finish fourth; he still looks capable of better and the longer trip could suit.
runners.comment: Slow early jumping in 3m1f Grade 1 novice at Cheltenham but he made late inroads into 4th

runners.name: Gorgeous Tom
runners.spotlight: Fourth behind two of these in 3m Grade 1 novice event at Cheltenham last March; ran creditably all three starts this winter, notably 11l fourth of 24 off this mark to Panic Attack at Newbury (3m2f) in November when he stuck to his task very well having been left at the shambolic start; improvement is needed but that race gave encouragement for longer trips and his dam is half-sister to Cappa Bleu who was placed in this race in 2012 and 2013.
runners.comment: 11l fourth off this mark to Panic Attack at Newbury (3m2f) and stuck to his task very well

runners.name: Gerri Colombe
runners.spotlight: Five Grade 1 wins and runner-up in the 2024 Cheltenham Gold Cup; reappearance at Christmas came after 421 days off and this season's form is well short of his best, but second to Grangeclare West at Fairyhouse was a big step back in the right direction and he comfortably obliged at odds-on for latest outing; cheekpieces last three starts; well handicapped on his old form and could be on his way back.
runners.comment: 2nd in 2024 Cheltenham Gold Cup and, some positive signs lately, could be on his way back

runners.name: High Class Hero
runners.spotlight: One positive is his staying-on third over an extended 3m4f in the bet365 Gold Cup at Sandown (good; 5lb higher today) last April; another is that he's trained by Willie Mullins, who described him in November as likely to be "an ideal type for the Grand National"; after a solid reappearance, he's been pulled up in good handicaps on his last two outings but both were in the mud and he got loose before the latest, which presumably didn't help.
runners.comment: Staying-on third over an extended 3m4f at Sandown (good; 5lb higher today) last April
runners.owner: Sullivan Group Limited & P Crowley & Neill Hughes

runners.name: Iroko
runners.spotlight: Staying-on fourth in last season's Grand National (good to soft) off 5lb lower as just a 7yo; a second crack at it has been his main target this term but he looked better than ever when winning from Firefox in a hot, three-runner race at Ascot (2m5f; second run after wind surgery) in December and had an excuse (scoped dirty) at the Cheltenham Festival last month; has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Staying-on fourth in 2025 National off 5lb lower; scoped dirty at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Imperial Saint
runners.spotlight: Novice campaign last season went well, notably over the conventional fences here where he registered three handicap wins (2m/2m4f) and was second at this meeting (3m1f); resumed progress with a win at Haydock (3m1f) this January but badly hampered at the Cheltenham Festival; this trip is a big step into the unknown but he needs a second look.
runners.comment: 3 wins over conventional fences here; 3m1f win this January but this trip is a big unknown

runners.name: Haiti Couleurs
runners.spotlight: In the last 13 months he's won Cheltenham's NH Chase (3m6f), the Irish National (3m5f), a hurdle race, Welsh National (near 3m7f; 12lb higher today) and a Newbury Grade 2; no way can he be ruled out, particularly as he finished well on top in those four chases, but there have also been two major blips this season; latest was in the Cheltenham Gold Cup, for which he was just 6-1 and made most but went to nothing turning for home (writing on the wall four out); however, he's bounced back before, the likely tempo of this race can suit him better and the trip looks like doing so as well.
runners.comment: Irish and Welsh National winner; Gold Cup was disappointing but he's bounced back before

runners.name: Mr Vango
runners.spotlight: Usually goes well in the mud; last season's hat-trick culminated in the 4m2f Midlands National and he rallied to go very close against Twig in the 3m2f Becher over these fences on first outing this term; while there was so much to admire to that point, he's cracked with alarming speed in 3m and 4m1f races on his last two outings.
runners.comment: Nothing to prove for stamina or this track, but he cracked alarmingly on last two outings

runners.name: Twig
runners.spotlight: Second at the 2024 Cheltenham Festival and started this season with two wins, notably by a short head from Mr Vango in the 3m2f Becher over these fences (wore cheekpieces second time); warmed up for this over hurdles after three months off; the Becher performance was doughty stuff but his only go at a marathon trip was last year's National in which he finished a remote tenth, always behind.
runners.comment: A doughty 3m2f win over these fences in the Becher but remote 10th in last year's National

runners.name: Amirite
runners.spotlight: A fluent fourth in the 2m5f Topham over these fences was the first of three big runs in good handicaps last April-July; ran creditably in one (Sandown in April 2024) of his three attempts at 3m4f-3m5f and excuses might be made for his latest two outings (first cross-country, then the mud) but other candidates are more persuasive.
runners.comment: A fluent fourth in the Topham over these fences last April; ran creditably once at 3m4f

runners.name: Captain Cody
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown seventh to Favori De Champdou at Christmas was a glimpse of what he's capable of; the performance which really gets the juices flowing, however, came in the Scottish National at Ayr (4m, good to soft) last April when very confidently ridden and unleashed to find a tremendous response on the run-in, cutting down the leader; it's very hard to rule him out after that, despite the 12lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Tremendous response on the run-in to win 4m Scottish National last April; 12lb higher now
runners.owner: Vincent Caldwell & Angela Shamoon & Mrs A Shamoon Ibgi

runners.name: Banbridge
runners.spotlight: So nearly won his second King George at Kempton over Christmas but, although he's a class act, excelling over 3m on good ground at that speed track doesn't mean that this test will suit him; his moderate late headway in the extended 2m4f Grade 1 Ryanair at Cheltenham last month isn't that persuasive either.
runners.comment: So nearly won his second King George at Kempton, which doesn't mean this test will suit

runners.name: Top Of The Bill
runners.spotlight: One of several who are now in the veteran stage but also in the form of their lives; 7l winner at Haydock (extended 3m1f) in November and returned there with an excellent, rallying second in the Grand National Trial (extended 3m4f, again good to soft) in February; ridden last three starts by Toby McCain-Mitchell; back up 2lb and an interesting prospect for 4m2f but this is a much stronger race.
runners.comment: An excellent, rallying second at Haydock (extended 3m4f) but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Monty's Star
runners.spotlight: Just 1-11 as a chaser, largely because he keeps trying in vain at Grade 1 level; fourth in last year's Cheltenham Gold Cup was one of his peak efforts, as was this season's never-nearer fifth in a Leopardstown Grade 1 at Christmas; he didn't fare so well in the Irish Gold Cup last time but was the only one matching strides with the top-class winner on the home turn; going up markedly in trip today, in a handicap, makes him a mouthwatering proposition.
runners.comment: Trying in vain at Grade 1 level; going up markedly in trip in a handicap is mouthwatering

runners.name: I Am Maximus
runners.spotlight: His only three long-distance events resulted in an Irish National win in 2023, a storming success in this race in 2024 (soft) and a valiant second, from further off the pace, in repeat bid here in 2025 (good to soft); what a record that is, and 50-1 second in a 3m Grade 1 at Leopardstown on penultimate start gives every encouragement that all his ability remains; the return of cheekpieces (tried once in 2022) is interesting but, off 1lb higher than last year, he should be set for another prominent finish.
runners.comment: 1st and 2nd in last two Grand Nationals; penultimate race suggests all his ability remains

runners.name: Champ Kiely
runners.spotlight: The furthest he's been is 3m1f in Grade 1 novice at Punchestown Festival (good to yielding) last April which he won in good style, showing improved form; went close over 2m in November and although he slipped up next time and met with heavy defeats twice since, he went well enough to the home turn in Irish Gold Cup on latest; whether he'll stay 4m2f is a big question but he could outrun his odds for a long way.
runners.comment: Whether he'll stay 4m2f is a big question but he could outrun his odds for a long way

runners.name: Spanish Harlem
runners.spotlight: Career with Willie Mullins took a long time to catch fire but it's done so this season and he looked set for victory in the Thyestes at Gowran Park (3m1f, heavy) in January when unseating at the final fence; pulled up behind Grangeclare West last time but back catalogue includes a creditable sixth in the Scottish National and fourth in bet365 Cup at Sandown, so he stays long distances; his revised mark since the Thyestes would be the chief worry.
runners.comment: Stays long distances; his revised mark since bold show in the Thyestes is the chief worry

runners.name: Quai De Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Third in the 3m5f Irish National last April, despite an indifferent round of jumping, and third of 16 with cheekpieces at Leopardstown on latest outing was his best form, despite just the 2m5f; kept jumping out to his right in the latter and although a case can be made for him, it would have to gloss over that he's completed only four of his eight chases.
runners.comment: Third in the 3m5f Irish National last April but completed only four of his eight chases

runners.name: Marble Sands
runners.spotlight: Negotiated these fences safely in the 2m5f Topham one year ago, albeit fading into 12th; a strong-staying display to win at Cheltenham (near 3m4f, soft; 5lb higher today) in November hints at untapped potential over long distances.
runners.comment: A strong-staying winner at Cheltenham (near 3m4f, soft; 5lb higher today) in November
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Beauport
runners.spotlight: Took well to these fences in last year's renewal but subsided rapidly after three out, which might have been surprising for one who'd won a 4m2f Midlands National (March 2024) but he seemed to race a bit too zestfully in the front rank; 3lb lower today, having run respectably on reappearance but moderately in two hurdle races since (wind surgery before latest); now tongue tied first time; a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Stays but he was too zestful in this race last year; now tongue tied first time

runners.name: Answer To Kayf
runners.spotlight: Faded into sixth at Punchestown (3m7f) last May on his only attempt at a marathon trip; worth another go, having rallied so well to win a major 3m handicap at Navan in November, but it's a little hard to ignore that clearly his best five ratings have been registered on soft or heavy going, as have all his four wins.
runners.comment: Faded in a 3m7f race; clearly his best five ratings were registered on soft or heavy going

runners.name: The Real Whacker
runners.spotlight: 11lb below his peak mark and well handicapped judged on best efforts, the most recent of which were over 3m on good ground late in 2024; muted in this three runs this term, only one of which was over fences but he ended up tailed off in that Wetherby race, which he'd won a year earlier; those past glories may now be too distant.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on peak efforts but those past glories may now be too distant

runners.name: Grangeclare West
runners.spotlight: Excellent 3l third in last year's renewal, somehow renewing his effort after diving at the final fence cost him plenty of momentum; creditable fifth at Sandown just three weeks later and surging clear from Gerri Colombe at Fairyhouse this February, after another last-fence dive, showed that he's at least as good as ever; 3lb higher than 12 months ago but lots to like.
runners.comment: 3l third last year, after diving at the final fence (up 3lb today); won his latest outing

runners.name: Lecky Watson
runners.spotlight: Unraced beyond 3m2f; came into his own as a novice last term, notably in the 3m Grade 1 at Cheltenham Festival which he won by 4l from Stellar Story; no impact at the top level in Ireland this season and he clambered over the final fence on his way to a heavy defeat behind several of today's rivals in a Grade 3 last time, so current prospects don't look so bright.
runners.comment: Won 3m Grade 1 at Cheltenham last March from Stellar Story; no impact in Ireland this term

races.race_name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (Grade 2) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bass Hunter
runners.spotlight: Assembling an impressive CV in bumpers, following all-the-way wins at Newbury (good to soft) and Ascot (soft) with an excellent third at the Cheltenham Festival (good) where he stuck to his task gamely after being headed over 1f out; that's the best form on offer and he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Followed wins at Newbury and Ascot with excellent third at Cheltenham; the one to beat

runners.name: Look Me
runners.spotlight: Has a compelling profile, having gone close on debut at the 2025 Irish Grand National meeting (soft to heavy) before making a winning start for Willie Mullins back at Fairyhouse (heavy) in February; has to prove his effectiveness on less testing ground, but he looks a smart prospect and this owner/trainer combination were successful in this event 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Smart prospect; followed close second with a cosy win, both at Fairyhouse; major chance

runners.name: One Knight
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since joining Emma Lavelle; easily saw off eight rivals at Hereford (good ground) last month after a long layoff; well up the pecking order on ratings and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: 2-2 since joining Emma Lavelle; well up the pecking order on ratings; should have a say

runners.name: Risky Obsession
runners.spotlight: Might have been unsuited by heavy ground when tailed off in Listed race at Newbury (100-30) in February, and he'd previously made an excellent impression when winning an Irish point (good to yielding) and a bumper at Warwick (good to soft); surely still has untapped potential and jockey bookings suggest he's the pick of Dan Skelton's three.
runners.comment: Well beaten on heavy last time but previous wins were full of promise; interesting

runners.name: Smile John Boy
runners.spotlight: Made all at Hereford (good) in November and has run well in defeat twice since, albeit finishing behind Risky Obsession at Warwick and Merlin Allen at Kempton; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Two good runs in defeat since his Hereford win but others have stronger claims

runners.name: Nightflyer
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for a different yard at Catterick (soft) in January, but his limitations seem to have been exposed since; only fourth at Warwick 16 days ago and he has much more on his plate here.
runners.comment: Only fourth at Warwick 16 days ago and has much more on his plate here

runners.name: Forthfactor
runners.spotlight: Ridden by Heidi Palin on both outings for current yard, winning at Hexham (good to soft) before finishing a creditable fifth to Bass Hunter at Ascot (soft) when he faded late on; may not have reached his ceiling just yet; one of three contenders for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: Hexham winner before fifth to Bass Hunter at Ascot; may not have reached ceiling just yet

runners.name: Ronnie Russet
runners.spotlight: Significant improvement with each of his three runs, winning at Newcastle in December and January (both good to soft); not hard pressed to pull 11l clear in a big field on latest; solid candidate with each-way claims.
runners.comment: Significant improvement with each run, winning twice at Newcastle; each-way claims

runners.name: Chicker
runners.spotlight: Successful in his first two races (on good ground) but has come unstuck in a higher grade since, well beaten behind Bass Hunter at the Cheltenham Festival last month; difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind Bass Hunter at the Cheltenham Festival; difficult to recommend

runners.name: Rices Pool
runners.spotlight: His second in an Irish point has substance and he justified favouritism in bumper at Taunton (good to soft) with more in hand than the relatively short winning margin suggests; Ben Pauling runs four and stable jockey Ben Jones rides this one; improvement required but certainly possible.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Taunton with something in hand; improvement required but possible

runners.name: The Perfect Poet
runners.spotlight: A fourth runner for the Ben Pauling yard, and like two of the others he's 1-1 under rules; well-backed favourite when comfortably accounting for four rivals at Fontwell (soft) four weeks ago; could be anything but this is a much stiffer task.
runners.comment: Favourite when making winning debut at Fontwell; could be anything but this is much harder

runners.name: Cooltobecareless
runners.spotlight: Favourite in both his bumpers, taking second at Chepstow (good) before going one better at Fakenham (soft); remains open to improvement but has plenty to find on the figures and Harry Skelton rides stablemate Risky Obsession.
runners.comment: Workmanlike winner at Fakenham; remains unexposed but has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Merry Away
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for £72,000 after finishing third in an Irish point in November, and he dug deep when making all in bumper at Warwick (good to soft) five weeks ago on debut for current yard; Ben Jones jumps ship to ride Rices Pool, but this one is unexposed and cannot be discounted.
runners.comment: Dug deep when making all at Warwick on rules debut; unexposed and cannot be discounted

runners.name: Moments Away
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement with each of his three runs, beaten off only late on when second at Chepstow (soft) on debut for this yard last month; may have more in the tank but he's still a maiden and this is his sternest assignment to date; fitted with a tongue-strap for the first time.
runners.comment: Steady improvement but he's 0-3 and this is his sternest assignment to date; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Merlin Allen
runners.spotlight: Beat his 11 opponents handsomely in junior event over C&D in November and ran well under a penalty against older rivals at Kempton (again good to soft) seven weeks ago; in good hands and remains open to progress.
runners.comment: C&D winner of junior event before running well against older rivals at Kempton; respected

runners.name: Governors Rock
runners.spotlight: Progressive in three outings in Ireland, winning under a penalty at Ballinrobe (soft) and subsequently changing hands for £100,000; however, he ran no sort of race in a Listed event at Newbury (heavy; 12-1) on his debut for current yard and this contest is even more competitive.
runners.comment: Two wins in Ireland but ran badly on British debut and this is even more competitive
runners.owner: Gummerson, Martin, W T Walsh & Williams

runners.name: Thelastmanfrompenr
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the stable's smart 1m7f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Notlongtillmay; off to the perfect start at Kelso (heavy; 7-1) in February, leading inside the final 1f and beating eight opponents; open to improvement but he's in much deeper here.
runners.comment: Off to the perfect start at Kelso in February; open to improvement but in much deeper here

runners.name: Barachiel
runners.spotlight: A little more improvement when runner-up at Taunton (good) last month, on first outing after wind surgery; now fitted with a hood and a tongue-strap but he looks out of his depth in this grade.
runners.comment: Some improvement when runner-up at Taunton last month but looks out of his depth

runners.name: Le Roi Remi
runners.spotlight: Overcame greenness to win at Sandown (good to soft) in November with plenty in hand, but that race has not worked out well and he could finish only midfield at Kempton next time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Wide-margin Sandown win has not worked out well; only midfield at Kempton next time

runners.name: Makingplansfor
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Newton Abbot and Lingfield but put firmly in his place in Listed race at Cheltenham in between; needs a great leap forward.
runners.comment: Put firmly in place in Listed race at Cheltenham in November; needs a great leap forward
